text.NRSV: The proverbs of Solomon son of David, king of Israel:
text.Rotherham: The proverbs of Solomon, son of David, King of Israel:
text.KJV: 1:1 The Prouerbes of Solomon the sonne of Dauid, King of Israel,
text.NABRE: 1:1 I. Title and Introduction Chapter 1 Purpose of the Proverbs of Solomon - The proverbs of Solomon, the son of David, king of Israel:

text.NRSV: For learning about wisdom and instruction, for understanding words of insight,
text.Rotherham: For the knowledge of wisdom and correction, for discerning the sayings of intelligence;
text.KJV: 1:2 To knowe wisedome and instruction, to perceiue the words of vnderstanding,
text.NABRE: 1:2 That people may know wisdom and discipline, may understand intelligent sayings;

text.NRSV: for gaining instruction in wise dealing, righteousness, justice, and equity;
text.Rotherham: For receiving the correction of prudence, righteousness and justice, and equity;
text.KJV: 1:3 To receiue the instruction of wisdome, iustice, and iudgement & equitie,
text.NABRE: 1:3 May receive instruction in wise conduct, in what is right, just and fair;

text.NRSV: to teach shrewdness to the simple, knowledge and prudence to the young—
text.Rotherham: For giving To the simple shrewdness, To the young man, knowledge and discretion.
text.KJV: 1:4 To giue subtiltie to the simple, to the yong man knowledge and discretion.
text.NABRE: 1:4 That resourcefulness may be imparted to the naive, knowledge and discretion to the young.

text.NRSV: let the wise also hear and gain in learning, and the discerning acquire skill,
text.Rotherham: A wise man, will hear, and will increase learning, and a, discreet man, wise counsels, will acquire,
text.KJV: 1:5 A wise man wil heare, and wil increase learning: and a man of vnderstanding shall attaine vnto wise counsels:
text.NABRE: 1:5 The wise by hearing them will advance in learning, the intelligent will gain sound guidance,

text.NRSV: to understand a proverb and a figure, the words of the wise and their riddles.
text.Rotherham: By gaining discernment of proverb, and satire, the words of the wise, and their dark sayings.
text.KJV: 1:6 To vnderstand a prouerbe, and the interpretation; the wordes of the wise, and their darke sayings.
text.NABRE: 1:6 To comprehend proverb and byword, the words of the wise and their riddles.

text.NRSV: The fear of the Lord is the beginning of knowledge; fools despise wisdom and instruction.
text.Rotherham: The reverence of Yahweh, is the beginning of knowledge, Wisdom and correction, the foolish have despised.
text.KJV: 1:7 ¶ The feare of the Lord is the beginning of knowledge: but fooles despise wisedome and instruction.
text.NABRE: 1:7 Fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge; fools despise wisdom and discipline.

text.NRSV: Hear, my child, your father’s instruction, and do not reject your mother’s teaching;
text.Rotherham: Hear, my son, the correction of thy father, and do not reject the instruction of thy mother;
text.KJV: 1:8 My sonne, heare the instruction of thy father, and forsake not the law of thy mother.
text.NABRE: 1:8 II. Instructions of Parents and of Woman Wisdom The Path of the Wicked: Greed and Violence - Hear, my son, your father’s instruction, and reject not your mother’s teaching;

text.NRSV: for they are a fair garland for your head, and pendants for your neck.
text.Rotherham: For, a wreath of beauty, shall they be to thy head, and chains of ornament, to thy neck.
text.KJV: 1:9 For they shall be an ornament of grace vnto thy head, and chaines about thy necke.
text.NABRE: 1:9 A graceful diadem will they be for your head; a pendant for your neck.

text.NRSV: My child, if sinners entice you, do not consent.
text.Rotherham: My son, if sinners entice thee, do not consent.
text.KJV: 1:10 ¶ My sonne, if sinners entise thee, consent thou not.
text.NABRE: 1:10 My son, should sinners entice you,

text.NRSV: If they say, “Come with us, let us lie in wait for blood; let us wantonly ambush the innocent;
text.Rotherham: If they say—Come with us,—Let us lie in wait for blood, Let us watch in secret for him who is needlessly innocent;
text.KJV: 1:11 If they say, Come with vs, let vs lay wait for blood, let vs lurke priuily for the innocent without cause:
text.NABRE: 1:11 do not go if they say, “Come along with us! Let us lie in wait for blood, unprovoked, let us trap the innocent;

text.NRSV: like Sheol let us swallow them alive and whole, like those who go down to the Pit.
text.Rotherham: Let us engulf them, like hades, alive, While in health, like them who are going down to the pit;
text.KJV: 1:12 Let vs swallow them vp aliue, as the graue, and whole, as those that goe downe into the pit:
text.NABRE: 1:12 Let us swallow them alive, like Sheol, whole, like those who go down to the pit!

text.NRSV: We shall find all kinds of costly things; we shall fill our houses with booty.
text.Rotherham: All costly substance, shall we find, We shall fill our houses with spoil;
text.KJV: 1:13 Wee shall finde all precious substance, wee shall fill our houses with spoile:
text.NABRE: 1:13 All kinds of precious wealth shall we gain, we shall fill our houses with booty;

text.NRSV: Throw in your lot among us; we will all have one purse”—
text.Rotherham: Thy lot, shalt thou cast into our midst, One purse, shall there be, for us all.
text.KJV: 1:14 Cast in thy lot among vs, let vs all haue one purse:
text.NABRE: 1:14 Cast in your lot with us, we shall all have one purse!”

text.NRSV: my child, do not walk in their way, keep your foot from their paths;
text.Rotherham: My son, do not walk in the way with them, Withhold thy foot from their path.
text.KJV: 1:15 My sonne, walke not thou in the way with them; refraine thy foot from their path:
text.NABRE: 1:15 My son, do not walk in the way with them, hold back your foot from their path!

text.NRSV: for their feet run to evil, and they hurry to shed blood.
text.Rotherham: For, their feet, to mischief, do run, and haste to the shedding of blood.
text.KJV: 1:16 For their feete runne to euil, and make haste to shed blood.
text.NABRE: 1:16 [For their feet run to evil, they hasten to shed blood.]

text.NRSV: For in vain is the net baited while any bird looks on.
text.Rotherham: Surely, in vain, is spread the net, in the sight of aught that hath wings!
text.KJV: 1:17 Surely in vaine the net is spread in the sight of any bird.
text.NABRE: 1:17 In vain a net is spread right under the eyes of any bird—

text.NRSV: But they lie in wait—for their own blood; they set an ambush—for their own lives.
text.Rotherham: Yet, they, for their own blood, lie in wait, They watch in secret for their own life.
text.KJV: 1:18 And they lay wait for their owne blood, they lurke priuily for their owne liues.
text.NABRE: 1:18 They lie in wait for their own blood, they set a trap for their own lives.

text.NRSV: Such is the end of all who are greedy for gain; it takes away the life of its possessors.
text.Rotherham: Such are the ways of every one that graspeth with greed, The life of the owners thereof, it taketh away!
text.KJV: 1:19 So are the waies of euery one that is greedie of gaine: which taketh away the life of the owners thereof.
text.NABRE: 1:19 This is the way of everyone greedy for loot: it takes away their lives.

text.NRSV: Wisdom cries out in the street; in the squares she raises her voice.
text.Rotherham: Wisdom, in the open place, soundeth forth, in the broadways, she raiseth her voice;
text.KJV: 1:20 ¶ Wisedome crieth without, she vttereth her voice in the streets:
text.NABRE: 1:20 Wisdom in Person Gives a Warning - Wisdom cries aloud in the street, in the open squares she raises her voice;

text.NRSV: At the busiest corner she cries out; at the entrance of the city gates she speaks:
text.Rotherham: At the head of bustling streets, she crieth aloud,—at the openings of the gates in the city—her sayings, she doth utter:—
text.KJV: 1:21 Shee crieth in the chiefe place of concourse, in the openings of the gates: in the city she vttereth her words, saying,
text.NABRE: 1:21 Down the crowded ways she calls out, at the city gates she utters her words:

text.NRSV: “How long, O simple ones, will you love being simple? How long will scoffers delight in their scoffing and fools hate knowledge?
text.Rotherham: How long, ye simple ones, will ye love simplicity? and, scoffers, in scoffing, delight them? and, dullards, hate knowledge?
text.KJV: 1:22 How long, ye simple ones, will ye loue simplicitie? and the scorners delight in their scorning, and fooles hate knowledge?
text.NABRE: 1:22 “How long, you naive ones, will you love naivete,

text.NRSV: Give heed to my reproof; I will pour out my thoughts to you; I will make my words known to you.
text.Rotherham: Will ye turn, at my reproof? Lo! I will pour out to you my spirit, I will make known my words to you.
text.KJV: 1:23 Turne you at my reproofe: behold, I will powre out my spirit vnto you, I will make knowen my wordes vnto you.
text.NABRE: 1:23 How long will you turn away at my reproof? [The arrogant delight in their arrogance, and fools hate knowledge.] Lo! I will pour out to you my spirit, I will acquaint you with my words:

text.NRSV: Because I have called and you refused, have stretched out my hand and no one heeded,
text.Rotherham: Because I called, and ye refused, I stretched out my hand, and no one regarded;
text.KJV: 1:24 ¶ Because I haue called, and yee refused, I haue stretched out my hand, and no man regarded:
text.NABRE: 1:24 ‘Because I called and you refused, extended my hand and no one took notice;

text.NRSV: and because you have ignored all my counsel and would have none of my reproof,
text.Rotherham: But ye dismissed all my counsel, and, to my rebuke, would not yield,
text.KJV: 1:25 But ye haue set at nought all my counsell, & would none of my reproofe:
text.NABRE: 1:25 Because you disdained all my counsel, and my reproof you ignored—

text.NRSV: I also will laugh at your calamity; I will mock when panic strikes you,
text.Rotherham: I also, at your calamity, will laugh, I will mock, when cometh your dread;
text.KJV: 1:26 I also will laugh at your calamitie, I wil mocke when your feare commeth.
text.NABRE: 1:26 I, in my turn, will laugh at your doom; will mock when terror overtakes you;

text.NRSV: when panic strikes you like a storm, and your calamity comes like a whirlwind, when distress and anguish come upon you.
text.Rotherham: When cometh as a crashing your dread, and, your calamity, as a storm-wind, overtaketh, when there come upon you, distress and anguish.
text.KJV: 1:27 When your feare commeth as desolation, and your destruction commeth as a whirlewinde; when distresse and anguish commeth vpon you:
text.NABRE: 1:27 When terror comes upon you like a storm, and your doom approaches like a whirlwind; when distress and anguish befall you.’

text.NRSV: Then they will call upon me, but I will not answer; they will seek me diligently, but will not find me.
text.Rotherham: Then, will they call me, but I will not answer, they will seek me diligently, but shall not find me.
text.KJV: 1:28 Then shall they call vpon mee, but I will not answere; they shall seeke me early, but they shall not finde me:
text.NABRE: 1:28 Then they will call me, but I will not answer; they will seek me, but will not find me,

text.NRSV: Because they hated knowledge and did not choose the fear of the Lord,
text.Rotherham: Because they hated knowledge, and, the reverence of Yahweh, did not choose;
text.KJV: 1:29 For that they hated knowledge, and did not choose the feare of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 1:29 Because they hated knowledge, and the fear of the LORD they did not choose.

text.NRSV: would have none of my counsel, and despised all my reproof,
text.Rotherham: Consented not to my counsel, disdained all my rebuke,
text.KJV: 1:30 They would none of my counsel: they despised all my reproofe.
text.NABRE: 1:30 They ignored my counsel, they spurned all my reproof;

text.NRSV: therefore they shall eat the fruit of their way and be sated with their own devices.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall they eat of the fruit of their own way, and, with their own counsels, be filled.
text.KJV: 1:31 Therefore shall they eate of the fruite of their owne way, and be filled with their owne deuices.
text.NABRE: 1:31 Well, then, they shall eat the fruit of their own way, and with their own devices be glutted.

text.NRSV: For waywardness kills the simple, and the complacency of fools destroys them;
text.Rotherham: For, the turning away of the simple, will slay them,—and, the carelessness of dullards, will destroy them.
text.KJV: 1:32 For the turning away of the simple shall slay them, and the prosperity of fooles shall destroy them.
text.NABRE: 1:32 For the straying of the naive kills them, the smugness of fools destroys them.

text.NRSV: but those who listen to me will live secure and will be at ease, without dread of disaster.”
text.Rotherham: Whereas, he that hearkeneth unto me, shall dwell safely, and be at rest, without dread of misfortune.
text.KJV: 1:33 But who so hearkneth vnto mee, shall dwell safely, and shall be quiet from feare of euill.
text.NABRE: 1:33 But whoever obeys me dwells in security, in peace, without fear of harm.”

text.NRSV: My child, if you accept my words and treasure up my commandments with you,
text.Rotherham: My son, if thou wilt receive my sayings, and, my commandments, wilt treasure up by thee;
text.KJV: 2:1 My sonne, if thou wilt receiue my words, and hide my commaundements with thee;
text.NABRE: 2:1 Chapter 2 The Blessings of Wisdom - My son, if you receive my words and treasure my commands,

text.NRSV: making your ear attentive to wisdom and inclining your heart to understanding;
text.Rotherham: So that thou direct, unto wisdom, thine ear, bend thy heart, unto understanding;
text.KJV: 2:2 So that thou incline thine eare vnto wisedome, and apply thine heart to vnderstanding:
text.NABRE: 2:2 Turning your ear to wisdom, inclining your heart to understanding;

text.NRSV: if you indeed cry out for insight, and raise your voice for understanding;
text.Rotherham: Yea if, for understanding, thou cry aloud, for knowledge, utter thy voice;
text.KJV: 2:3 Yea if thou cryest after knowledge, and liftest vp thy voyce for vnderstanding:
text.NABRE: 2:3 Yes, if you call for intelligence, and to understanding raise your voice;

text.NRSV: if you seek it like silver, and search for it as for hidden treasures;
text.Rotherham: If thou seek her as silver, and, like hid treasure, thou search for her,
text.KJV: 2:4 If thou seekest her as siluer, and searchest for her, as for hid treasures:
text.NABRE: 2:4 If you seek her like silver, and like hidden treasures search her out,

text.NRSV: then you will understand the fear of the Lord and find the knowledge of God.
text.Rotherham: Then, shalt thou understand the reverence of Yahweh, and, the knowledge of God, shalt thou find.
text.KJV: 2:5 Then shalt thou vnderstand the feare of the Lord, and find the knowledge of God.
text.NABRE: 2:5 Then will you understand the fear of the LORD; the knowledge of God you will find;

text.NRSV: For the Lord gives wisdom; from his mouth come knowledge and understanding;
text.Rotherham: For, Yahweh, giveth wisdom, out of his mouth, knowledge and understanding;
text.KJV: 2:6 For the Lord giueth wisedome: out of his mouth commeth knowledge, and vnderstanding.
text.NABRE: 2:6 For the LORD gives wisdom, from his mouth come knowledge and understanding;

text.NRSV: he stores up sound wisdom for the upright; he is a shield to those who walk blamelessly,
text.Rotherham: Yea he treasureth, for the upright, safety, A shield [is he] to them who walk in integrity,
text.KJV: 2:7 He layeth vp sound wisedome for the righteous: he is a buckler to them that walke vprightly.
text.NABRE: 2:7 He has success in store for the upright, is the shield of those who walk honestly,

text.NRSV: guarding the paths of justice and preserving the way of his faithful ones.
text.Rotherham: To him that observeth the paths of justice, yea, the way of his men of lovingkindness, he doth guard.
text.KJV: 2:8 He keepeth the pathes of iudgement, and preserueth the way of his Saints.
text.NABRE: 2:8 Guarding the paths of justice, protecting the way of his faithful ones,

text.NRSV: Then you will understand righteousness and justice and equity, every good path;
text.Rotherham: Then, shalt thou understand righteousness and justice, and equity—every noble course.
text.KJV: 2:9 Then shalt thou vnderstand righteousnesse, and iudgement, and equity; yea euery good path.
text.NABRE: 2:9 Then you will understand what is right and just, what is fair, every good path;

text.NRSV: for wisdom will come into your heart, and knowledge will be pleasant to your soul;
text.Rotherham: When wisdom entereth thy heart, and, knowledge, to thy soul, is sweet,
text.KJV: 2:10 ¶ When wisedome entreth into thine heart, and knowledge is pleasant vnto thy soule;
text.NABRE: 2:10 For wisdom will enter your heart, knowledge will be at home in your soul,

text.NRSV: prudence will watch over you; and understanding will guard you.
text.Rotherham: Discretion, shall watch over thee, understanding, shall preserve thee:—
text.KJV: 2:11 Discretion shall preserue thee, vnderstanding shall keepe thee:
text.NABRE: 2:11 Discretion will watch over you, understanding will guard you;

text.NRSV: It will free you from the way of evil, from those who utter perverse things,
text.Rotherham: To rescue thee from the way of the wrongful, from the man that speaketh perverse things;
text.KJV: 2:12 To deliuer thee from the way of the euill man, from the man that speaketh froward things.
text.NABRE: 2:12 Saving you from the way of the wicked, from those whose speech is perverse.

text.NRSV: who forsake the paths of uprightness to walk in the ways of darkness,
text.Rotherham: From them who forsake the paths of rectitude, to walk in the ways of darkness;
text.KJV: 2:13 Who leaue the pathes of vprightnesse, to walke in the wayes of darkenesse:
text.NABRE: 2:13 From those who have left the straight paths to walk in the ways of darkness,

text.NRSV: who rejoice in doing evil and delight in the perverseness of evil;
text.Rotherham: Who rejoice to do wrong, exult in the perversities of the wrongful;
text.KJV: 2:14 Who reioyce to doe euill, and delight in the frowardnesse of the wicked.
text.NABRE: 2:14 Who delight in doing evil and celebrate perversity;

text.NRSV: those whose paths are crooked, and who are devious in their ways.
text.Rotherham: Whose paths, are twisted, and they are tortuous in their tracks:
text.KJV: 2:15 Whose wayes are crooked, and they froward in their pathes.
text.NABRE: 2:15 Whose ways are crooked, whose paths are devious;

text.NRSV: You will be saved from the loose woman, from the adulteress with her smooth words,
text.Rotherham: To rescue thee, from the woman that is a stranger, from the female unknown, who with her speeches seduceth;
text.KJV: 2:16 To deliuer thee from the strange woman, euen from the stranger, which flattereth with her words:
text.NABRE: 2:16 Saving you from a stranger, from a foreign woman with her smooth words,

text.NRSV: who forsakes the partner of her youth and forgets her sacred covenant;
text.Rotherham: Who forsaketh the friend of her youth, and, the covenant of her God, hath forgotten;
text.KJV: 2:17 Which forsaketh the guide of her youth, and forgetteth the couenant of her God.
text.NABRE: 2:17 One who forsakes the companion of her youth and forgets the covenant of her God;

text.NRSV: for her way leads down to death, and her paths to the shades;
text.Rotherham: For she hath appointed, unto death, her house, and unto the shades, her courses;
text.KJV: 2:18 For her house inclineth vnto death, and her pathes vnto the dead:
text.NABRE: 2:18 For her path sinks down to death, and her footsteps lead to the shades.

text.NRSV: those who go to her never come back, nor do they regain the paths of life.
text.Rotherham: None who go in unto her, come back, neither attain they unto the paths of life:
text.KJV: 2:19 None that goe vnto her, returne againe, neither take they hold of the pathes of life.
text.NABRE: 2:19 None who enter there come back, or gain the paths of life.

text.NRSV: Therefore walk in the way of the good, and keep to the paths of the just.
text.Rotherham: To the end that thou walk in the way of good men, and, the paths of the righteous, that thou observe.
text.KJV: 2:20 That thou mayest walke in the way of good men, and keepe the pathes of the righteous.
text.NABRE: 2:20 Thus you may walk in the way of the good, and keep to the paths of the just.

text.NRSV: For the upright will abide in the land, and the innocent will remain in it;
text.Rotherham: For, the upright, shall abide on the earth,—and, the men of integrity, shall remain therein;
text.KJV: 2:21 For the vpright shall dwell in the land, and the perfect shall remaine in it.
text.NABRE: 2:21 For the upright will dwell in the land, people of integrity will remain in it;

text.NRSV: but the wicked will be cut off from the land, and the treacherous will be rooted out of it.
text.Rotherham: But, the lawless, out of the earth, shall be cut off, and, traitors, shall they tear away therefrom.
text.KJV: 2:22 But the wicked shall be cut off from the earth, and the transgressours shalbe rooted out of it.
text.NABRE: 2:22 But the wicked will be cut off from the land, the faithless will be rooted out of it.

text.NRSV: My child, do not forget my teaching, but let your heart keep my commandments;
text.Rotherham: My son, mine instruction, do not thou forget, and, my commandment, let thy heart observe;
text.KJV: 3:1 My sonne, forget not my lawe; but let thine heart keepe my commaundements:
text.NABRE: 3:1 Chapter 3 Confidence in God Leads to Prosperity - My son, do not forget my teaching, take to heart my commands;

text.NRSV: for length of days and years of life and abundant welfare they will give you.
text.Rotherham: For, length of days and years of life, and blessedness, shall they add to thee.
text.KJV: 3:2 For length of dayes, and long life, and peace shall they adde to thee.
text.NABRE: 3:2 For many days, and years of life, and peace, will they bring you.

text.NRSV: Do not let loyalty and faithfulness forsake you; bind them around your neck, write them on the tablet of your heart.
text.Rotherham: Lovingkindness and faithfulness, let them not forsake thee,—bind them, upon thy neck, write them upon the tablet of thy heart:
text.KJV: 3:3 Let not mercy and trueth forsake thee: bind them about thy necke, write them vpon the table of thine heart.
text.NABRE: 3:3 Do not let love and fidelity forsake you; bind them around your neck; write them on the tablet of your heart.

text.NRSV: So you will find favor and good repute in the sight of God and of people.
text.Rotherham: So find thou favour and good repute, in the eyes of God and man.
text.KJV: 3:4 So shalt thou find fauour, and good vnderstanding in the sight of God, and man.
text.NABRE: 3:4 Then will you win favor and esteem before God and human beings.

text.NRSV: Trust in the Lord with all your heart, and do not rely on your own insight.
text.Rotherham: Trust thou in Yahweh, with all thy heart, and, unto thine own understanding, do not lean:
text.KJV: 3:5 ¶ Trust in the Lord with all thine heart; and leaue not vnto thine owne vnderstanding.
text.NABRE: 3:5 Trust in the LORD with all your heart, on your own intelligence do not rely;

text.NRSV: In all your ways acknowledge him, and he will make straight your paths.
text.Rotherham: In all thy ways, acknowledge him, and, he, will make straight thy paths.
text.KJV: 3:6 In all thy wayes acknowledge him, and he shall direct thy pathes.
text.NABRE: 3:6 In all your ways be mindful of him, and he will make straight your paths.

text.NRSV: Do not be wise in your own eyes; fear the Lord, and turn away from evil.
text.Rotherham: Do not become wise in thine own eyes, revere Yahweh, and avoid evil:
text.KJV: 3:7 ¶ Be not wise in thine owne eyes: feare the Lord, and depart from euill.
text.NABRE: 3:7 Do not be wise in your own eyes, fear the LORD and turn away from evil;

text.NRSV: It will be a healing for your body and a refreshment for your bones.
text.Rotherham: Healing, shall it be to thy body, and refreshing, to thy bones.
text.KJV: 3:8 It shalbe health to thy nauill, and marrow to thy bones.
text.NABRE: 3:8 This will mean health for your flesh and vigor for your bones.

text.NRSV: Honor the Lord with your substance and with the first fruits of all your produce;
text.Rotherham: Honour thou Yahweh, with thy substance, and with the firstfruit of all thine increase;
text.KJV: 3:9 Honour the Lord with thy substance, and with the first fruits of all thine increase.
text.NABRE: 3:9 Honor the LORD with your wealth, with first fruits of all your produce;

text.NRSV: then your barns will be filled with plenty, and your vats will be bursting with wine.
text.Rotherham: So shall thy storehouses be filled with plenty, and, with new wine, shall thy vats overflow.
text.KJV: 3:10 So shall thy barnes be filled with plenty, and thy presses shall burst out with new wine.
text.NABRE: 3:10 Then will your barns be filled with plenty, with new wine your vats will overflow.

text.NRSV: My child, do not despise the Lord’s discipline or be weary of his reproof,
text.Rotherham: The, chastening of Yahweh, my son, do not reject, nor loathe his rebuke;
text.KJV: 3:11 ¶ My sonne, despise not the chastening of the Lord: neither be weary of his correction.
text.NABRE: 3:11 The discipline of the LORD, my son, do not spurn; do not disdain his reproof;

text.NRSV: for the Lord reproves the one he loves, as a father the son in whom he delights.
text.Rotherham: For, whom Yahweh loveth, he correcteth, he causeth pain to the son in whom he delighteth.
text.KJV: 3:12 For whom the Lord loueth, he correcteth, euen as a father the sonne, in whom he delighteth.
text.NABRE: 3:12 For whom the LORD loves he reproves, as a father, the son he favors.

text.NRSV: Happy are those who find wisdom, and those who get understanding,
text.Rotherham: How happy the man who hath found wisdom, and the man who draweth forth understanding,
text.KJV: 3:13 ¶ Happy is the man that findeth wisedome, and the man that getteth vnderstanding.
text.NABRE: 3:13 The Benefits of Finding Wisdom - Happy the one who finds wisdom, the one who gains understanding!

text.NRSV: for her income is better than silver, and her revenue better than gold.
text.Rotherham: For better is her merchandise, than the merchandise of silver, yea, than gold, her increase;
text.KJV: 3:14 For the merchandise of it is better then the merchandise of siluer, and the gaine thereof, then fine gold.
text.NABRE: 3:14 Her profit is better than profit in silver, and better than gold is her revenue;

text.NRSV: She is more precious than jewels, and nothing you desire can compare with her.
text.Rotherham: More precious, is she, than corals, yea, none of thy delightful things, doth equal her:
text.KJV: 3:15 She is more precious then Rubies: and all the things thou canst desire, are not to be compared vnto her.
text.NABRE: 3:15 She is more precious than corals, and no treasure of yours can compare with her.

text.NRSV: Long life is in her right hand; in her left hand are riches and honor.
text.Rotherham: Length of days, is in her right hand, in her left, are riches and honour;
text.KJV: 3:16 Length of dayes is in her right hand: and in her left hand, riches and honour.
text.NABRE: 3:16 Long life is in her right hand, in her left are riches and honor;

text.NRSV: Her ways are ways of pleasantness, and all her paths are peace.
text.Rotherham: Her ways, are ways of pleasantness, and, all her paths, are peace;
text.KJV: 3:17 Her wayes are wayes of plesantnesse: and all her pathes are peace.
text.NABRE: 3:17 Her ways are pleasant ways, and all her paths are peace;

text.NRSV: She is a tree of life to those who lay hold of her; those who hold her fast are called happy.
text.Rotherham: A tree of life, is she, to them who secure her,—and, they who hold her fast, are every one to be pronounced happy.
text.KJV: 3:18 She is a tree of life, to them that lay hold vpon her: and happy is euery one that retaineth her.
text.NABRE: 3:18 She is a tree of life to those who grasp her, and those who hold her fast are happy.

text.NRSV: The Lord by wisdom founded the earth; by understanding he established the heavens;
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, in wisdom, founded the earth, establishing the heavens with understanding;
text.KJV: 3:19 The Lord by wisedome hath founded the earth; by vnderstanding hath he established the heauens.
text.NABRE: 3:19 The LORD by wisdom founded the earth, established the heavens by understanding;

text.NRSV: by his knowledge the deeps broke open, and the clouds drop down the dew.
text.Rotherham: By his knowledge, the resounding deeps were burst open, and, the skies, drop down dew.
text.KJV: 3:20 By his knowledge the depthes are broken vp; and the cloudes droppe downe the dew.
text.NABRE: 3:20 By his knowledge the depths are split, and the clouds drop down dew.

text.NRSV: My child, do not let these escape from your sight: keep sound wisdom and prudence,
text.Rotherham: My son, let them not depart from thine eyes, guard thou counsel, and purpose:
text.KJV: 3:21 ¶ My sonne, let not them depart from thine eyes: keepe sound wisedome and discretion.
text.NABRE: 3:21 Justice Toward One’s Neighbor Brings Blessing - My son, do not let these slip from your sight: hold to deliberation and planning;

text.NRSV: and they will be life for your soul and adornment for your neck.
text.Rotherham: So shall they become life to thy soul, and an adorning to thy neck;
text.KJV: 3:22 So shall they bee life vnto thy soule, and grace to thy necke.
text.NABRE: 3:22 So will they be life to your soul, and an adornment for your neck.

text.NRSV: Then you will walk on your way securely and your foot will not stumble.
text.Rotherham: Then, shalt thou walk securely in thy way, and, thy foot, shall not stumble;
text.KJV: 3:23 Then shalt thou walke in thy way safely, & thy foot shall not stumble.
text.NABRE: 3:23 Then you may go your way securely; your foot will never stumble;

text.NRSV: If you sit down, you will not be afraid; when you lie down, your sleep will be sweet.
text.Rotherham: When thou sittest down, thou shalt have no dread, yea thou shalt lie down, and sweet shall be thy sleep.
text.KJV: 3:24 When thou lyest downe, thou shalt not be afraide: yea, thou shalt lye downe, and thy sleepe shalbe sweet.
text.NABRE: 3:24 When you lie down, you will not be afraid, when you rest, your sleep will be sweet.

text.NRSV: Do not be afraid of sudden panic, or of the storm that strikes the wicked;
text.Rotherham: Be not thou afraid of sudden dread, nor of the desolation of the lawless, when it cometh.
text.KJV: 3:25 Be not afraid of sudden feare, neither of the desolation of the wicked, when it commeth.
text.NABRE: 3:25 Do not be afraid of sudden terror, of the ruin of the wicked when it comes;

text.NRSV: for the Lord will be your confidence and will keep your foot from being caught.
text.Rotherham: For, Yahweh, will be in all thy ways, and will keep thy foot from the snare.
text.KJV: 3:26 For the Lord shalbe thy confidence, and shall keepe thy foote from being taken.
text.NABRE: 3:26 For the LORD will be your confidence, and will keep your foot from the snare.

text.NRSV: Do not withhold good from those to whom it is due, when it is in your power to do it.
text.Rotherham: Withhold not good from them who ask it, when it is in power of thy hand to do it:
text.KJV: 3:27 ¶ Withhold not good from them to whom it is due, when it is in the power of thine hand to doe it.
text.NABRE: 3:27 Do not withhold any goods from the owner when it is in your power to act.

text.NRSV: Do not say to your neighbor, “Go, and come again, tomorrow I will give it”—when you have it with you.
text.Rotherham: Do not say to thy neighbour, Go and come again, and to-morrow I will give, when thou hast it by thee.
text.KJV: 3:28 Say not vnto thy neighbour, Goe, and come againe, and to morrow I will giue, when thou hast it by thee.
text.NABRE: 3:28 Say not to your neighbor, “Go, come back tomorrow, and I will give it to you,” when all the while you have it.

text.NRSV: Do not plan harm against your neighbor who lives trustingly beside you.
text.Rotherham: Devise not against thy neighbour an injury, seeing that, he, dwelleth securely by thee.
text.KJV: 3:29 Deuise not euil against thy neighbour, seeing hee dwelleth securely by thee.
text.NABRE: 3:29 Do not plot evil against your neighbors, when they live at peace with you.

text.NRSV: Do not quarrel with anyone without cause, when no harm has been done to you.
text.Rotherham: Contend not with a man without cause, if he hath dealt thee no wrong.
text.KJV: 3:30 ¶ Striue not with a man without cause, if hee haue done thee no harme.
text.NABRE: 3:30 Do not contend with someone without cause, with one who has done you no harm.

text.NRSV: Do not envy the violent and do not choose any of their ways;
text.Rotherham: Do not thou envy the man of violence, neither choose thou any of his ways;
text.KJV: 3:31 ¶ Enuie thou not the oppressour, and choose none of his wayes.
text.NABRE: 3:31 Do not envy the violent and choose none of their ways:

text.NRSV: for the perverse are an abomination to the Lord, but the upright are in his confidence.
text.Rotherham: For, an abomination to Yahweh, is the tortuous man, but, with the upright, he is intimate.
text.KJV: 3:32 For the froward is abomination to the Lord: but his secret is with the righteous.
text.NABRE: 3:32 To the LORD the devious are an abomination, but the upright are close to him.

text.NRSV: The Lord’s curse is on the house of the wicked, but he blesses the abode of the righteous.
text.Rotherham: The curse of Yahweh, is in the house of the lawless one, but, the home of the righteous, he blesseth.
text.KJV: 3:33 ¶ The curse of the Lord is in the house of the wicked: but he blesseth the habitation of the iust.
text.NABRE: 3:33 The curse of the LORD is on the house of the wicked, but the dwelling of the just he blesses;

text.NRSV: Toward the scorners he is scornful, but to the humble he shows favor.
text.Rotherham: Though at scoffers he scoffeth, yet, to the humbled, he granteth favour.
text.KJV: 3:34 Surely he scorneth the scorners: but he giueth grace vnto the lowly.
text.NABRE: 3:34 Those who scoff, he scoffs at, but the lowly he favors.

text.NRSV: The wise will inherit honor, but stubborn fools, disgrace.
text.Rotherham: Honour, shall the wise inherit,—but, as for dullards, shame shall carry them away.
text.KJV: 3:35 The wise shall inherite glory, but shame shalbe the promotion of fooles.
text.NABRE: 3:35 The wise will possess glory, but fools will bear shame.

text.NRSV: Listen, children, to a father’s instruction, and be attentive, that you may gain insight;
text.Rotherham: Hear, ye sons, the correction of a father, and attend, that ye may know understanding.
text.KJV: 4:1 Heare, ye children, the instruction of a father, and attend to know vnderstanding.
text.NABRE: 4:1 Chapter 4 The Teacher as Model Disciple - Hear, O children, a father’s instruction, be attentive, that you may gain understanding!

text.NRSV: for I give you good precepts: do not forsake my teaching.
text.Rotherham: For, good teaching, have I given you, mine instruction, do not ye forsake.
text.KJV: 4:2 For I giue you good doctrine: forsake you not my law.
text.NABRE: 4:2 Yes, excellent advice I give you; my teaching do not forsake.

text.NRSV: When I was a son with my father, tender, and my mother’s only child,
text.Rotherham: For, a son, became I to my father, tender and most precious in the sight of my mother.
text.KJV: 4:3 For I was my fathers sonne, tender and onely beloued in the sight of my mother.
text.NABRE: 4:3 When I was my father’s child, tender, the darling of my mother,

text.NRSV: he taught me, and said to me, “Let your heart hold fast my words; keep my commandments, and live.
text.Rotherham: So he taught me, and said to me—Let thy heart, lay hold of my words, Keep my commandments and live!
text.KJV: 4:4 He taught me also, and said vnto me, Let thine heart reteine my wordes: keepe my commandements, and liue.
text.NABRE: 4:4 He taught me and said to me: “Let your heart hold fast my words: keep my commands, and live!

text.NRSV: Get wisdom; get insight: do not forget, nor turn away from the words of my mouth.
text.Rotherham: Acquire wisdom, acquire understanding, Do not forget, neither decline thou from the sayings of my mouth.
text.KJV: 4:5 Get wisedome, get vnderstanding: forget it not, neither decline from the wordes of my mouth.
text.NABRE: 4:5 Get wisdom, get understanding! Do not forget or turn aside from the words of my mouth.

text.NRSV: Do not forsake her, and she will keep you; love her, and she will guard you.
text.Rotherham: Do not forsake her, and she will guard thee,—love her and she will keep thee.
text.KJV: 4:6 Forsake her not, and she shall preserue thee: loue her, and she shall keepe thee.
text.NABRE: 4:6 Do not forsake her, and she will preserve you; love her, and she will safeguard you;

text.NRSV: The beginning of wisdom is this: Get wisdom, and whatever else you get, get insight.
text.Rotherham: The principal thing, is wisdom, acquire thou wisdom, With all thine acquisition, acquire thou understanding.
text.KJV: 4:7 Wisedome is the principall thing, therefore get wisedome: and with all thy getting, get vnderstanding.
text.NABRE: 4:7 The beginning of wisdom is: get wisdom; whatever else you get, get understanding.

text.NRSV: Prize her highly, and she will exalt you; she will honor you if you embrace her.
text.Rotherham: Exalt her, and she will set thee on high, she will bring thee to honour, when thou dost embrace her:
text.KJV: 4:8 Exalt her, and shee shall promote thee: shee shall bring thee to honour, when thou doest imbrace her.
text.NABRE: 4:8 Extol her, and she will exalt you; she will bring you honors if you embrace her;

text.NRSV: She will place on your head a fair garland; she will bestow on you a beautiful crown.”
text.Rotherham: She will give for thy head, a wreath of beauty, A crown of adorning, will she bestow upon thee.
text.KJV: 4:9 She shall giue to thine head an ornament of grace, a crowne of glory shall she deliuer to thee.
text.NABRE: 4:9 She will put on your head a graceful diadem; a glorious crown will she bestow on you.”

text.NRSV: Hear, my child, and accept my words, that the years of your life may be many.
text.Rotherham: Hear, my son, and receive my sayings, and they will multiply to thee the years of life.
text.KJV: 4:10 Heare, O my sonne, and receiue my sayings: and the yeeres of thy life shalbe many.
text.NABRE: 4:10 The Two Ways - Hear, my son, and receive my words, and the years of your life shall be many.

text.NRSV: I have taught you the way of wisdom; I have led you in the paths of uprightness.
text.Rotherham: In the way of wisdom, have I taught thee, I have guided thee in tracks of uprightness.
text.KJV: 4:11 I haue taught thee in the way of wisedome: I haue lead thee in right pathes.
text.NABRE: 4:11 On the way of wisdom I direct you, I lead you on straight paths.

text.NRSV: When you walk, your step will not be hampered; and if you run, you will not stumble.
text.Rotherham: When thou walkest, thy step shall not be hemmed in, and, if thou runnest, thou shalt not stumble.
text.KJV: 4:12 When thou goest, thy steps shall not be straitned, and when thou runnest, thou shalt not stumble.
text.NABRE: 4:12 When you walk, your step will not be impeded, and should you run, you will not stumble.

text.NRSV: Keep hold of instruction; do not let go; guard her, for she is your life.
text.Rotherham: Take fast hold of correction, let her not go,—keep her, for, she, is thy life.
text.KJV: 4:13 Take fast hold of instruction, let her not goe; keepe her, for she is thy life.
text.NABRE: 4:13 Hold fast to instruction, never let it go; keep it, for it is your life.

text.NRSV: Do not enter the path of the wicked, and do not walk in the way of evildoers.
text.Rotherham: Upon the path of the lawless, do not thou enter, and do not advance in the way of the wicked:
text.KJV: 4:14 ¶ Enter not into the path of the wicked, and goe not in the way of euill men.
text.NABRE: 4:14 The path of the wicked do not enter, nor walk in the way of the evil;

text.NRSV: Avoid it; do not go on it; turn away from it and pass on.
text.Rotherham: Avoid it, do not pass thereon—turn from it, and depart.
text.KJV: 4:15 Auoid it, passe not by it, turne from it, and passe away.
text.NABRE: 4:15 Shun it, do not cross it, turn aside from it, pass on.

text.NRSV: For they cannot sleep unless they have done wrong; they are robbed of sleep unless they have made someone stumble.
text.Rotherham: For they sleep not, unless they can do mischief,—They rob themselves of their sleep, if they cannot cause someone to stumble,
text.KJV: 4:16 For they sleepe not except they haue done mischiefe: and their sleepe is taken away vnlesse they cause some to fall.
text.NABRE: 4:16 For they cannot rest unless they have done evil; if they do not trip anyone they lose sleep.

text.NRSV: For they eat the bread of wickedness and drink the wine of violence.
text.Rotherham: For they consume bread gotten by lawlessness, and, wine obtained by violence, they drink.
text.KJV: 4:17 For they eate the bread of wickednesse, and drinke the wine of violence.
text.NABRE: 4:17 For they eat the bread of wickedness and drink the wine of violence.

text.NRSV: But the path of the righteous is like the light of dawn, which shines brighter and brighter until full day.
text.Rotherham: But, the path of the righteous, is as the light of dawn,—going on and brightening, unto meridian day.
text.KJV: 4:18 But the path of the iust is as the shining light that shineth more and more vnto the perfect day.
text.NABRE: 4:18 But the path of the just is like shining light, that grows in brilliance till perfect day.

text.NRSV: The way of the wicked is like deep darkness; they do not know what they stumble over.
text.Rotherham: The way of the lawless, is like darkness, they know not, at what they stumble.
text.KJV: 4:19 The way of the wicked is as darknes: they know not at what they stumble.
text.NABRE: 4:19 The way of the wicked is like darkness; they do not know on what they stumble.

text.NRSV: My child, be attentive to my words; incline your ear to my sayings.
text.Rotherham: My son, to my words, attend, to my sayings, incline thou thine ear;
text.KJV: 4:20 ¶ My sonne, attend to my words, incline thine eare vnto my sayings.
text.NABRE: 4:20 With Your Whole Being Heed My Words and Live - My son, to my words be attentive, to my sayings incline your ear;

text.NRSV: Do not let them escape from your sight; keep them within your heart.
text.Rotherham: Let them not depart from thine eyes, keep them in the midst of thy heart;
text.KJV: 4:21 Let them not depart from thine eyes: keepe them in the midst of thine heart.
text.NABRE: 4:21 Let them not slip from your sight, keep them within your heart;

text.NRSV: For they are life to those who find them, and healing to all their flesh.
text.Rotherham: For, life, they are, to them who find them,—and, to every part of one’s flesh, they bring healing.
text.KJV: 4:22 For they are life vnto those that find them, and health to all their flesh.
text.NABRE: 4:22 For they are life to those who find them, bringing health to one’s whole being.

text.NRSV: Keep your heart with all vigilance, for from it flow the springs of life.
text.Rotherham: Above all that must be guarded, keep thou thy heart, for, out of it, are the issues of life.
text.KJV: 4:23 ¶ Keepe thy heart with all diligence: for out of it are the issues of life.
text.NABRE: 4:23 With all vigilance guard your heart, for in it are the sources of life.

text.NRSV: Put away from you crooked speech, and put devious talk far from you.
text.Rotherham: Remove from thee, perverseness of mouth, and, craftiness of lips, put far from thee.
text.KJV: 4:24 Put away from thee a froward mouth, and peruerse lips put farre from thee.
text.NABRE: 4:24 Dishonest mouth put away from you, deceitful lips put far from you.

text.NRSV: Let your eyes look directly forward, and your gaze be straight before you.
text.Rotherham: Let, thine eyes, right onward, look,—and, thine eyelashes, point straight before thee.
text.KJV: 4:25 Let thine eyes looke right on, and let thine eye lids looke straight before thee.
text.NABRE: 4:25 Let your eyes look straight ahead and your gaze be focused forward.

text.NRSV: Keep straight the path of your feet, then all your ways will be sure.
text.Rotherham: Make level the track of thy foot, that, all thy ways, may be directed aright:
text.KJV: 4:26 Ponder the path of thy feet, and let all thy wayes be established.
text.NABRE: 4:26 Survey the path for your feet, and all your ways will be sure.

text.NRSV: Do not swerve to the right or to the left; turn your foot away from evil.
text.Rotherham: Decline not, to the right hand or to the left,—Turn away thy foot from wickedness.
text.KJV: 4:27 Turne not to the right hande nor to the left: remoue thy foot frō euil.
text.NABRE: 4:27 Turn neither to right nor to left, keep your foot far from evil.

text.NRSV: My child, be attentive to my wisdom; incline your ear to my understanding,
text.Rotherham: My son, to my wisdom, attend, and, to mine understanding, incline thou thine ear:
text.KJV: 5:1 My sonne, attend vnto my wisedome, and bowe thine eare to my vnderstanding.
text.NABRE: 5:1 Chapter 5 Warning Against Adultery - My son, to my wisdom be attentive, to understanding incline your ear,

text.NRSV: so that you may hold on to prudence, and your lips may guard knowledge.
text.Rotherham: That thou mayest preserve discretion,—and, as for knowledge, that thy lips may guard it.
text.KJV: 5:2 That thou mayest regard discretion, and that thy lips may keepe knowledge.
text.NABRE: 5:2 That you may act discreetly, and your lips guard what you know.

text.NRSV: For the lips of a loose woman drip honey, and her speech is smoother than oil;
text.Rotherham: For, with sweet droppings, drip the lips of her that is a stranger, and, smoother than oil, is her mouth;
text.KJV: 5:3 ¶ For the lips of a strange woman drop as an hony combe, and her mouth is smoother then oyle.
text.NABRE: 5:3 Indeed, the lips of the stranger drip honey, and her mouth is smoother than oil;

text.NRSV: but in the end she is bitter as wormwood, sharp as a two-edged sword.
text.Rotherham: But, the latter end of her, is bitter as wormwood, sharp, as a two-edged sword!
text.KJV: 5:4 But her end is bitter as wormewood, sharpe as a two edged sword.
text.NABRE: 5:4 But in the end she is as bitter as wormwood, as sharp as a two-edged sword.

text.NRSV: Her feet go down to death; her steps follow the path to Sheol;
text.Rotherham: Her feet, are going down to death,—on hades, will her steps take firm hold.
text.KJV: 5:5 Her feete goe downe to death: her steps take hold on hell.
text.NABRE: 5:5 Her feet go down to death, her steps reach Sheol;

text.NRSV: she does not keep to the smooth path of life; her steps wander, and she does not know it.
text.Rotherham: Lest, the path of life, she should ponder, her tracks have wandered she knoweth not [whither] .
text.KJV: 5:6 Lest thou shouldest ponder the path of life, her wayes are moueable, that thou canst not know them.
text.NABRE: 5:6 Her paths ramble, you know not where, lest you see before you the road to life.

text.NRSV: And now, my children, listen to me, and do not depart from the words of my mouth.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, ye sons, hearken unto me, and do not turn away from the sayings of my mouth.
text.KJV: 5:7 Heare me now therefore, O yee children: & depart not from the words of my mouth.
text.NABRE: 5:7 So now, children, listen to me, do not stray from the words of my mouth.

text.NRSV: Keep your way far from her, and do not go near the door of her house;
text.Rotherham: Keep far from her thy way, and do not go near the opening of her house:
text.KJV: 5:8 Remoue thy way farre from her, and come not nie the doore of her house:
text.NABRE: 5:8 Keep your way far from her, do not go near the door of her house,

text.NRSV: or you will give your honor to others, and your years to the merciless,
text.Rotherham: Lest thou give, to other men, thy vigour, and thy years, to him that is cruel:
text.KJV: 5:9 Lest thou giue thine honour vnto others, and thy yeeres vnto the cruell:
text.NABRE: 5:9 Lest you give your honor to others, and your years to a merciless one;

text.NRSV: and strangers will take their fill of your wealth, and your labors will go to the house of an alien;
text.Rotherham: Lest strange men, be well fed, by thy strength, and, thy toils, be in the house of the alien.
text.KJV: 5:10 Lest strangers be filled with thy wealth, and thy labors be in the house of a stranger,
text.NABRE: 5:10 Lest outsiders take their fill of your wealth, and your hard-won earnings go to another’s house;

text.NRSV: and at the end of your life you will groan, when your flesh and body are consumed,
text.Rotherham: So shalt thou grieve in thy latter end, in the failing of thy flesh and of thy healthy condition;
text.KJV: 5:11 And thou mourne at the last, when thy flesh and thy body are consumed,
text.NABRE: 5:11 And you groan in the end, when your flesh and your body are consumed;

text.NRSV: and you say, “Oh, how I hated discipline, and my heart despised reproof!
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt say—How I hated correction! and, reproof, my heart disdained;
text.KJV: 5:12 And say, How haue I hated instruction, and my heart despised reproofe?
text.NABRE: 5:12 And you say, “Oh, why did I hate instruction, and my heart spurn reproof!

text.NRSV: I did not listen to the voice of my teachers or incline my ear to my instructors.
text.Rotherham: Neither hearkened I to the voice of my teachers, nor, to my instructors, inclined I mine ear:
text.KJV: 5:13 And haue not obeyed the voyce of my teachers, nor inclined mine eare to them that instructed me?
text.NABRE: 5:13 Why did I not listen to the voice of my teachers, incline my ear to my instructors!

text.NRSV: Now I am at the point of utter ruin in the public assembly.”
text.Rotherham: Soon was I in all evil, in the midst of convocation and assembly.
text.KJV: 5:14 I was almost in all euill, in the midst of the congregation & assembly.
text.NABRE: 5:14 I am all but ruined, in the midst of the public assembly!”

text.NRSV: Drink water from your own cistern, flowing water from your own well.
text.Rotherham: Drink thou water out of thine own cistern, and flowing streams out of the midst of thine own well.
text.KJV: 5:15 ¶ Drinke waters out of thine owne cisterne, and running waters out of thine owne well.
text.NABRE: 5:15 Drink water from your own cistern, running water from your own well.

text.NRSV: Should your springs be scattered abroad, streams of water in the streets?
text.Rotherham: Let not thy fountains, flow over, abroad, in the streets, dividings of waters:
text.KJV: 5:16 Let thy fountaines bee dispersed abroad, and riuers of waters in the streets.
text.NABRE: 5:16 Should your water sources be dispersed abroad, streams of water in the streets?

text.NRSV: Let them be for yourself alone, and not for sharing with strangers.
text.Rotherham: Let them be for thyself, alone, and not for strangers with thee.
text.KJV: 5:17 Let them be onely thine owne, and not strangers with thee.
text.NABRE: 5:17 Let them be yours alone, not shared with outsiders;

text.NRSV: Let your fountain be blessed, and rejoice in the wife of your youth,
text.Rotherham: Let thy well-spring be blessed,—and get thy joy from the wife of thy youth:—
text.KJV: 5:18 Let thy fountaine be blessed: and reioyce with the wife of thy youth.
text.NABRE: 5:18 Let your fountain be blessed and have joy of the wife of your youth,

text.NRSV: a lovely hind, a graceful doe. May her breasts satisfy you at all times; may you be intoxicated always by her love.
text.Rotherham: A loving hind! a graceful doe! let, her bosom, content thee at all times, and, in her love, mayst thou stray evermore.
text.KJV: 5:19 Let her bee as the louing Hinde and pleasant Roe, let her breasts satisfie thee at all times, and be thou rauisht alwayes with her loue.
text.NABRE: 5:19 your lovely hind, your graceful doe. Of whose love you will ever have your fill, and by her ardor always be intoxicated.

text.NRSV: Why should you be intoxicated, my son, by another woman and embrace the bosom of an adulteress?
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, then, shouldst thou stray, my son, with a strange woman? or embrace the bosom of a woman unknown?
text.KJV: 5:20 And why wilt thou, my sonne, be rauisht with a strange woman, and imbrace the bosome of a stranger?
text.NABRE: 5:20 Why then, my son, should you be intoxicated with a stranger, and embrace another woman?

text.NRSV: For human ways are under the eyes of the Lord, and he examines all their paths.
text.Rotherham: For, before the eyes of Yahweh, are the ways of a man,—and, all his tracks, doth he consider:
text.KJV: 5:21 For the wayes of man are before the eyes of the Lord, and he pondereth all his goings.
text.NABRE: 5:21 Indeed, the ways of each person are plain to the LORD’s sight; all their paths he surveys;

text.NRSV: The iniquities of the wicked ensnare them, and they are caught in the toils of their sin.
text.Rotherham: His own iniquities, shall entrap him with the lawless, and, by the cords of his own sin, shall he be seized.
text.KJV: 5:22 ¶ His owne iniquities shall take the wicked himselfe, and he shall be holden with the coards of his sinnes.
text.NABRE: 5:22 By their own iniquities the wicked will be caught, in the meshes of their own sin they will be held fast;

text.NRSV: They die for lack of discipline, and because of their great folly they are lost.
text.Rotherham: He, shall die, for lack of discipline, and, by the abounding of his perversity, shall he be lost.
text.KJV: 5:23 He shall die without instruction, and in the greatnesse of his folly he shal goe astray.
text.NABRE: 5:23 They will die from lack of discipline, lost because of their great folly.

text.NRSV: My child, if you have given your pledge to your neighbor, if you have bound yourself to a stranger,
text.Rotherham: My son, if thou have become surety for thy neighbour,—have struck for a stranger thy hands,
text.KJV: 6:1 My sonne, if thou bee surety for thy friend, it thou hast stricken thy hand with a stranger,
text.NABRE: 6:1 Chapter 6 Miscellaneous Proverbs - Against Going Surety for One’s Neighbor My son, if you have become surety to your neighbor, given your hand in pledge to another,

text.NRSV: you are snared by the utterance of your lips, caught by the words of your mouth.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast been snared by the sayings of thy mouth,—thou hast been caught by the sayings of thy mouth.
text.KJV: 6:2 Thou art snared with the words of thy mouth, thou art taken with the wordes of thy mouth.
text.NABRE: 6:2 You have been snared by the utterance of your lips, caught by the words of your mouth;

text.NRSV: So do this, my child, and save yourself, for you have come into your neighbor’s power: go, hurry, and plead with your neighbor.
text.Rotherham: Do this then, my son, and deliver thyself, When thou hast come into the hand of thy neighbour, Go, haste thee, and urge thy neighbour;
text.KJV: 6:3 Doe this now, my sonne, and deliuer thy selfe, when thou art come into the hand of thy friend: goe, humble thy selfe, and make sure thy friend.
text.NABRE: 6:3 So do this, my son, to free yourself, since you have fallen into your neighbor’s power: Go, hurry, rouse your neighbor!

text.NRSV: Give your eyes no sleep and your eyelids no slumber;
text.Rotherham: Do not give sleep to thine eyes, or slumber to thine eyelashes;
text.KJV: 6:4 Giue not sleepe to thine eyes, nor slumber to thine eyelids.
text.NABRE: 6:4 Give no sleep to your eyes, nor slumber to your eyelids;

text.NRSV: save yourself like a gazelle from the hunter, like a bird from the hand of the fowler.
text.Rotherham: Deliver thyself, as a gazelle out of the hand, and as a bird, out of the hand of the fowler.
text.KJV: 6:5 Deliuer thy selfe as a Roe from the hand of the hunter, and as a bird from the hand of the fowler.
text.NABRE: 6:5 Free yourself like a gazelle from the hunter, or like a bird from the hand of the fowler.

text.NRSV: Go to the ant, you lazybones; consider its ways, and be wise.
text.Rotherham: Go to the ant, thou sluggard, observe her ways, and be wise;
text.KJV: 6:6 ¶ Goe to the Ant, thou sluggard, consider her wayes, and be wise.
text.NABRE: 6:6 The Ant and the Sluggard at Harvest Go to the ant, O sluggard, study her ways and learn wisdom;

text.NRSV: Without having any chief or officer or ruler,
text.Rotherham: Which, having no harvest, scribe, or ruler,
text.KJV: 6:7 Which hauing no guide, ouerseer, or ruler,
text.NABRE: 6:7 For though she has no chief, no commander or ruler,

text.NRSV: it prepares its food in summer, and gathers its sustenance in harvest.
text.Rotherham: Prepareth, in the summer, her food, hath collected, in the harvest, her sustenance.
text.KJV: 6:8 Prouideth her meat in the Summer, and gathereth her food in the haruest.
text.NABRE: 6:8 She procures her food in the summer, stores up her provisions in the harvest.

text.NRSV: How long will you lie there, O lazybones? When will you rise from your sleep?
text.Rotherham: How long, O sluggard, wilt thou lie? how long ere thou rise from thy sleep?
text.KJV: 6:9 How long wilt thou sleepe, O sluggard? when wilt thou arise out of thy sleepe?
text.NABRE: 6:9 How long, O sluggard, will you lie there? when will you rise from your sleep?

text.NRSV: A little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to rest,
text.Rotherham: A little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to rest:—
text.KJV: 6:10 Yet a little sleepe, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to sleepe.
text.NABRE: 6:10 A little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the arms to rest—

text.NRSV: and poverty will come upon you like a robber, and want like an armed warrior.
text.Rotherham: So shall come in, as a highwayman, thy poverty, and, thy want, as one armed with a shield.
text.KJV: 6:11 So shall thy pouertie come as one that trauaileth, and thy want as an armed man.
text.NABRE: 6:11 Then poverty will come upon you like a robber, and want like a brigand.

text.NRSV: A scoundrel and a villain goes around with crooked speech,
text.Rotherham: An abandoned man, a man of iniquity, [is he] who—goeth on in perversity of mouth;
text.KJV: 6:12 ¶ A naughtie person, a wicked man walketh with a froward mouth.
text.NABRE: 6:12 The Scoundrel Scoundrels, villains, are they who deal in crooked talk.

text.NRSV: winking the eyes, shuffling the feet, pointing the fingers,
text.Rotherham: Winketh with his eyes, speaketh with his foot, pointeth with his fingers;
text.KJV: 6:13 He winketh with his eyes, he speaketh with his feete, hee teacheth with his fingers.
text.NABRE: 6:13 Shifty of eye, feet ever moving, pointing with fingers,

text.NRSV: with perverted mind devising evil, continually sowing discord;
text.Rotherham: [Hath] perverse things in his heart, deviseth mischief on every occasion, strifes, he sendeth forth.
text.KJV: 6:14 Frowardnesse is in his heart, he deuiseth mischiefe continually, he soweth discord.
text.NABRE: 6:14 They have perversity in their hearts, always plotting evil, sowing discord.

text.NRSV: therefore calamity will come upon them suddenly; in a moment they will be broken beyond healing.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, suddenly cometh his doom, in a moment, shall he be torn in pieces and there be no mending.
text.KJV: 6:15 Therefore shall his calamitie come suddenly; suddenly shall hee be broken without remedie.
text.NABRE: 6:15 Therefore their doom comes suddenly; in an instant they are crushed beyond cure.

text.NRSV: There are six things that the Lord hates, seven that are an abomination to him:
text.Rotherham: These six things, doth Yahweh hate, yea, seven, are the abomination of his soul:—
text.KJV: 6:16 ¶ These sixe things doeth the Lord hate; yea seuen are an abomination vnto him:
text.NABRE: 6:16 What the Lord Rejects There are six things the LORD hates, yes, seven are an abomination to him;

text.NRSV: haughty eyes, a lying tongue, and hands that shed innocent blood,
text.Rotherham: Eyes that are lofty, a tongue that is false, and hands shedding innocent blood;
text.KJV: 6:17 A proude looke, a lying tongue, and hands that shed innocent blood:
text.NABRE: 6:17 Haughty eyes, a lying tongue, hands that shed innocent blood,

text.NRSV: a heart that devises wicked plans, feet that hurry to run to evil,
text.Rotherham: A heart contriving iniquitous devices, feet hasting to run into mischief;
text.KJV: 6:18 An heart that deuiseth wicked imaginations, feet that be swift in running to mischiefe:
text.NABRE: 6:18 A heart that plots wicked schemes, feet that are quick to run to evil,

text.NRSV: a lying witness who testifies falsely, and one who sows discord in a family.
text.Rotherham: One that uttereth lies—a false witness, and one sending forth strifes between brethren.
text.KJV: 6:19 A false witnesse that speaketh lies; and him that soweth discord among brethren.
text.NABRE: 6:19 The false witness who utters lies, and the one who sows discord among kindred.

text.NRSV: My child, keep your father’s commandment, and do not forsake your mother’s teaching.
text.Rotherham: Observe thou, my son, the commandment of thy father, and do not decline from the instruction of thy mother:
text.KJV: 6:20 ¶ My sonne, keepe thy fathers commandement, and forsake not the law of thy mother.
text.NABRE: 6:20 Warning Against Adultery Observe, my son, your father’s command, and do not reject your mother’s teaching;

text.NRSV: Bind them continually on your heart; tie them around your neck.
text.Rotherham: Bind them upon thy heart continually, fasten them upon thy neck;
text.KJV: 6:21 Binde them continually vpon thine heart, and tie them about thy necke.
text.NABRE: 6:21 Keep them fastened over your heart always, tie them around your neck.

text.NRSV: When you walk, they will lead you; when you lie down, they will watch over you; and when you awake, they will talk with you.
text.Rotherham: When thou walkest abroad, it shall guide thee, when thou sleepest, it shall watch over thee, when thou wakest, it shall speak to thee:
text.KJV: 6:22 When thou goest, it shall leade thee; when thou sleepest, it shall keepe thee; and when thou awakest, it shall talke with thee.
text.NABRE: 6:22 When you lie down they will watch over you, when you wake, they will share your concerns; wherever you turn, they will guide you.

text.NRSV: For the commandment is a lamp and the teaching a light, and the reproofs of discipline are the way of life,
text.Rotherham: For, a lamp, is the commandment, and, the instruction, a light, and, the way of life, are the reproofs of correction:
text.KJV: 6:23 For the Commandement is a lampe, and the Lawe is light: and reproofes of instruction are the way of life:
text.NABRE: 6:23 For the command is a lamp, and the teaching a light, and a way to life are the reproofs that discipline,

text.NRSV: to preserve you from the wife of another, from the smooth tongue of the adulteress.
text.Rotherham: To keep thee from the wicked woman, from the flattery of the tongue of her that is a stranger.
text.KJV: 6:24 To keepe thee from the euill woman, from the flatterie of the tongue of a strange woman.
text.NABRE: 6:24 Keeping you from another’s wife, from the smooth tongue of the foreign woman.

text.NRSV: Do not desire her beauty in your heart, and do not let her capture you with her eyelashes;
text.Rotherham: Do not covet her beauty, in thy heart, neither let her take thee, by her eyelashes;
text.KJV: 6:25 Lust not after her beautie in thine heart; neither let her take thee with her eyelids.
text.NABRE: 6:25 Do not lust in your heart after her beauty, do not let her captivate you with her glance!

text.NRSV: for a prostitute’s fee is only a loaf of bread, but the wife of another stalks a man’s very life.
text.Rotherham: Because, for the sake of an impure woman, [a man may be brought] even to a cake of bread,—and, a man’s wife, for a precious soul, may hunt!
text.KJV: 6:26 For by meanes of a whorish woman, a man is brought to a piece of bread: and the adulteresse will hunt for the precious life.
text.NABRE: 6:26 For the price of a harlot may be scarcely a loaf of bread, But a married woman is a trap for your precious life.

text.NRSV: Can a man carry fire in his bosom and his clothes not be burned?
text.Rotherham: Can a man snatch up fire in his bosom, and, his clothes, not be burned?
text.KJV: 6:27 Can a man take fire in his bosome, and his clothes not be burnt?
text.NABRE: 6:27 Can a man take embers into his bosom, and his garments not be burned?

text.NRSV: Or can one walk on hot coals and his feet not be scorched?
text.Rotherham: Or can a man walk upon hot coals, and, his feet, not be burned?
text.KJV: 6:28 Can one goe vpon hote coales, and his feete not be burnt?
text.NABRE: 6:28 Or can a man walk on live coals, and his feet not be scorched?

text.NRSV: So is he who sleeps with his neighbor’s wife; no one who touches her will go unpunished.
text.Rotherham: So, he that goeth in unto his neighbour’s wife, no man shall be guiltless who toucheth her!
text.KJV: 6:29 So he that goeth in to his neighbours wife; whosoeuer toucheth her, shall not be innocent.
text.NABRE: 6:29 So with him who sleeps with another’s wife— none who touches her shall go unpunished.

text.NRSV: Thieves are not despised who steal only to satisfy their appetite when they are hungry.
text.Rotherham: Men despise not a thief, when he stealeth, to satisfy his appetite, because he is famished;
text.KJV: 6:30 Men doe not despise a thiefe, if he steale to satisfie his soule, when hee is hungry:
text.NABRE: 6:30 Thieves are not despised if out of hunger they steal to satisfy their appetite.

text.NRSV: Yet if they are caught, they will pay sevenfold; they will forfeit all the goods of their house.
text.Rotherham: Yet, if found, he must pay back sevenfold, All the substance of his house, must he give:
text.KJV: 6:31 But if he be found, he shall restore seuenfold, he shall giue all the substance of his house.
text.NABRE: 6:31 Yet if caught they must pay back sevenfold, yield up all the wealth of their house.

text.NRSV: But he who commits adultery has no sense; he who does it destroys himself.
text.Rotherham: He that committeth adultery with a woman, lacketh sense, A destroyer of his own life, is he that doeth it;
text.KJV: 6:32 But who so committeth adultery with a woman, lacketh vnderstanding: hee that doeth it, destroyeth his owne soule.
text.NABRE: 6:32 But those who commit adultery have no sense; those who do it destroy themselves.

text.NRSV: He will get wounds and dishonor, and his disgrace will not be wiped away.
text.Rotherham: Smiting and shame, shall he find, and, his reproach, shall not be wiped out;
text.KJV: 6:33 A wound and dishonour shall he get, and his reproch shall not be wiped away.
text.NABRE: 6:33 They will be beaten and disgraced, and their shame will not be wiped away;

text.NRSV: For jealousy arouses a husband’s fury, and he shows no restraint when he takes revenge.
text.Rotherham: For, jealousy, is the rage of a man, nor will he spare, in the day of avenging;
text.KJV: 6:34 For iealousie is the rage of a man: therefore he will not spare in the day of vengeance.
text.NABRE: 6:34 For passion enrages the husband, he will have no pity on the day of vengeance;

text.NRSV: He will accept no compensation, and refuses a bribe no matter how great.
text.Rotherham: He will not look, at any ransom, neither will he consent, though thou increase the bribe.
text.KJV: 6:35 He will not regard any ransome; neither will hee rest content, though thou giuest many giftes.
text.NABRE: 6:35 He will not consider any restitution, nor be satisfied by your many bribes.

text.NRSV: My child, keep my words and store up my commandments with you;
text.Rotherham: My son, keep my sayings, and, my commandments, treasure by thee;
text.KJV: 7:1 My sonne, keepe my words, and lay vp my commaundements with thee.
text.NABRE: 7:1 Chapter 7 The Seduction - My son, keep my words, and treasure my commands.

text.NRSV: keep my commandments and live, keep my teachings as the apple of your eye;
text.Rotherham: Keep my commandments and live, and mine instruction, as the pupil of thine eye;
text.KJV: 7:2 Keepe my commandements, and liue: and my law as the apple of thine eye.
text.NABRE: 7:2 Keep my commands and live, and my teaching as the apple of your eye;

text.NRSV: bind them on your fingers, write them on the tablet of your heart.
text.Rotherham: Bind them upon thy fingers, write them, on the tablet of thy heart;
text.KJV: 7:3 Bind them vpon thy fingers, write them vpon the table of thine heart.
text.NABRE: 7:3 Bind them on your fingers, write them on the tablet of your heart.

text.NRSV: Say to wisdom, “You are my sister,” and call insight your intimate friend,
text.Rotherham: Say unto wisdom, My sister, thou! and, an acquaintance, call thou, understanding:
text.KJV: 7:4 Say vnto Wisedome, Thou art my sister, and call Understanding thy kinse woman,
text.NABRE: 7:4 Say to Wisdom, “You are my sister!” Call Understanding, “Friend!”

text.NRSV: that they may keep you from the loose woman, from the adulteress with her smooth words.
text.Rotherham: That thou mayest be kept, from the woman that is a stranger, from the female unknown, who, with her speeches, doth flatter.
text.KJV: 7:5 That they may keepe thee from the strange woman, from the stranger which flattereth with her words.
text.NABRE: 7:5 That they may keep you from a stranger, from the foreign woman with her smooth words.

text.NRSV: For at the window of my house I looked out through my lattice,
text.Rotherham: For, in the window of my house, through my lattice, I looked out;
text.KJV: 7:6 ¶ For at the windowe of my house I looked through my casement,
text.NABRE: 7:6 For at the window of my house, through my lattice I looked out

text.NRSV: and I saw among the simple ones, I observed among the youths, a young man without sense,
text.Rotherham: And saw among the simple ones, discerned among the youths, A young man lacking sense;
text.KJV: 7:7 And behelde among the simple ones, I discerned among the youths, a yong man void of vnderstanding,
text.NABRE: 7:7 And I saw among the naive, I observed among the young men, a youth with no sense,

text.NRSV: passing along the street near her corner, taking the road to her house
text.Rotherham: Passing through the street, near her corner, and, on the way to her house, he sauntered along;
text.KJV: 7:8 Passing through the streete neere her corner, and he went the way to her house,
text.NABRE: 7:8 Crossing the street near the corner, then walking toward her house,

text.NRSV: in the twilight, in the evening, at the time of night and darkness.
text.Rotherham: In the twilight, in the evening of the day, in the midst of the night, and the gloom;
text.KJV: 7:9 In the twilight in the euening, in the blacke and darke night:
text.NABRE: 7:9 In the twilight, at dusk of day, in the very dark of night.

text.NRSV: Then a woman comes toward him, decked out like a prostitute, wily of heart.
text.Rotherham: And lo! a woman, came to meet him, attired as one unchaste, of a wily heart.
text.KJV: 7:10 And behold, there met him a woman, with the attire of an harlot, and subtill of heart.
text.NABRE: 7:10 Then the woman comes to meet him, dressed like a harlot, with secret designs.

text.NRSV: She is loud and wayward; her feet do not stay at home;
text.Rotherham: Boisterous, is she, and rebellious, In her house, abide not her feet;
text.KJV: 7:11 (She is loud and stubburne, her feet abide not in her house:
text.NABRE: 7:11 She is raucous and unruly, her feet cannot stay at home;

text.NRSV: now in the street, now in the squares, and at every corner she lies in wait.
text.Rotherham: Now outside, now in the broadways, and, near every corner, she lieth in wait:
text.KJV: 7:12 Now is shee without, now in the streetes, and lieth in waite at euery corner.)
text.NABRE: 7:12 Now she is in the streets, now in the open squares, lurking in ambush at every corner.

text.NRSV: She seizes him and kisses him, and with impudent face she says to him:
text.Rotherham: So she caught him, and kissed him, and, embolding her face, she said to him:
text.KJV: 7:13 So she caught him, and kissed him, and with an impudent face, said vnto him,
text.NABRE: 7:13 Then she grabs him, kisses him, and with an impudent look says to him:

text.NRSV: “I had to offer sacrifices, and today I have paid my vows;
text.Rotherham: Peace-offerings, are by me, to-day, have I paid my vows;
text.KJV: 7:14 I haue peace offerings with me: this day haue I paid my vowes.
text.NABRE: 7:14 “I owed peace offerings, and today I have fulfilled my vows;

text.NRSV: so now I have come out to meet you, to seek you eagerly, and I have found you!
text.Rotherham: For this cause, came I forth to meet thee, to seek diligently thy face, and I have found thee:
text.KJV: 7:15 Therefore came I forth to meete thee, diligently to seeke thy face, and I haue found thee.
text.NABRE: 7:15 So I came out to meet you, to look for you, and I have found you!

text.NRSV: I have decked my couch with coverings, colored spreads of Egyptian linen;
text.Rotherham: Coverlets, have I spread on my couch of pleasure, dark-hued stuffs, of the yarn of Egypt;
text.KJV: 7:16 I haue deckt my bed with couerings of tapestrie, with carued workes, with fine linnen of Egypt.
text.NABRE: 7:16 With coverlets I have spread my couch, with brocaded cloths of Egyptian linen;

text.NRSV: I have perfumed my bed with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon.
text.Rotherham: I have sprinkled my bed, with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon:
text.KJV: 7:17 I haue perfumed my bed with myrrhe, aloes, and cynamom.
text.NABRE: 7:17 I have sprinkled my bed with myrrh, with aloes, and with cinnamon.

text.NRSV: Come, let us take our fill of love until morning; let us delight ourselves with love.
text.Rotherham: Come! let us take our fill of endearments, until morning, let us delight ourselves with caresses;
text.KJV: 7:18 Come, let vs take our fill of loue vntill the morning, let vs solace our selues with loues.
text.NABRE: 7:18 Come, let us drink our fill of love, until morning, let us feast on love!

text.NRSV: For my husband is not at home; he has gone on a long journey.
text.Rotherham: For the husband is not in his house, he hath gone on a journey afar;
text.KJV: 7:19 For the good-man is not at home, he is gone a long iourney.
text.NABRE: 7:19 For my husband is not at home, he has gone on a long journey;

text.NRSV: He took a bag of money with him; he will not come home until full moon.”
text.Rotherham: A bag of silver, hath he taken in his hand, On the day of the full moon, will he enter his house.
text.KJV: 7:20 He hath taken a bag of money with him, and will come home at the day appointed.
text.NABRE: 7:20 A bag of money he took with him, he will not return home till the full moon.”

text.NRSV: With much seductive speech she persuades him; with her smooth talk she compels him.
text.Rotherham: She turneth him aside, with her great persuasiveness,—with the flattery of her lips, she compelleth him:
text.KJV: 7:21 With much faire speech she caused him to yeeld, with the flattering of her lips she forced him.
text.NABRE: 7:21 She wins him over by repeated urging, with her smooth lips she leads him astray.

text.NRSV: Right away he follows her, and goes like an ox to the slaughter, or bound like a stag to the trap
text.Rotherham: Going after her instantly, as an ox, to the slaughter, he entereth, and, as in fetters, unto the correction of a fool.
text.KJV: 7:22 He goeth after her straightway, as an oxe goeth to the slaughter, or as a foole to the correction of the stocks,
text.NABRE: 7:22 He follows her impulsively, like an ox that goes to slaughter; Like a stag that bounds toward the net,

text.NRSV: until an arrow pierces its entrails. He is like a bird rushing into a snare, not knowing that it will cost him his life.
text.Rotherham: Until an arrow cleaveth his liver, as a bird hasteth into a snare, and knoweth not, that, for his life, it is!
text.KJV: 7:23 Til a dart strike through his liuer, as a bird hasteth to the snare, and knoweth not that it is for his life.
text.NABRE: 7:23 till an arrow pierces its liver; Like a bird that rushes into a snare, unaware that his life is at stake.

text.NRSV: And now, my children, listen to me, and be attentive to the words of my mouth.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, ye sons, hearken unto me, and attend, to the sayings of my mouth;
text.KJV: 7:24 ¶ Hearken vnto me now therefore, O ye children, and attend to the words of my mouth.
text.NABRE: 7:24 So now, children, listen to me, be attentive to the words of my mouth!

text.NRSV: Do not let your hearts turn aside to her ways; do not stray into her paths;
text.Rotherham: Let not thy heart, turn aside to her ways, Do not go astray, in her paths.
text.KJV: 7:25 Let not thine heart decline to her wayes, goe not astray in her paths.
text.NABRE: 7:25 Do not let your heart turn to her ways, do not go astray in her paths;

text.NRSV: for many are those she has laid low, and numerous are her victims.
text.Rotherham: For, many, wounded, hath she caused to fall,—yea strong men, slain wholly by her:
text.KJV: 7:26 For shee hath cast downe many wounded: yea many strong men haue bene slaine by her.
text.NABRE: 7:26 For many are those she has struck down dead, numerous, those she has slain.

text.NRSV: Her house is the way to Sheol, going down to the chambers of death.
text.Rotherham: Ways to hades, are in her house, descending into the chambers of death.
text.KJV: 7:27 Her house is the way to hell, going downe to the chambers of death.
text.NABRE: 7:27 Her house is a highway to Sheol, leading down into the chambers of death.

text.NRSV: Does not wisdom call, and does not understanding raise her voice?
text.Rotherham: Doth not, wisdom, cry aloud? and, understanding, send forth her voice?
text.KJV: 8:1 Doeth not Wisedome crie? & Understanding put foorth her voice?
text.NABRE: 8:1 Chapter 8 The Discourse of Wisdom - Does not Wisdom call, and Understanding raise her voice?

text.NRSV: On the heights, beside the way, at the crossroads she takes her stand;
text.Rotherham: At the top of the high places above the way, at the place where paths meet, she taketh her stand:
text.KJV: 8:2 Shee standeth in the top of high places, by the way in the places of the pathes.
text.NABRE: 8:2 On the top of the heights along the road, at the crossroads she takes her stand;

text.NRSV: beside the gates in front of the town, at the entrance of the portals she cries out:
text.Rotherham: Beside the gates, at the entrance of the city,—at the going in of the openings, she shouteth:—
text.KJV: 8:3 She cryeth at the gates, at the entrie of the citie, at the comming in at the doores.
text.NABRE: 8:3 By the gates at the approaches of the city, in the entryways she cries aloud:

text.NRSV: “To you, O people, I call, and my cry is to all that live.
text.Rotherham: Unto you, O men, I call, and, my voice, is unto the sons of men;
text.KJV: 8:4 Unto you, O men, I call, and my voice is to the sonnes of man.
text.NABRE: 8:4 “To you, O people, I call; my appeal is to you mortals.

text.NRSV: O simple ones, learn prudence; acquire intelligence, you who lack it.
text.Rotherham: Understand, ye simple ones, shrewdness, and, ye dullards understand sense;
text.KJV: 8:5 O yee simple, vnderstand wisedome: and yee fooles, be yee of an vnderstanding heart.
