text.NABRE: 8:5 You naive ones, gain prudence, you fools, gain sense.

text.NRSV: Hear, for I will speak noble things, and from my lips will come what is right;
text.Rotherham: Hear, for, princely things, will I speak, and the opening of my lips shall be of equity;
text.KJV: 8:6 Heare, for I will speake of excellent things: and the opening of my lippes shalbe right things.
text.NABRE: 8:6 Listen! for noble things I speak; my lips proclaim honest words.

text.NRSV: for my mouth will utter truth; wickedness is an abomination to my lips.
text.Rotherham: For, faithfulness, shall my mouth softly utter, but, the abomination of my lips, shall be lawlessness;
text.KJV: 8:7 For my mouth shall speake truth, and wickednesse is an abomination to my lippes.
text.NABRE: 8:7 Indeed, my mouth utters truth, and my lips abhor wickedness.

text.NRSV: All the words of my mouth are righteous; there is nothing twisted or crooked in them.
text.Rotherham: In righteousness, shall be all the sayings of my mouth, nothing therein, shall be crafty or perverse;
text.KJV: 8:8 All the words of my mouth are in righteousnes, there is nothing froward or peruerse in them.
text.NABRE: 8:8 All the words of my mouth are sincere, none of them wily or crooked;

text.NRSV: They are all straight to one who understands and right to those who find knowledge.
text.Rotherham: All of them, shall be plain, to them who would understand, and just, to such as would gain knowledge.
text.KJV: 8:9 They are all plaine to him that vnderstandeth: and right to them that find knowledge.
text.NABRE: 8:9 All of them are straightforward to the intelligent, and right to those who attain knowledge.

text.NRSV: Take my instruction instead of silver, and knowledge rather than choice gold;
text.Rotherham: Receive my correction, and not silver, and knowledge, rather than choicest gold.
text.KJV: 8:10 Receiue my instruction, and not siluer: and knowledge rather then choise gold.
text.NABRE: 8:10 Take my instruction instead of silver, and knowledge rather than choice gold.

text.NRSV: for wisdom is better than jewels, and all that you may desire cannot compare with her.
text.Rotherham: For better is wisdom, than ornaments of coral, and, no delightful things, can equal her.
text.KJV: 8:11 For wisedome is better then rubies: and all the things that may be desired, are not to be compared to it.
text.NABRE: 8:11 [For Wisdom is better than corals, and no treasures can compare with her.]

text.NRSV: I, wisdom, live with prudence, and I attain knowledge and discretion.
text.Rotherham: I, wisdom, inhabit shrewdness,—and, the knowledge of sagacious things, I gain.
text.KJV: 8:12 I wisedome dwell with prudence, and find out knowledge of witty inuentions.
text.NABRE: 8:12 I, Wisdom, dwell with prudence, and useful knowledge I have.

text.NRSV: The fear of the Lord is hatred of evil. Pride and arrogance and the way of evil and perverted speech I hate.
text.Rotherham: The reverence of Yahweh, is to hate wickedness: pride, arrogance, and the way of wickedness; And a mouth of perverse things, do I hate.
text.KJV: 8:13 The feare of the Lord is to hate euill: pride and arrogancie, and the euill way, and the froward mouth doe I hate.
text.NABRE: 8:13 [The fear of the LORD is hatred of evil;] Pride, arrogance, the evil way, and the perverse mouth I hate.

text.NRSV: I have good advice and sound wisdom; I have insight, I have strength.
text.Rotherham: Mine, are counsel, and effective working, I, am understanding, mine, is valour:
text.KJV: 8:14 Counsell is mine, and sound wisedome: I am vnderstanding, I haue strength.
text.NABRE: 8:14 Mine are counsel and advice; Mine is strength; I am understanding.

text.NRSV: By me kings reign, and rulers decree what is just;
text.Rotherham: By me, kings reign, and dignitaries decree righteousness;
text.KJV: 8:15 By me kings reigne, and princes decree iustice.
text.NABRE: 8:15 By me kings reign, and rulers enact justice;

text.NRSV: by me rulers rule, and nobles, all who govern rightly.
text.Rotherham: By me, rulers govern, and nobles—all the righteous judges:
text.KJV: 8:16 By me Princes rule, and Nobles, euen all the Iudges of the earth.
text.NABRE: 8:16 By me princes govern, and nobles, all the judges of the earth.

text.NRSV: I love those who love me, and those who seek me diligently find me.
text.Rotherham: I love, them who love me, and, they who diligently seek me, find me:
text.KJV: 8:17 I loue them that loue me, and those that seeke me early, shall find me.
text.NABRE: 8:17 Those who love me I also love, and those who seek me find me.

text.NRSV: Riches and honor are with me, enduring wealth and prosperity.
text.Rotherham: Riches and honour, are with me, lordly wealth, and righteousness;
text.KJV: 8:18 Riches and honour are with me, yea durable riches and righteousnesse.
text.NABRE: 8:18 With me are riches and honor, wealth that endures, and righteousness.

text.NRSV: My fruit is better than gold, even fine gold, and my yield than choice silver.
text.Rotherham: Better is my fruit, than gold—yea fine gold, and mine increase, than choice silver;
text.KJV: 8:19 My fruite is better then gold, yea then fine gold, and my reuenue then choise siluer.
text.NABRE: 8:19 My fruit is better than gold, even pure gold, and my yield than choice silver.

text.NRSV: I walk in the way of righteousness, along the paths of justice,
text.Rotherham: In the way of righteousness, I march along, in the middle of the paths of justice:
text.KJV: 8:20 I leade in the way of righteousnesse, in the midst of the pathes of iudgment.
text.NABRE: 8:20 On the way of righteousness I walk, along the paths of justice,

text.NRSV: endowing with wealth those who love me, and filling their treasuries.
text.Rotherham: That I may cause them who love me to inherit substance, and, their treasuries, I may fill.
text.KJV: 8:21 That I may cause those that loue me, to inherite substance: and I will fill their treasures.
text.NABRE: 8:21 Granting wealth to those who love me, and filling their treasuries.

text.NRSV: The Lord created me at the beginning of his work, the first of his acts of old.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, had constituted me the beginning of his way, before his works, at the commencement of that time;
text.KJV: 8:22 The Lord possessed me in the beginning of his way, before his works of old.
text.NABRE: 8:22 “The LORD begot me, the beginning of his works, the forerunner of his deeds of long ago;

text.NRSV: Ages ago I was set up, at the first, before the beginning of the earth.
text.Rotherham: At the outset of the ages, had I been established, in advance of the antiquities of the earth;
text.KJV: 8:23 I was set vp from euerlasting, from the beginning, or euer the earth was.
text.NABRE: 8:23 From of old I was formed, at the first, before the earth.

text.NRSV: When there were no depths I was brought forth, when there were no springs abounding with water.
text.Rotherham: When there was no resounding deep, I had been brought forth, when there were no fountains, abounding with water;
text.KJV: 8:24 When there were no depthes, I was brought forth: when there were no fountaines abounding with water.
text.NABRE: 8:24 When there were no deeps I was brought forth, when there were no fountains or springs of water;

text.NRSV: Before the mountains had been shaped, before the hills, I was brought forth—
text.Rotherham: Ere yet the mountains had been settled, before the hills, had I been brought forth;
text.KJV: 8:25 Before the mountaines were setled: before the hilles, was I brought foorth:
text.NABRE: 8:25 Before the mountains were settled into place, before the hills, I was brought forth;

text.NRSV: when he had not yet made earth and fields, or the world’s first bits of soil.
text.Rotherham: Or ever he had made the land and the wastes, or the top of the dry parts of the world:
text.KJV: 8:26 While as yet he had not made the earth, nor the fields, nor the highest part of the dust of the world.
text.NABRE: 8:26 When the earth and the fields were not yet made, nor the first clods of the world.

text.NRSV: When he established the heavens, I was there, when he drew a circle on the face of the deep,
text.Rotherham: When he prepared the heavens, there, was I! When he decreed a vault upon the face of the resounding deep;
text.KJV: 8:27 When hee prepared the heauens, I was there: when he set a compasse vpon the face of the depth.
text.NABRE: 8:27 When he established the heavens, there was I, when he marked out the vault over the face of the deep;

text.NRSV: when he made firm the skies above, when he established the fountains of the deep,
text.Rotherham: When he made firm the skies above, when the fountains of the resounding deep, waxed strong;
text.KJV: 8:28 When he established the cloudes aboue: when he strengthned the fountaines of the deepe.
text.NABRE: 8:28 When he made firm the skies above, when he fixed fast the springs of the deep;

text.NRSV: when he assigned to the sea its limit, so that the waters might not transgress his command, when he marked out the foundations of the earth,
text.Rotherham: When he fixed for the sea its bound, that, the waters, should not go beyond his bidding, when he decreed the foundations of the earth:—
text.KJV: 8:29 When he gaue to the sea his decree, that the waters should not passe his commandement: when he appointed the foundations of the earth:
text.NABRE: 8:29 When he set for the sea its limit, so that the waters should not transgress his command; When he fixed the foundations of earth,

text.NRSV: then I was beside him, like a master worker; and I was daily his delight, rejoicing before him always,
text.Rotherham: Then became I beside him, a firm and sure worker, then became I filled with delight, day by day, exulting before him on every occasion;
text.KJV: 8:30 Then I was by him, as one brought vp with him: and I was daily his delight, reioycing alwayes before him:
text.NABRE: 8:30 then was I beside him as artisan; I was his delight day by day, playing before him all the while,

text.NRSV: rejoicing in his inhabited world and delighting in the human race.
text.Rotherham: Exulting in the fruitful land of his earth, Yea, my fulness of delight, was with the sons of men.
text.KJV: 8:31 Reioycing in the habitable part of his earth, and my delights were with the sonnes of men.
text.NABRE: 8:31 Playing over the whole of his earth, having my delight with human beings.

text.NRSV: And now, my children, listen to me: happy are those who keep my ways.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, ye sons, hearken to me, for how happy are they who, to my ways, pay regard!
text.KJV: 8:32 Nowe therefore hearken vnto me, O yee children: for blessed are they that keepe my wayes.
text.NABRE: 8:32 Now, children, listen to me; happy are they who keep my ways.

text.NRSV: Hear instruction and be wise, and do not neglect it.
text.Rotherham: Hear ye correction, and be wise, and do not neglect.
text.KJV: 8:33 Heare instruction, and bee wise, and refuse it not.
text.NABRE: 8:33 Listen to instruction and grow wise, do not reject it!

text.NRSV: Happy is the one who listens to me, watching daily at my gates, waiting at my doorposts.
text.Rotherham: How happy the man that doth hearken to me,—keeping guard at my doors, day by day, watching at the posts of my gates;
text.KJV: 8:34 Blessed is the man that heareth me: watching daily at my gates, waiting at the postes of my doores.
text.NABRE: 8:34 Happy the one who listens to me, attending daily at my gates, keeping watch at my doorposts;

text.NRSV: For whoever finds me finds life and obtains favor from the Lord;
text.Rotherham: For, he that findeth me, findeth life, and hath obtained favour from Yahweh;
text.KJV: 8:35 For whoso findeth mee, findeth life, and shall obtaine fauour of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 8:35 For whoever finds me finds life, and wins favor from the LORD;

text.NRSV: but those who miss me injure themselves; all who hate me love death.”
text.Rotherham: But, he that misseth me, wrongeth his own soul, all who hate me, love death.
text.KJV: 8:36 But hee that sinneth against me, wrongeth his owne soule; all they that hate me, loue death.
text.NABRE: 8:36 But those who pass me by do violence to themselves; all who hate me love death.”

text.NRSV: Wisdom has built her house, she has hewn her seven pillars.
text.Rotherham: Wisdom, hath builded her house, hath hewn out her seven pillars;
text.KJV: 9:1 Wisedome hath builded her house: she hath hewen out her seuen pillars.
text.NABRE: 9:1 Chapter 9 The Two Women Invite Passersby to Their Banquets - Woman Wisdom Issues Her Invitation Wisdom has built her house, she has set up her seven columns;

text.NRSV: She has slaughtered her animals, she has mixed her wine, she has also set her table.
text.Rotherham: hath slaughtered her beasts, hath mingled her wine, hath even set in order her table;
text.KJV: 9:2 She hath killed her beastes; she hath mingled her wine: she hath also furnished her table.
text.NABRE: 9:2 She has prepared her meat, mixed her wine, yes, she has spread her table.

text.NRSV: She has sent out her servant-girls, she calls from the highest places in the town,
text.Rotherham: hath sent forth her maidens, She crieth aloud, upon the tops of the heights of the city:
text.KJV: 9:3 She hath sent forth her maidens; she cryeth vpon the highest places of the citie.
text.NABRE: 9:3 She has sent out her maidservants; she calls from the heights out over the city:

text.NRSV: “You that are simple, turn in here!” To those without sense she says,
text.Rotherham: Whoso is simple, let him turn in hither, as for him that lacketh sense, she saith to him—
text.KJV: 9:4 Who so is simple, let him turne in hither: as for him that wanteth vnderstanding, she sayth to him:
text.NABRE: 9:4 “Let whoever is naive turn in here; to any who lack sense I say,

text.NRSV: “Come, eat of my bread and drink of the wine I have mixed.
text.Rotherham: Come, feed on my food, and drink of the wine I have mingled;
text.KJV: 9:5 Come, eate of my bread, and drinke of the wine, which I haue mingled.
text.NABRE: 9:5 Come, eat of my food, and drink of the wine I have mixed!

text.NRSV: Lay aside immaturity, and live, and walk in the way of insight.”
text.Rotherham: Forsake the simple ones, and live, and advance in the way of understanding.
text.KJV: 9:6 Forsake the foolish, and liue; and goe in the way of vnderstanding.
text.NABRE: 9:6 Forsake foolishness that you may live; advance in the way of understanding.”

text.NRSV: Whoever corrects a scoffer wins abuse; whoever rebukes the wicked gets hurt.
text.Rotherham: He that rebuketh a scoffer, getteth to himself contempt, and, he that reproveth a lawless man, [getteth to himself] his shame.
text.KJV: 9:7 He that reproueth a scorner, getteth to himselfe shame: and he that rebuketh a wicked man, getteth himselfe a blot.
text.NABRE: 9:7 Miscellaneous Aphorisms Whoever corrects the arrogant earns insults; and whoever reproves the wicked incurs opprobrium.

text.NRSV: A scoffer who is rebuked will only hate you; the wise, when rebuked, will love you.
text.Rotherham: Do not reprove a scoffer, lest he hate thee, Reprove a wise man, and he will love thee.
text.KJV: 9:8 Reproue not a scorner, lest hee hate thee: rebuke a wise man, and hee will loue thee.
text.NABRE: 9:8 Do not reprove the arrogant, lest they hate you; reprove the wise, and they will love you.

text.NRSV: Give instruction to the wise, and they will become wiser still; teach the righteous and they will gain in learning.
text.Rotherham: Give to a wise man, and he will be wiser still, Inform a righteous man, and he will increase learning.
text.KJV: 9:9 Giue instruction to a wise man, and he will be yet wiser: teach a iust man, and he will increase in learning.
text.NABRE: 9:9 Instruct the wise, and they become still wiser; teach the just, and they advance in learning.

text.NRSV: The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom, and the knowledge of the Holy One is insight.
text.Rotherham: The beginning of wisdom, is the reverence of Yahweh, and, the knowledge of the Holy, is understanding;
text.KJV: 9:10 The feare of the Lord is the beginning of wisedome: and the knowledge of the holy is vnderstanding.
text.NABRE: 9:10 The beginning of wisdom is fear of the LORD, and knowledge of the Holy One is understanding.

text.NRSV: For by me your days will be multiplied, and years will be added to your life.
text.Rotherham: For, by me, shall be multiplied—thy days, and there be added to thee—the years of life.
text.KJV: 9:11 For by me thy dayes shall be multiplied: and the yeeres of thy life shalbe increased.
text.NABRE: 9:11 For by me your days will be multiplied and the years of your life increased.

text.NRSV: If you are wise, you are wise for yourself; if you scoff, you alone will bear it.
text.Rotherham: If thou art wise, thou art wise for thyself, but, if thou scoff, alone, shalt thou bear it.
text.KJV: 9:12 If thou be wise, thou shalt be wise for thy selfe: but if thou scornest, thou alone shalt beare it.
text.NABRE: 9:12 If you are wise, wisdom is to your advantage; if you are arrogant, you alone shall bear it.

text.NRSV: The foolish woman is loud; she is ignorant and knows nothing.
text.Rotherham: The woman Stupidity, is boisterous, so simple that she knoweth not what she would do;
text.KJV: 9:13 ¶ A foolish woman is clamorous: she is simple, & knoweth nothing.
text.NABRE: 9:13 Woman Folly Issues Her Invitation Woman Folly is raucous, utterly foolish; she knows nothing.

text.NRSV: She sits at the door of her house, on a seat at the high places of the town,
text.Rotherham: So she sitteth at the entrance of her house, upon a seat, in the heights of the city;
text.KJV: 9:14 For she sitteth at the doore of her house on a seate, in the high places of the Citie:
text.NABRE: 9:14 She sits at the door of her house upon a seat on the city heights,

text.NRSV: calling to those who pass by, who are going straight on their way,
text.Rotherham: To invite them who pass by the way, who are going straight on in their paths:
text.KJV: 9:15 To call passengers who go right on their wayes:
text.NABRE: 9:15 Calling to passersby as they go on their way straight ahead:

text.NRSV: “You who are simple, turn in here!” And to those without sense she says,
text.Rotherham: Whoso is simple, let him turn aside hither, and, as for him that lacketh sense, she saith to him:
text.KJV: 9:16 Who so is simple, let him turne in hither: and as for him that wanteth vnderstanding, she saith to him;
text.NABRE: 9:16 “Let those who are naive turn in here, to those who lack sense I say,

text.NRSV: “Stolen water is sweet, and bread eaten in secret is pleasant.”
text.Rotherham: Stolen waters, will be sweet,—and a secret meal, will be pleasant;
text.KJV: 9:17 Stollen waters are sweet, and bread eaten in secret is pleasant.
text.NABRE: 9:17 Stolen water is sweet, and bread taken secretly is pleasing!”

text.NRSV: But they do not know that the shades are there, that her guests are in the depths of Sheol.
text.Rotherham: But he knoweth not, that the shades are there; In the depths of hades, are her guests.
text.KJV: 9:18 But hee knoweth not that the dead are there, and that her guests are in the depths of hell.
text.NABRE: 9:18 Little do they know that the shades are there, that her guests are in the depths of Sheol!

text.NRSV: The proverbs of Solomon. A wise child makes a glad father, but a foolish child is a mother’s grief.
text.Rotherham: The Proverbs of Solomon: A wise son, maketh a glad father, but, a foolish son, is the grief of his mother.
text.KJV: 10:1 The Prouerbes of Solomon: A wise sonne maketh a glad father: but a foolish sonne is the heauinesse of his mother.
text.NABRE: 10:1 III. First Solomonic Collection of Sayings Chapter 10 - The Proverbs of Solomon: A wise son gives his father joy, but a foolish son is a grief to his mother.

text.NRSV: Treasures gained by wickedness do not profit, but righteousness delivers from death.
text.Rotherham: The treasures of lawlessness, do not profit, but, righteousness, delivereth from death.
text.KJV: 10:2 Treasures of wickednesse profit nothing: but righteousnes deliuereth from death.
text.NABRE: 10:2 Ill-gotten treasures profit nothing, but justice saves from death.

text.NRSV: The Lord does not let the righteous go hungry, but he thwarts the craving of the wicked.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, suffereth not to famish, the soul of the righteous, but, the desire of the lawless, he thrusteth away.
text.KJV: 10:3 The Lord will not suffer the soule of the righteous to famish: but he casteth away the substance of the wicked.
text.NABRE: 10:3 The LORD does not let the just go hungry, but the craving of the wicked he thwarts.

text.NRSV: A slack hand causes poverty, but the hand of the diligent makes rich.
text.Rotherham: He becometh poor, who dealeth with a slack hand, but, the hand of the diligent, maketh rich.
text.KJV: 10:4 Hee becommeth poore that dealeth with a slacke hand: but the hand of the diligent, maketh rich.
text.NABRE: 10:4 The slack hand impoverishes, but the busy hand brings riches.

text.NRSV: A son who gathers in summer is prudent, but a son who sleeps in harvest brings shame.
text.Rotherham: He that gathereth in summer, is a prudent son, he that sleepeth long in harvest, is a son causing shame.
text.KJV: 10:5 Hee that gathereth in Summer, is a wise sonne: but hee that sleepeth in haruest, is a sonne that causeth shame.
text.NABRE: 10:5 A son who gathers in summer is a credit; a son who slumbers during harvest, a disgrace.

text.NRSV: Blessings are on the head of the righteous, but the mouth of the wicked conceals violence.
text.Rotherham: Blessings, are for the head of the righteous man, but, the mouth of the lawless, covereth up wrong.
text.KJV: 10:6 Blessings are vpon the head of the iust: but violence couereth the mouth of the wicked.
text.NABRE: 10:6 Blessings are for the head of the just; but the mouth of the wicked conceals violence.

text.NRSV: The memory of the righteous is a blessing, but the name of the wicked will rot.
text.Rotherham: The memory of the righteous, yieldeth blessing, but, the name of the lawless, dieth out.
text.KJV: 10:7 The memorie of the iust is blessed: but the name of the wicked shall rot.
text.NABRE: 10:7 The memory of the just serves as blessing, but the name of the wicked will rot.

text.NRSV: The wise of heart will heed commandments, but a babbling fool will come to ruin.
text.Rotherham: The wise in heart, will accept commandments, but, he that is foolish with his lips, shall be thrust away.
text.KJV: 10:8 The wise in heart will receiue commaundements: but a prating foole shall fall.
text.NABRE: 10:8 A wise heart accepts commands, but a babbling fool will be overthrown.

text.NRSV: Whoever walks in integrity walks securely, but whoever follows perverted ways will be found out.
text.Rotherham: He that walketh uprightly, may walk securely, but, he that maketh crooked his ways, shall be found out.
text.KJV: 10:9 He that walketh vprightly, walketh surely: but he that peruerteth his wayes, shalbe knowen.
text.NABRE: 10:9 Whoever walks honestly walks securely, but one whose ways are crooked will fare badly.

text.NRSV: Whoever winks the eye causes trouble, but the babbling fool will come to ruin.
text.Rotherham: He that winketh with the eye, causeth sorrow, and, he that is foolish with his lips, shall be thrust aside.
text.KJV: 10:10 Hee that winketh with the eye, causeth sorrow: but a prating foole shall fall.
text.NABRE: 10:10 One who winks at a fault causes trouble, but one who frankly reproves promotes peace.

text.NRSV: The mouth of the righteous is a fountain of life, but the mouth of the wicked conceals violence.
text.Rotherham: A well-spring of life, is the mouth of the righteous, but, the mouth of the lawless, covereth wrong.
text.KJV: 10:11 The mouth of a righteous man is a well of life: but violence couereth the mouth of the wicked.
text.NABRE: 10:11 The mouth of the just is a fountain of life, but the mouth of the wicked conceals violence.

text.NRSV: Hatred stirs up strife, but love covers all offenses.
text.Rotherham: Hatred, stirreth up strifes, but, over all transgressions, love throweth a covering.
text.KJV: 10:12 Hatred stirreth vp strifes: but loue couereth all sinnes.
text.NABRE: 10:12 Hatred stirs up disputes, but love covers all offenses.

text.NRSV: On the lips of one who has understanding wisdom is found, but a rod is for the back of one who lacks sense.
text.Rotherham: In the lips of the intelligent, is found wisdom, but, a rod, is for the back of him that lacketh sense.
text.KJV: 10:13 In the lips of him that hath vnderstanding, wisedome is found: but a rod is for the backe of him that is voyd of vnderstanding.
text.NABRE: 10:13 On the lips of the intelligent is found wisdom, but a rod for the back of one without sense.

text.NRSV: The wise lay up knowledge, but the babbling of a fool brings ruin near.
text.Rotherham: Wise men, treasure up knowledge, but, the mouth of the foolish, is a terror near at hand.
text.KJV: 10:14 Wise men lay vp knowledge: but the mouth of the foolish is neere destruction.
text.NABRE: 10:14 The wise store up knowledge, but the mouth of a fool is imminent ruin.

text.NRSV: The wealth of the rich is their fortress; the poverty of the poor is their ruin.
text.Rotherham: The substance of the rich, is his strong city, the terror of the poor, is their poverty.
text.KJV: 10:15 The rich mans wealth is his strong citie: the destruction of the poore is their pouertie.
text.NABRE: 10:15 The wealth of the rich is their strong city; the ruin of the poor is their poverty.

text.NRSV: The wage of the righteous leads to life, the gain of the wicked to sin.
text.Rotherham: The labour of the righteous, [leadeth] to life, the increase of the lawless, to sin.
text.KJV: 10:16 The labour of the righteous tendeth to life: the fruite of the wicked to sinne.
text.NABRE: 10:16 The labor of the just leads to life, the gains of the wicked, to futility.

text.NRSV: Whoever heeds instruction is on the path to life, but one who rejects a rebuke goes astray.
text.Rotherham: On the way to life, is he that heedeth correction, but, he that hateth reproof, is going astray.
text.KJV: 10:17 He is in the way of life that keepeth instruction: but hee that refuseth reproofe, erreth.
text.NABRE: 10:17 Whoever follows instruction is in the path to life, but whoever disregards reproof goes astray.

text.NRSV: Lying lips conceal hatred, and whoever utters slander is a fool.
text.Rotherham: He that concealeth hatred, hath false lips, and he that sendeth forth slander, the same, is a dullard.
text.KJV: 10:18 Hee that hideth hatred with lying lippes, and he that vttereth a slander, is a foole.
text.NABRE: 10:18 Whoever conceals hatred has lying lips, and whoever spreads slander is a fool.

text.NRSV: When words are many, transgression is not lacking, but the prudent are restrained in speech.
text.Rotherham: In the multitude of words, there wanteth not transgression, but, he that restraineth his lips, sheweth prudence.
text.KJV: 10:19 In the multitude of words there wanteth not sinne: but he that refraineth his lippes, is wise.
text.NABRE: 10:19 Where words are many, sin is not wanting; but those who restrain their lips do well.

text.NRSV: The tongue of the righteous is choice silver; the mind of the wicked is of little worth.
text.Rotherham: Choice silver, is the tongue of the righteous, but, the sense of the lawless, is very small.
text.KJV: 10:20 The tongue of the iust is as choise siluer: the heart of the wicked is little worth.
text.NABRE: 10:20 Choice silver is the tongue of the just; the heart of the wicked is of little worth.

text.NRSV: The lips of the righteous feed many, but fools die for lack of sense.
text.Rotherham: The lips of the righteous, feed multitudes, but, the foolish, for lack of sense, shall die.
text.KJV: 10:21 The lippes of the righteous feed many: but fooles die for want of wisedome.
text.NABRE: 10:21 The lips of the just nourish many, but fools die for want of sense.

text.NRSV: The blessing of the Lord makes rich, and he adds no sorrow with it.
text.Rotherham: The blessing of Yahweh, itself maketh rich, and he addeth no grievance therewith.
text.KJV: 10:22 The blessing of the Lord, it maketh rich, and hee addeth no sorrow with it.
text.NABRE: 10:22 It is the LORD’s blessing that brings wealth, and no effort can substitute for it.

text.NRSV: Doing wrong is like sport to a fool, but wise conduct is pleasure to a person of understanding.
text.Rotherham: It is, mere sport to a stupid man, to commit lewdness, but, wisdom, pertaineth to a man of understanding.
text.KJV: 10:23 It is as a sport to a foole to doe mischiefe: but a man of vnderstanding hath wisedome.
text.NABRE: 10:23 Crime is the entertainment of the fool; but wisdom is for the person of understanding.

text.NRSV: What the wicked dread will come upon them, but the desire of the righteous will be granted.
text.Rotherham: The dread of the lawless one, the same, shall overtake him, but, the desire of the righteous, shall he granted.
text.KJV: 10:24 The feare of the wicked, it shall come vpon him: but the desire of the righteous shalbe granted.
text.NABRE: 10:24 What the wicked fear will befall them, but the desire of the just will be granted.

text.NRSV: When the tempest passes, the wicked are no more, but the righteous are established forever.
text.Rotherham: Like the passing away of a tempest, so the lawless one is not, but, the righteous, [hath] an age-abiding foundation.
text.KJV: 10:25 As the whirlewinde passeth, so is the wicked no more: but the righteous is an euerlasting foundation.
text.NABRE: 10:25 When the tempest passes, the wicked are no more; but the just are established forever.

text.NRSV: Like vinegar to the teeth, and smoke to the eyes, so are the lazy to those who send them.
text.Rotherham: As vinegar to the teeth, and as smoke to the eyes, so, is the sluggard, to them who send him.
text.KJV: 10:26 As vineger to the teeth, and as smoke to the eyes, so is the sluggard to them that send him.
text.NABRE: 10:26 As vinegar to the teeth, and smoke to the eyes, are sluggards to those who send them.

text.NRSV: The fear of the Lord prolongs life, but the years of the wicked will be short.
text.Rotherham: The reverence of Yahweh, addeth days, but, the years of the lawless, shall be shortened.
text.KJV: 10:27 The feare of the Lord prolongeth dayes: but the yeeres of the wicked shalbe shortened.
text.NABRE: 10:27 Fear of the LORD prolongs life, but the years of the wicked are cut short.

text.NRSV: The hope of the righteous ends in gladness, but the expectation of the wicked comes to nothing.
text.Rotherham: The hope of the righteous, shall be gladness, but, the expectation of the lawless, shall vanish.
text.KJV: 10:28 The hope of the righteous shall bee gladnesse: but the expectation of the wicked shall perish.
text.NABRE: 10:28 The hope of the just brings joy, but the expectation of the wicked perishes.

text.NRSV: The way of the Lord is a stronghold for the upright, but destruction for evildoers.
text.Rotherham: A refuge for the blameless, is the path of Yahweh, but, destruction, [awaiteth] the workers of iniquity.
text.KJV: 10:29 The way of the Lord is strength to the vpright: but destruction shall bee to the workers of iniquitie.
text.NABRE: 10:29 The LORD is a stronghold to those who walk honestly, downfall for evildoers.

text.NRSV: The righteous will never be removed, but the wicked will not inhabit the land.
text.Rotherham: The righteous, to times age-abiding, shall remain unshaken, but, the lawless, shall not inhabit the earth.
text.KJV: 10:30 The righteous shall neuer bee remooued: but the wicked shall not inhabite the earth.
text.NABRE: 10:30 The just will never be disturbed, but the wicked will not abide in the land.

text.NRSV: The mouth of the righteous brings forth wisdom, but the perverse tongue will be cut off.
text.Rotherham: The mouth of the righteous, beareth the fruit of wisdom, but, a perverse tongue, shall be cut off.
text.KJV: 10:31 The mouth of the iust bringeth foorth wisedome: but the froward tongue shalbe cut out.
text.NABRE: 10:31 The mouth of the just yields wisdom, but the perverse tongue will be cut off.

text.NRSV: The lips of the righteous know what is acceptable, but the mouth of the wicked what is perverse.
text.Rotherham: The lips of the righteous, know what is pleasing, but, the mouth of the lawless, [speaketh] perversities.
text.KJV: 10:32 The lips of the righteous know what is acceptable: but the mouth of the wicked speaketh frowardnesse.
text.NABRE: 10:32 The lips of the just know favor, but the mouth of the wicked, perversion.

text.NRSV: A false balance is an abomination to the Lord, but an accurate weight is his delight.
text.Rotherham: A deceptive balance, is an abomination to Yahweh, but, a full weight, is his delight.
text.KJV: 11:1 A false ballance is abomination to the Lord: but a iust weight is his delight.
text.NABRE: 11:1 Chapter 11 - False scales are an abomination to the LORD, but an honest weight, his delight.

text.NRSV: When pride comes, then comes disgrace; but wisdom is with the humble.
text.Rotherham: When pride cometh, then cometh contempt, but, with the modest, is wisdom.
text.KJV: 11:2 When pride commeth, then commeth shame: but with the lowly is wisedome.
text.NABRE: 11:2 When pride comes, disgrace comes; but with the humble is wisdom.

text.NRSV: The integrity of the upright guides them, but the crookedness of the treacherous destroys them.
text.Rotherham: The integrity of the upright, shall guide them, but, the crookedness of the treacherous, shall be their ruin.
text.KJV: 11:3 The integritie of the vpright shall guide them: but the peruersenesse of transgressours shall destroy them.
text.NABRE: 11:3 The honesty of the upright guides them; the faithless are ruined by their duplicity.

text.NRSV: Riches do not profit in the day of wrath, but righteousness delivers from death.
text.Rotherham: Wealth, shall not profit, in the day of wrath, but, righteousness, shall deliver from death.
text.KJV: 11:4 Riches profite not in the day of wrath: but righteousnesse deliuereth from death.
text.NABRE: 11:4 Wealth is useless on a day of wrath, but justice saves from death.

text.NRSV: The righteousness of the blameless keeps their ways straight, but the wicked fall by their own wickedness.
text.Rotherham: The righteousness of the blameless, shall smooth his way, but, by his own lawlessness, shall the lawless one, fall.
text.KJV: 11:5 The righteousnesse of the perfect shall direct his way: but the wicked shall fall by his owne wickednesse.
text.NABRE: 11:5 The justice of the honest makes their way straight, but by their wickedness the wicked fall.

text.NRSV: The righteousness of the upright delivers them, but the treacherous are taken captive by their schemes.
text.Rotherham: The righteousness of the upright, shall deliver them, but, by their own craving, shall the treacherous be captured.
text.KJV: 11:6 The righteousnesse of the vpright shall deliuer them: but transgressours shall be taken in their owne naughtinesse.
text.NABRE: 11:6 The justice of the upright saves them, but the faithless are caught in their own intrigue.

text.NRSV: When the wicked die, their hope perishes, and the expectation of the impious comes to nothing.
text.Rotherham: When the lawless man dieth, his expectation, perisheth, and, the hope of strong men, hath vanished.
text.KJV: 11:7 When a wicked man dieth, his expectation shall perish: and the hope of vniust men perisheth.
text.NABRE: 11:7 When a person dies, hope is destroyed; expectation pinned on wealth is destroyed.

text.NRSV: The righteous are delivered from trouble, and the wicked get into it instead.
text.Rotherham: The righteous man, out of distress, is delivered, then cometh a lawless man into his place.
text.KJV: 11:8 The righteous is deliuered out of trouble, and the wicked commeth in his stead.
text.NABRE: 11:8 The just are rescued from a tight spot, but the wicked fall into it instead.

text.NRSV: With their mouths the godless would destroy their neighbors, but by knowledge the righteous are delivered.
text.Rotherham: With the mouth, a profane man destroyeth his neighbour, but, through knowledge, shall righteous men be delivered.
text.KJV: 11:9 An hypocrite with his mouth destroyeth his neighbour: but through knowledge shall the iust be deliuered.
text.NABRE: 11:9 By a word the impious ruin their neighbors, but through their knowledge the just are rescued.

text.NRSV: When it goes well with the righteous, the city rejoices; and when the wicked perish, there is shouting.
text.Rotherham: When it is, well with the righteous, the city, exulteth, When the lawless perish, there is a shout of triumph.
text.KJV: 11:10 When it goeth well with the righteous, the citie reioyceth: and when the wicked perish, there is shouting.
text.NABRE: 11:10 When the just prosper, the city rejoices; when the wicked perish, there is jubilation.

text.NRSV: By the blessing of the upright a city is exalted, but it is overthrown by the mouth of the wicked.
text.Rotherham: When the upright are blessed, exalted is the city, but, by the mouth of the lawless, it is overthrown.
text.KJV: 11:11 By the blessing of the vpright the citie is exalted; but it is ouerthrowen by the mouth of the wicked.
text.NABRE: 11:11 Through the blessing of the upright the city is exalted, but through the mouth of the wicked it is overthrown.

text.NRSV: Whoever belittles another lacks sense, but one who has understanding remains silent.
text.Rotherham: He that sheweth contempt for his neighbour, lacketh sense, but, a man of understanding, observeth silence.
text.KJV: 11:12 He that is void of wisedome, despiseth his neighbour: but a man of vnderstanding holdeth his peace.
text.NABRE: 11:12 Whoever reviles a neighbor lacks sense, but the intelligent keep silent.

text.NRSV: A gossip goes about telling secrets, but one who is trustworthy in spirit keeps a confidence.
text.Rotherham: He that goeth about talebearing, revealeth a secret, but, he that is faithful in spirit, concealeth a matter.
text.KJV: 11:13 A tale-bearer reuealeth secrets: but hee that is of a faithfull spirit, concealeth the matter.
text.NABRE: 11:13 One who slanders reveals secrets, but a trustworthy person keeps a confidence.

text.NRSV: Where there is no guidance, a nation falls, but in an abundance of counselors there is safety.
text.Rotherham: With no guidance, a people will fall, but, safety, lieth in the greatness of the counsellor.
text.KJV: 11:14 Where no counsell is, the people fall: but in the multitude of counsellers there is safetie.
text.NABRE: 11:14 For lack of guidance a people falls; security lies in many counselors.

text.NRSV: To guarantee loans for a stranger brings trouble, but whoever refuses to shake hands on a guarantee is safe.
text.Rotherham: He that becometh surety for a stranger, goeth to utter ruin, but, he that hateth striking hands, is secure.
text.KJV: 11:15 Hee that is suretie for a stranger, shall smart for it: and hee that hateth suretiship, is sure.
text.NABRE: 11:15 Harm will come to anyone going surety for another, but whoever hates giving pledges is secure.

text.NRSV: A gracious woman gets honor, but she who hates virtue is a throne of disgrace. The timid become destitute, but the aggressive gain riches.
text.Rotherham: A gracious wife, obtaineth honour, but, the diligent, shall obtain wealth.
text.KJV: 11:16 A gracious woman retaineth honour: and strong men retaine riches.
text.NABRE: 11:16 A gracious woman gains esteem, and ruthless men gain wealth.

text.NRSV: Those who are kind reward themselves, but the cruel do themselves harm.
text.Rotherham: The man of lovingkindness, dealeth well with his own soul, but the cruel man, troubleth his own flesh.
text.KJV: 11:17 The mercifull man doeth good to his owne soule: but he that is cruell, troubleth his owne flesh.
text.NABRE: 11:17 Kindly people benefit themselves, but the merciless harm themselves.

text.NRSV: The wicked earn empty wages, but those who sow righteousness receive a true reward.
text.Rotherham: The lawless man, earneth the wages of falsehood, but, he that soweth righteousness, hath the reward of fidelity.
text.KJV: 11:18 The wicked worketh a deceitfull worke: but to him that soweth righteousnesse, shall be a sure reward.
text.NABRE: 11:18 The wicked make empty profits, but those who sow justice have a sure reward.

text.NRSV: Whoever is steadfast in righteousness will live, but whoever pursues evil will die.
text.Rotherham: A righteous son, [turneth] to life, but, he that pursueth wickedness, to his own death.
text.KJV: 11:19 As righteousnesse tendeth to life: so he that pursueth euill, pursueth it to his owne death.
text.NABRE: 11:19 Justice leads toward life, but pursuit of evil, toward death.

text.NRSV: Crooked minds are an abomination to the Lord, but those of blameless ways are his delight.
text.Rotherham: The abomination of Yahweh, are they of perverse heart, but, his delight, are the men of blameless life.
text.KJV: 11:20 They that are of a froward heart, are abomination to the Lord: but such as are vpright in their way, are his delight.
text.NABRE: 11:20 The crooked in heart are an abomination to the LORD, but those who walk blamelessly are his delight.

text.NRSV: Be assured, the wicked will not go unpunished, but those who are righteous will escape.
text.Rotherham: Hand to hand, the wicked man shall not be held innocent, but, the seed of the righteous, hath been delivered.
text.KJV: 11:21 Though hand ioyne in hand, the wicked shall not be vnpunished: but the seede of the righteous shall be deliuered.
text.NABRE: 11:21 Be assured, the wicked shall not go unpunished, but the offspring of the just shall escape.

text.NRSV: Like a gold ring in a pig’s snout is a beautiful woman without good sense.
text.Rotherham: As a ring of gold in the snout of a swine, is a woman of beauty who hath abandoned discretion.
text.KJV: 11:22 As a iewell of golde in a swines snowt; so is a faire woman which is without discretion.
text.NABRE: 11:22 Like a golden ring in a swine’s snout is a beautiful woman without judgment.

text.NRSV: The desire of the righteous ends only in good; the expectation of the wicked in wrath.
text.Rotherham: The desire of the righteous, is only good, the expectation of the lawless, is wrath.
text.KJV: 11:23 The desire of the righteous is onely good: but the expectation of the wicked is wrath.
text.NABRE: 11:23 The desire of the just ends only in good; the expectation of the wicked is wrath.

text.NRSV: Some give freely, yet grow all the richer; others withhold what is due, and only suffer want.
text.Rotherham: There is who scattereth, and increaseth yet more, and who withholdeth of what is due, only to come to want.
text.KJV: 11:24 There is that scattereth, and yet increaseth; and there is that withholdeth more then is meete, but it tendeth to pouertie.
text.NABRE: 11:24 One person is lavish yet grows still richer; another is too sparing, yet is the poorer.

text.NRSV: A generous person will be enriched, and one who gives water will get water.
text.Rotherham: The liberal soul, shall be enriched, and, he that refresheth, shall himself also be refreshed.
text.KJV: 11:25 The liberall soule shalbe made fat: and he that watereth, shall be watered also himselfe.
text.NABRE: 11:25 Whoever confers benefits will be amply enriched, and whoever refreshes others will be refreshed.

text.NRSV: The people curse those who hold back grain, but a blessing is on the head of those who sell it.
text.Rotherham: He that keepeth back corn, the populace will curse him, but there is, a blessing, for the head of him that selleth.
text.KJV: 11:26 Hee that withholdeth corne, the people shall curse him: but blessing shall be vpon the head of him that selleth it.
text.NABRE: 11:26 Whoever hoards grain, the people curse, but blessings are on the head of one who distributes it!

text.NRSV: Whoever diligently seeks good seeks favor, but evil comes to the one who searches for it.
text.Rotherham: He that diligently seeketh good, aimeth at favour, but, he that studieth mischief, it shall come on himself.
text.KJV: 11:27 He that diligently seeketh good, procureth fauour: but hee that seeketh mischiefe, it shall come vnto him.
text.NABRE: 11:27 Those who seek the good seek favor, but those who pursue evil will have evil come upon them.

text.NRSV: Those who trust in their riches will wither, but the righteous will flourish like green leaves.
text.Rotherham: He that trusteth in his riches, the same, shall fall, but, like the leaf, shall the righteous break forth.
text.KJV: 11:28 He that trusteth in his riches, shall fall: but the righteous shall flourish as a branch.
text.NABRE: 11:28 Those who trust in their riches will fall, but like green leaves the just will flourish.

text.NRSV: Those who trouble their households will inherit wind, and the fool will be servant to the wise.
text.Rotherham: He that troubleth his own house, shall inherit the wind, but, a servant, shall the foolish be, to the wise in heart.
text.KJV: 11:29 He that troubleth his owne house, shall inherite the winde: and the foole shall be seruant to the wise of heart.
text.NABRE: 11:29 Those who trouble their household inherit the wind, and fools become slaves to the wise of heart.

text.NRSV: The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life, but violence takes lives away.
text.Rotherham: The fruit of the righteous, is a tree of life, and, he that rescueth souls, is wise.
text.KJV: 11:30 The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life: and hee that winneth soules, is wise.
text.NABRE: 11:30 The fruit of justice is a tree of life, and one who takes lives is a sage.

text.NRSV: If the righteous are repaid on earth, how much more the wicked and the sinner!
text.Rotherham: Lo! the righteous, in the earth shall be recompensed, how much more the lawless and the sinner.
text.KJV: 11:31 Behold, the righteous shalbe recompensed in the earth: much more the wicked and the sinner.
text.NABRE: 11:31 If the just are recompensed on the earth, how much more the wicked and the sinner!

text.NRSV: Whoever loves discipline loves knowledge, but one who hates revelry is stupid.
text.Rotherham: He that loveth correction, loveth knowledge, but, he that hateth reproof, is brutish.
text.KJV: 12:1 Whoso loueth instruction, loueth knowledge: but he that hateth reproofe, is brutish.
text.NABRE: 12:1 Chapter 12 - Whoever loves discipline loves knowledge, but whoever hates reproof is stupid.

text.NRSV: The good obtain favor from the Lord, but he condemns those who devise evil.
text.Rotherham: A good man, obtaineth favour from Yahweh, but, a man of wicked devices, will he condemn.
text.KJV: 12:2 A good man obtaineth fauour of the Lord: but a man of wicked deuices will he condemne.
text.NABRE: 12:2 A good person wins favor from the LORD, but the schemer he condemns.

text.NRSV: No one finds security by wickedness, but the root of the righteous will never be moved.
text.Rotherham: A son of earth, shall not be established, by lawlessness, but, the root of the righteous, shall not be disturbed.
text.KJV: 12:3 A man shall not bee established by wickednesse: but the roote of the righteous shall not be mooued.
text.NABRE: 12:3 No one is made secure by wickedness, but the root of the just will never be disturbed.

text.NRSV: A good wife is the crown of her husband, but she who brings shame is like rottenness in his bones.
text.Rotherham: A virtuous woman, is the crown of her husband, but, a veritable decay in his bones, is she that causeth shame.
text.KJV: 12:4 A vertuous woman is a crowne to her husband: but she that maketh ashamed, is as rottennesse in his bones.
text.NABRE: 12:4 A woman of worth is the crown of her husband, but a disgraceful one is like rot in his bones.

text.NRSV: The thoughts of the righteous are just; the counsels of the wicked are treacherous.
text.Rotherham: The plans of the righteous, are just, but, the counsels of the lawless, are deceitful.
text.KJV: 12:5 The thoughts of the righteous are right: but the counsels of the wicked are deceit.
text.NABRE: 12:5 The plans of the just are right; the designs of the wicked are deceit.

text.NRSV: The words of the wicked are a deadly ambush, but the speech of the upright delivers them.
text.Rotherham: The words of the lawless, are—of lying in wait for blood, but, the mouth of the upright, shall deliver them.
text.KJV: 12:6 The words of the wicked are to lie in waite for blood: but the mouth of the vpright shall deliuer them.
text.NABRE: 12:6 The words of the wicked are a deadly ambush, but the speech of the upright saves them.

text.NRSV: The wicked are overthrown and are no more, but the house of the righteous will stand.
text.Rotherham: Overthrown are the lawless and they are not, but, the house of the righteous, shall stand.
text.KJV: 12:7 The wicked are ouerthrowen, and are not: but the house of the righteous shall stand.
text.NABRE: 12:7 Overthrow the wicked and they are no more, but the house of the just stands firm.

text.NRSV: One is commended for good sense, but a perverse mind is despised.
text.Rotherham: In proportion to his prudence, is a man, to be praised, but, the perverse in heart, shall be an object of contempt.
text.KJV: 12:8 A man shall be commended according to his wisedome: but hee that is of a peruerse heart, shall be despised.
text.NABRE: 12:8 For their good sense people are praised, but the perverse of heart are despised.

text.NRSV: Better to be despised and have a servant, than to be self-important and have no food.
text.Rotherham: Better slighted, and have a servant, than to honour oneself, and come short of bread.
text.KJV: 12:9 Hee that is despised and hath a seruant, is better then he that honoureth himselfe, and lacketh bread.
text.NABRE: 12:9 Better to be slighted and have a servant than put on airs and lack bread.

text.NRSV: The righteous know the needs of their animals, but the mercy of the wicked is cruel.
text.Rotherham: A righteous man, regardeth the desire of his beast, but, the compassions of the lawless, are cruel.
text.KJV: 12:10 A righteous man regardeth the life of his beast: but the tender mercies of the wicked are cruell.
text.NABRE: 12:10 The just take care of their livestock, but the compassion of the wicked is cruel.

text.NRSV: Those who till their land will have plenty of food, but those who follow worthless pursuits have no sense.
text.Rotherham: He that tilleth his land, shall be filled with food, but, he that runneth after worthless men, lacketh sense.
text.KJV: 12:11 Hee that tilleth his land, shall bee satisfied with bread: but he that followeth vaine persons, is void of vnderstanding.
text.NABRE: 12:11 Those who till their own land have food in plenty, but those who engage in idle pursuits lack sense.

text.NRSV: The wicked covet the proceeds of wickedness, but the root of the righteous bears fruit.
text.Rotherham: A lawless man craveth the prey of the wicked, but, the root of the righteous, is enduring.
text.KJV: 12:12 The wicked desireth the net of euill men: but the roote of the righteous yeeldeth fruit.
text.NABRE: 12:12 A wicked person desires the catch of evil people, but the root of the righteous will bear fruit.

text.NRSV: The wicked are ensnared by the transgression of their lips, but the righteous escape from trouble.
text.Rotherham: By the transgression of the lips, is the wicked man, ensnared, but the righteous, cometh out of distress.
text.KJV: 12:13 The wicked is snared by the transgression of his lippes: but the iust shall come out of trouble.
text.NABRE: 12:13 By the sin of their lips the wicked are ensnared, but the just escape from a tight spot.

text.NRSV: From the fruit of the mouth one is filled with good things, and manual labor has its reward.
text.Rotherham: Of the fruit of a man’s mouth, shall he be satisfied with good, and, the dealing of the hands of a son of earth, shall be paid back to him.
text.KJV: 12:14 A man shall bee satisfied with good by the fruit of his mouth, and the recompence of a mans hands shall bee rendred vnto him.
text.NABRE: 12:14 From the fruit of their mouths people have their fill of good, and the works of their hands come back upon them.

text.NRSV: Fools think their own way is right, but the wise listen to advice.
text.Rotherham: The way of the foolish, is right in his own eyes, but, he that hearkeneth to counsel, is wise.
text.KJV: 12:15 The way of a foole is right in his owne eyes: but he that hearkeneth vnto counsell, is wise.
text.NABRE: 12:15 The way of fools is right in their own eyes, but those who listen to advice are the wise.

text.NRSV: Fools show their anger at once, but the prudent ignore an insult.
text.Rotherham: A foolish man, on the [same] day, letteth his vexation be known, but, he that concealeth an affront, is prudent.
text.KJV: 12:16 A fooles wrath is presently knowen: but a prudent man couereth shame.
text.NABRE: 12:16 Fools immediately show their anger, but the shrewd conceal contempt.

text.NRSV: Whoever speaks the truth gives honest evidence, but a false witness speaks deceitfully.
text.Rotherham: He that whispereth faithfulness, declareth righteousness, but, a false witness, is a fraud.
text.KJV: 12:17 He that speaketh trueth, sheweth foorth righteousnesse: but a false witnesse, deceit.
text.NABRE: 12:17 Whoever speaks honestly testifies truly, but the deceitful make lying witnesses.

text.NRSV: Rash words are like sword thrusts, but the tongue of the wise brings healing.
text.Rotherham: There is who babbleth, as with thrusts of a sword, but, the tongue of the wise, hath healing.
text.KJV: 12:18 There is that speaketh like the pearcings of a sword: but the tongue of the wise is health.
text.NABRE: 12:18 The babble of some people is like sword thrusts, but the tongue of the wise is healing.

text.NRSV: Truthful lips endure forever, but a lying tongue lasts only a moment.
text.Rotherham: A lip that is faithful, shall be firm to futurity, but, only for a twinkling, is the tongue that is false.
text.KJV: 12:19 The lippe of trueth shall bee established for euer: but a lying tongue is but for a moment.
text.NABRE: 12:19 Truthful lips endure forever, the lying tongue, for only a moment.

text.NRSV: Deceit is in the mind of those who plan evil, but those who counsel peace have joy.
text.Rotherham: Deceit, is in the heart of contrivers of mischief, but, to the counsellors of peace, shall be joy.
text.KJV: 12:20 Deceit is in the heart of them that imagine euill: but to the counsellours of peace, is ioy.
text.NABRE: 12:20 Deceit is in the heart of those who plot evil, but those who counsel peace have joy.

text.NRSV: No harm happens to the righteous, but the wicked are filled with trouble.
text.Rotherham: Unseemly to the righteous, is any trouble, but, the lawless, are full of misfortune.
text.KJV: 12:21 There shall no euill happen to the iust: but the wicked shall bee filled with mischiefe.
text.NABRE: 12:21 No harm befalls the just, but the wicked are overwhelmed with misfortune.

text.NRSV: Lying lips are an abomination to the Lord, but those who act faithfully are his delight.
text.Rotherham: An abomination to Yahweh, are lips that are false, but, he that dealeth faithfully, is his delight.
text.KJV: 12:22 Lying lippes are abomination to the Lord: but they that deale truely, are his delight.
text.NABRE: 12:22 Lying lips are an abomination to the LORD, but those who are truthful, his delight.

text.NRSV: One who is clever conceals knowledge, but the mind of a fool broadcasts folly.
text.Rotherham: A prudent man, concealeth knowledge, but, the heart of dullards, proclaimeth folly.
text.KJV: 12:23 A prudent man concealeth knowledge: but the heart of fooles proclaimeth foolishnesse.
text.NABRE: 12:23 The shrewd conceal knowledge, but the hearts of fools proclaim folly.

text.NRSV: The hand of the diligent will rule, while the lazy will be put to forced labor.
text.Rotherham: The hand of the diligent, shall bear rule, but, the indolent, shall come under tribute.
text.KJV: 12:24 The hand of the diligent shall beare rule: but the slouthfull shall bee vnder tribute.
text.NABRE: 12:24 The diligent hand will govern, but sloth makes for forced labor.

text.NRSV: Anxiety weighs down the human heart, but a good word cheers it up.
text.Rotherham: When anxiety is in the heart of a man, one may bow him down, but, a good word, maketh him glad.
text.KJV: 12:25 Heauinesse in the heart of man maketh it stoope: but a good word maketh it glad.
text.NABRE: 12:25 Worry weighs down the heart, but a kind word gives it joy.

text.NRSV: The righteous at best are better than their neighbors, but the way of the wicked leads them astray.
text.Rotherham: The righteous man guideth his neighbour, but, the way of the lawless, leadeth them astray.
text.KJV: 12:26 The righteous is more excellent then his neighbour: but the way of the wicked seduceth them.
text.NABRE: 12:26 The just act as guides to their neighbors, but the way of the wicked leads them astray.

text.NRSV: The lazy do not roast their game, but the diligent possess precious wealth.
text.Rotherham: Indolence, roasteth not, his own game, but, the substance of a man, is precious, when he is diligent.
text.KJV: 12:27 The slouthfull man rosteth not that which he tooke in hunting: but the substance of a diligent man is precious.
text.NABRE: 12:27 Sloth does not catch its prey, but the wealth of the diligent is splendid.

text.NRSV: In the path of righteousness there is life, in her tread there is no death.
text.Rotherham: In the way of righteousness, is life, and, in the path thereof, immortality,
text.KJV: 12:28 In the way of righteousnesse is life, and in the path-way thereof there is no death.
text.NABRE: 12:28 In the path of justice is life, but the way of abomination leads to death.

text.NRSV: A wise child loves discipline, but a scoffer does not listen to rebuke.
text.Rotherham: A wise son, [cometh of] a father’s correction, but, a scoffer, heareth not a rebuke.
text.KJV: 13:1 A wise sonne heareth his fathers instruction: but a scorner heareth not rebuke.
text.NABRE: 13:1 Chapter 13 - A wise son loves correction, but the scoffer heeds no rebuke.

text.NRSV: From the fruit of their words good people eat good things, but the desire of the treacherous is for violence.
text.Rotherham: Of the fruit of his mouth, shall a man eat what is good, but, the soul of the treacherous, [shall be sated with] violence.
text.KJV: 13:2 A man shall eate good by the fruit of his mouth: but the soule of the transgressours, shall eate violence.
text.NABRE: 13:2 From the fruit of the mouth one enjoys good things, but from the throat of the treacherous comes violence.

text.NRSV: Those who guard their mouths preserve their lives; those who open wide their lips come to ruin.
text.Rotherham: He that watcheth his mouth, guardeth his soul, He that openeth wide his lips, [it shall be] his ruin.
text.KJV: 13:3 He that keepeth his mouth, keepeth his life: but hee that openeth wide his lips, shall haue destruction.
text.NABRE: 13:3 Those who guard their mouths preserve themselves; those who open wide their lips bring ruin.

text.NRSV: The appetite of the lazy craves, and gets nothing, while the appetite of the diligent is richly supplied.
text.Rotherham: The sluggard, desireth, but his soul hath, nothing. But, the soul of the diligent, shall be enriched.
text.KJV: 13:4 The soule of the sluggard desireth, and hath nothing: but the soule of the diligent shall be made fat.
text.NABRE: 13:4 The appetite of the sluggard craves but has nothing, but the appetite of the diligent is amply satisfied.

text.NRSV: The righteous hate falsehood, but the wicked act shamefully and disgracefully.
text.Rotherham: A word of falsehood, the righteous man, hateth, but, the lawless, causeth shame and disgrace.
text.KJV: 13:5 A righteous man hateth lying: but a wicked man is loathsome, and commeth to shame.
text.NABRE: 13:5 The just hate deceitful words, but the wicked are odious and disgraceful.

text.NRSV: Righteousness guards one whose way is upright, but sin overthrows the wicked.
text.Rotherham: Righteousness, guardeth the man of blameless way, but, lawlessness, overthroweth the sinner.
text.KJV: 13:6 Righteousnesse keepeth him that is vpright in the way: but wickednesse ouerthroweth the sinner.
text.NABRE: 13:6 Justice guards one who walks honestly, but sin leads the wicked astray.

text.NRSV: Some pretend to be rich, yet have nothing; others pretend to be poor, yet have great wealth.
text.Rotherham: There is who feigneth himself rich, yet hath nothing at all, who pleadeth poverty, yet hath great substance.
text.KJV: 13:7 There is that maketh himselfe rich, yet hath nothing: there is that maketh himselfe poore, yet hath great riches.
text.NABRE: 13:7 One acts rich but has nothing; another acts poor but has great wealth.

text.NRSV: Wealth is a ransom for a person’s life, but the poor get no threats.
text.Rotherham: The ransom of a man’s life, is his wealth, but, the poor, heareth not rebuke.
text.KJV: 13:8 The ransome of a mans life are his riches: but the poore heareth not rebuke.
text.NABRE: 13:8 People’s riches serve as ransom for their lives, but the poor do not even hear a threat.

text.NRSV: The light of the righteous rejoices, but the lamp of the wicked goes out.
text.Rotherham: The light of the righteous, rejoiceth, but, the lamp of the lawless, goeth out.
text.KJV: 13:9 The light of the righteous reioyceth: but the lampe of the wicked shall be put out.
text.NABRE: 13:9 The light of the just gives joy, but the lamp of the wicked goes out.

text.NRSV: By insolence the heedless make strife, but wisdom is with those who take advice.
text.Rotherham: Only by pride, doth one cause contention, but, with the well-advised, is wisdom.
text.KJV: 13:10 Onely by pride commeth contention: but with the well aduised is wisedome.
text.NABRE: 13:10 The stupid sow discord by their insolence, but wisdom is with those who take counsel.

text.NRSV: Wealth hastily gotten will dwindle, but those who gather little by little will increase it.
text.Rotherham: Wealth gotten by greed, diminisheth, but, he that gathereth by little, increaseth.
text.KJV: 13:11 Wealth gotten by vanitie shall be diminished: but he that gathereth by labour, shall increase.
text.NABRE: 13:11 Wealth won quickly dwindles away, but gathered little by little, it grows.

text.NRSV: Hope deferred makes the heart sick, but a desire fulfilled is a tree of life.
text.Rotherham: Hope deferred, sickeneth the heart,—but, a tree of life, is desire fulfilled.
text.KJV: 13:12 Hope deferred maketh the heart sicke: but when the desire commeth, it is a tree of life.
text.NABRE: 13:12 Hope deferred makes the heart sick, but a wish fulfilled is a tree of life.

text.NRSV: Those who despise the word bring destruction on themselves, but those who respect the commandment will be rewarded.
text.Rotherham: He that despiseth a matter, shall get pledged thereto, but, he that revereth a commandment, the same shall be recompensed.
text.KJV: 13:13 Whoso despiseth the word, shall be destroyed: but he that feareth the commaundement, shall be rewarded.
text.NABRE: 13:13 Whoever despises the word must pay for it, but whoever reveres the command will be rewarded.

text.NRSV: The teaching of the wise is a fountain of life, so that one may avoid the snares of death.
text.Rotherham: the instruction of the wise, is a well-spring of life, by departing from the snares of death.
text.KJV: 13:14 The lawe of the wise is a fountaine of life, to depart from the snares of death.
text.NABRE: 13:14 The teaching of the wise is a fountain of life, turning one from the snares of death.

text.NRSV: Good sense wins favor, but the way of the faithless is their ruin.
text.Rotherham: Sound discretion, yieldeth favour, but, the way of the treacherous, is rugged.
text.KJV: 13:15 Good vnderstanding giueth fauour: but the way of transgressours is hard.
text.NABRE: 13:15 Good sense brings favor, but the way of the faithless is their ruin.

text.NRSV: The clever do everything with knowledge, but fools display their folly.
text.Rotherham: Every prudent man, maketh use of knowledge, but, a dullard, spreadeth folly.
text.KJV: 13:16 Euery prudent man dealeth with knowledge: but a foole layeth open his folly.
text.NABRE: 13:16 The shrewd always act prudently but the foolish parade folly.

text.NRSV: A bad messenger overthrows the sender, but a faithful envoy brings healing.
text.Rotherham: A lawless messenger, falleth into mischief, but, a faithful herald, bringeth healing.
text.KJV: 13:17 A wicked messenger falleth into mischiefe: but a faithfull ambassadour is health.
text.NABRE: 13:17 A wicked messenger brings on disaster, but a trustworthy envoy is a healing remedy.

text.NRSV: Poverty and disgrace are for the one who ignores instruction, but one who heeds reproof is honored.
text.Rotherham: Poverty and contempt, are for him that neglecteth correction, but, he that regardeth reproof, shall be honoured.
text.KJV: 13:18 Pouerty and shame shall be to him that refuseth instruction: but he that regardeth reproofe, shall be honoured.
text.NABRE: 13:18 Poverty and shame befall those who let go of discipline, but those who hold on to reproof receive honor.

text.NRSV: A desire fulfilled is sweet to the soul, but to turn away from evil is an abomination to fools.
text.Rotherham: A desire fulfilled, is sweet to the soul, but it is, an abomination to the lawless, to depart from evil.
text.KJV: 13:19 The desire accomplished is sweet to the soule: but it is abomination to fooles to depart from euill.
text.NABRE: 13:19 Desire fulfilled delights the soul, but turning from evil is an abomination to fools.

text.NRSV: Whoever walks with the wise becomes wise, but the companion of fools suffers harm.
text.Rotherham: He that walketh with the wise, becometh wise, but, the friend of dullards, becometh foolish.
text.KJV: 13:20 He that walketh with wise men, shall be wise: but a companion of fooles shall be destroyed.
text.NABRE: 13:20 Walk with the wise and you become wise, but the companion of fools fares badly.

text.NRSV: Misfortune pursues sinners, but prosperity rewards the righteous.
text.Rotherham: Evil pursueth, sinners, but, unto the righteous, shall good be recompensed.
text.KJV: 13:21 Euill pursueth sinners: but to the righteous, good shall be repayd.
text.NABRE: 13:21 Misfortune pursues sinners, but the just shall be recompensed with good.

text.NRSV: The good leave an inheritance to their children’s children, but the sinner’s wealth is laid up for the righteous.
text.Rotherham: A good man, leaveth an inheritance to children’s children, but, laid up for the righteous, is the wealth of the sinner.
text.KJV: 13:22 A good man leaueth an inheritance to his childrens children: and the wealth of the sinner is layd vp for the iust.
text.NABRE: 13:22 The good leave an inheritance to their children’s children, but the wealth of the sinner is stored up for the just.

text.NRSV: The field of the poor may yield much food, but it is swept away through injustice.
text.Rotherham: Much food, is in the fallow ground of the poor, but there is that is swept away, for want of justice.
text.KJV: 13:23 Much food is in the tillage of the poore: but there is that is destroyed for want of iudgement.
text.NABRE: 13:23 The tillage of the poor yields abundant food, but possessions are swept away for lack of justice.

text.NRSV: Those who spare the rod hate their children, but those who love them are diligent to discipline them.
text.Rotherham: He that withholdeth his rod, hateth his son,—but, he that loveth him, carefully correcteth him.
text.KJV: 13:24 He that spareth his rod, hateth his sonne: but he that loueth him, chasteneth him betimes.
text.NABRE: 13:24 Whoever spares the rod hates the child, but whoever loves will apply discipline.

text.NRSV: The righteous have enough to satisfy their appetite, but the belly of the wicked is empty.
text.Rotherham: The righteous, eateth to satisfy his appetite, but, the belly of the lawless, shall want.
text.KJV: 13:25 The righteous eateth to the satisfying of his soule: but the belly of the wicked shall want.
text.NABRE: 13:25 When the just eat, their hunger is appeased; but the belly of the wicked suffers want.

text.NRSV: The wise woman builds her house, but the foolish tears it down with her own hands.
text.Rotherham: Every, wise woman, buildeth up her house, but, a foolish one, with her own hands, would break it down.
text.KJV: 14:1 Euery wise woman buildeth her house; but the foolish plucketh it downe with her hands.
text.NABRE: 14:1 Chapter 14 - Wisdom builds her house, but Folly tears hers down with her own hands.

text.NRSV: Those who walk uprightly fear the Lord, but one who is devious in conduct despises him.
text.Rotherham: He that walketh in his uprightness, is one who revereth Yahweh, but, he that is crooked in his way, is one who despiseth him.
text.KJV: 14:2 He that walketh in his vprightnesse, feareth the Lord: but he that is peruerse in his wayes, despiseth him.
text.NABRE: 14:2 Those who walk uprightly fear the LORD, but those who are devious in their ways spurn him.

text.NRSV: The talk of fools is a rod for their backs, but the lips of the wise preserve them.
text.Rotherham: In the mouth of the foolish, is a haughty rod, but, as for the lips of the wise, thou shalt give heed to them!
text.KJV: 14:3 In the mouth of the foolish is a rod of pride: but the lippes of the wise shall preserue them.
text.NABRE: 14:3 In the mouth of the fool is a rod for pride, but the lips of the wise preserve them.

text.NRSV: Where there are no oxen, the manger is clean; but abundant crops come by the strength of the ox.
text.Rotherham: Without cattle, the crib is clean, but, much increase, is in the strength of the ox.
text.KJV: 14:4 Where no Oxen are, the crib is cleane: but much increase is by the strength of the Oxe.
text.NABRE: 14:4 Where there are no oxen, the crib is clean; but abundant crops come through the strength of the bull.

text.NRSV: A faithful witness does not lie, but a false witness breathes out lies.
text.Rotherham: A faithful witness, will not deceive, but a false witness, uttereth deceitful things.
text.KJV: 14:5 A faithfull witnesse will not lye: but a false witnesse will vtter lyes.
text.NABRE: 14:5 A trustworthy witness does not lie, but one who spouts lies makes a lying witness.

text.NRSV: A scoffer seeks wisdom in vain, but knowledge is easy for one who has understanding.
text.Rotherham: A scoffer seeketh wisdom, and there is none, but, knowledge, to the discerning, is easy.
text.KJV: 14:6 A scorner seeketh wisedome, and findeth it not: but knowledge is easie vnto him that vnderstandeth.
text.NABRE: 14:6 The scoffer seeks wisdom in vain, but knowledge is easy for the intelligent.

text.NRSV: Leave the presence of a fool, for there you do not find words of knowledge.
text.Rotherham: Get thee from the presence of a man that is a dullard, when thou perceivest not the lips of knowledge.
text.KJV: 14:7 Goe from the presence of a foolish man, when thou perceiuest not in him the lippes of knowledge.
text.NABRE: 14:7 Go from the face of the fool; you get no knowledge from such lips.

text.NRSV: The wisdom of the clever is to understand their way, but the folly of fools is deceiving.
text.Rotherham: The wisdom of the prudent, is to understand his way, but, the folly of dullards, is a fraud.
text.KJV: 14:8 The wisedome of the prudent is to vnderstand his way: but the folly of fooles is deceit.
text.NABRE: 14:8 The wisdom of the shrewd enlightens their way, but the folly of fools is deceit.

text.NRSV: Fools mock at the guilt offering, but the upright enjoy God’s favor.
text.Rotherham: the foolish, scoff at guilt, but, between the upright, is good pleasure.
text.KJV: 14:9 Fooles make a mocke at sinne: but among the righteous there is fauour.
text.NABRE: 14:9 The wicked scorn a guilt offering, but the upright find acceptance.

text.NRSV: The heart knows its own bitterness, and no stranger shares its joy.
text.Rotherham: The heart, knoweth its own bitterness, and, in its joy, no stranger shareth.
text.KJV: 14:10 The heart knoweth his owne bitternesse; and a stranger doth not intermeddle with his ioy.
text.NABRE: 14:10 The heart knows its own bitterness, and its joy no stranger shares.

text.NRSV: The house of the wicked is destroyed, but the tent of the upright flourishes.
text.Rotherham: The house of the lawless, shall be destroyed, but, the tent of the upright, shall flourish.
text.KJV: 14:11 The house of the wicked shall bee ouerthrowen: but the tabernacle of the vpright shall flourish.
text.NABRE: 14:11 The house of the wicked will be destroyed, but the tent of the upright will flourish.

text.NRSV: There is a way that seems right to a person, but its end is the way to death.
text.Rotherham: There is a way that enticeth a man, but, at the latter end thereof, are the ways of death!
text.KJV: 14:12 There is a way which seemeth right vnto a man: but the end thereof are the wayes of death.
text.NABRE: 14:12 Sometimes a way seems right, but the end of it leads to death!

text.NRSV: Even in laughter the heart is sad, and the end of joy is grief.
text.Rotherham: Even, in laughter, the heart may be in pain, and, the latter end of gladness, be grief.
text.KJV: 14:13 Euen in laughter the heart is sorrowfull; and the end of that mirth is heauinesse.
text.NABRE: 14:13 Even in laughter the heart may be sad, and the end of joy may be sorrow.

text.NRSV: The perverse get what their ways deserve, and the good, what their deeds deserve.
text.Rotherham: With his own ways, shall be filled the backslider in heart, and, from himself, shall a good man [be satisfied] .
text.KJV: 14:14 The backslider in heart shall be filled with his owne wayes: and a good man shall be satisfied from himselfe.
text.NABRE: 14:14 From their own ways turncoats are sated, from their own actions, the loyal.

text.NRSV: The simple believe everything, but the clever consider their steps.
text.Rotherham: The simple, believeth every word, but, the prudent, looketh well to his going.
text.KJV: 14:15 The simple beleeueth euery word: but the prudent man looketh well to his going.
text.NABRE: 14:15 The naive believe everything, but the shrewd watch their steps.

text.NRSV: The wise are cautious and turn away from evil, but fools throw off restraint and are careless.
text.Rotherham: A wise man, feareth, and avoideth evil, but, a dullard, is haughty and confident.
text.KJV: 14:16 A wise man feareth, and departeth from euill: but the foole rageth, and is confident.
text.NABRE: 14:16 The wise person is cautious and turns from evil; the fool is reckless and gets embroiled.

text.NRSV: Those who are quick-tempered help folly, and those who scheme are hated.
text.Rotherham: The impatient, worketh folly, and, a man of wicked devices, is hated.
text.KJV: 14:17 Hee that is soone angry, dealeth foolishly: and a man of wicked deuices is hated.
text.NABRE: 14:17 The quick-tempered make fools of themselves, and schemers are hated.

text.NRSV: The simple are adorned with folly, but the clever are crowned with knowledge.
text.Rotherham: The simple inherit folly, but, the shrewd, crowneth himself with knowledge.
text.KJV: 14:18 The simple inherite folly: but the prudent are crowned with knowledge.
text.NABRE: 14:18 The simple have folly as an adornment, but the shrewd wear knowledge as a crown.

text.NRSV: The evil bow down before the good, the wicked at the gates of the righteous.
text.Rotherham: Abased are the wicked, before the good, and, the lawless, at the gates of the righteous.
text.KJV: 14:19 The euill bowe before the good: and the wicked at the gates of the righteous.
text.NABRE: 14:19 The malicious bow down before the good, and the wicked, at the gates of the just.

text.NRSV: The poor are disliked even by their neighbors, but the rich have many friends.
text.Rotherham: Even by his neighbour, is a poor man hated, but, the lovers of the rich man, are many.
text.KJV: 14:20 The poore is hated euen of his owne neighbour: but the rich hath many friends.
text.NABRE: 14:20 Even by their neighbors the poor are despised, but a rich person’s friends are many.

text.NRSV: Those who despise their neighbors are sinners, but happy are those who are kind to the poor.
text.Rotherham: He that sheweth contempt for his neighbour, sinneth, but, he that sheweth favour to the afflicted, how happy is he!
text.KJV: 14:21 He that despiseth his neighbour, sinneth: but he that hath mercy on the poore, happy is he.
text.NABRE: 14:21 Whoever despises the hungry comes up short, but happy the one who is kind to the poor!

text.NRSV: Do they not err who plan evil? Those who plan good find loyalty and faithfulness.
text.Rotherham: Shall they not, become vagrants, who devise evil? but, lovingkindness and faithfulness, [be to] them who devise good.
text.KJV: 14:22 Doe they not erre that deuise euil? but mercy and trueth shall be to them that deuise good.
text.NABRE: 14:22 Do not those who plan evil go astray? But those who plan good win steadfast loyalty.

text.NRSV: In all toil there is profit, but mere talk leads only to poverty.
text.Rotherham: By all labour, there will be abundance, but, the talk of the lips, tendeth only to want.
text.KJV: 14:23 In all labour there is profit: but the talke of the lippes tendeth onely to penury.
text.NABRE: 14:23 In all labor there is profit, but mere talk tends only to loss.

text.NRSV: The crown of the wise is their wisdom, but folly is the garland of fools.
text.Rotherham: the crown of the wise, is their wealth, but, the folly of dullards, is, folly.
text.KJV: 14:24 The crowne of the wise is their riches: but the foolishnesse of fooles is folly.
text.NABRE: 14:24 The crown of the wise is wealth; the diadem of fools is folly.

text.NRSV: A truthful witness saves lives, but one who utters lies is a betrayer.
text.Rotherham: A deliverer of souls, is a faithful witness, but, he that uttereth falsehoods, is a fraud.
text.KJV: 14:25 A true witnesse deliuereth soules: but a deceitfull witnesse speaketh lyes.
text.NABRE: 14:25 The truthful witness saves lives, but whoever utters lies is a betrayer.

text.NRSV: In the fear of the Lord one has strong confidence, and one’s children will have a refuge.
text.Rotherham: In the reverence of Yahweh, is strong security, and, his children, shall have a place of refuge.
text.KJV: 14:26 In the feare of the Lord is strong confidence: and his children shall haue a place of refuge.
text.NABRE: 14:26 The fear of the LORD is a strong defense, a refuge even for one’s children.

text.NRSV: The fear of the Lord is a fountain of life, so that one may avoid the snares of death.
text.Rotherham: The reverence of Yahweh, is a well-spring of life, that a man may avoid the snares of death.
text.KJV: 14:27 The feare of the Lord, is a fountaine of life, to depart from the snares of death.
text.NABRE: 14:27 The fear of the LORD is a fountain of life, turning one from the snares of death.

text.NRSV: The glory of a king is a multitude of people; without people a prince is ruined.
text.Rotherham: In the multitude of a people, is the majesty of the king, but, in the ceasing of population, is the ruin of the prince.
text.KJV: 14:28 In the multitude of people is the kings honour: but in the want of people is the destruction of the prince.
text.NABRE: 14:28 A multitude of subjects is the glory of the king; but if his people are few, a prince is ruined.

text.NRSV: Whoever is slow to anger has great understanding, but one who has a hasty temper exalts folly.
text.Rotherham: One slow to anger, hath great understanding, but, the hasty in spirit, exalteth folly.
text.KJV: 14:29 Hee that is slow to wrath, is of great vnderstanding: but hee that is hasty of spirit, exalteth folly.
