text.NABRE: 14:29 Long-suffering results in great wisdom; a short temper raises folly high.

text.NRSV: A tranquil mind gives life to the flesh, but passion makes the bones rot.
text.Rotherham: The life of the whole body, is a tranquil mind, but, a decay of the bones, is jealousy.
text.KJV: 14:30 A sound heart, is the life of the flesh: but enuie, the rottennesse of the bones.
text.NABRE: 14:30 A tranquil mind gives life to the body, but jealousy rots the bones.

text.NRSV: Those who oppress the poor insult their Maker, but those who are kind to the needy honor him.
text.Rotherham: The oppressor of the poor, hath reproached his Maker, but he that sheweth favour to the needy, is one who, holdeth Him in honour.
text.KJV: 14:31 Hee that oppresseth the poore, reprocheth his Maker: but hee that honoureth him, hath mercy on the poore.
text.NABRE: 14:31 Those who oppress the poor revile their Maker, but those who are kind to the needy honor him.

text.NRSV: The wicked are overthrown by their evildoing, but the righteous find a refuge in their integrity.
text.Rotherham: By his own wickedness, shall the lawless man, be thrust down, but the righteous, seeketh refuge in his integrity.
text.KJV: 14:32 The wicked is driuen away in his wickednes: but the righteous hath hope in his death.
text.NABRE: 14:32 The wicked are overthrown by their wickedness, but the just find a refuge in their integrity.

text.NRSV: Wisdom settles in the mind of one who has understanding, but it is not known in the heart of fools.
text.Rotherham: In the heart of the intelligent, reposeth wisdom, but, in the midst of dullards, it maketh itself known.
text.KJV: 14:33 Wisedome resteth in the heart of him that hath vnderstanding: but that which is in the midst of fooles, is made knowen.
text.NABRE: 14:33 Wisdom can remain silent in the discerning heart, but among fools she must make herself known.

text.NRSV: Righteousness exalts a nation, but sin is a reproach to any people.
text.Rotherham: Righteousness, exalteth a nation, but, a reproach to any people, is sin.
text.KJV: 14:34 Righteousnes exalteth a nation: but sinne is a reproch to any people.
text.NABRE: 14:34 Justice exalts a nation, but sin is a people’s disgrace.

text.NRSV: A king favors a servant who acts wisely, but his anger falls on one who acts shamefully.
text.Rotherham: The good-pleasure of a king, is due to a servant who is discreet, but, his indignation, shall be against him that bringeth shame.
text.KJV: 14:35 The Kings fauour is toward a wise seruant: but his wrath, is against him that causeth shame.
text.NABRE: 14:35 The king favors the skillful servant, but the shameless one incurs his wrath.

text.NRSV: A soft answer turns away wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger.
text.Rotherham: A soft answer, turneth away wrath, but a cutting word raiseth anger.
text.KJV: 15:1 A soft answere turneth away wrath: but grieuous words stirre vp anger.
text.NABRE: 15:1 Chapter 15 - A mild answer turns back wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger.

text.NRSV: The tongue of the wise dispenses knowledge, but the mouths of fools pour out folly.
text.Rotherham: The tongue of the wise, adorneth knowledge,—but, the mouth of dullards, belcheth out folly.
text.KJV: 15:2 The tongue of the wise, vseth knowledge aright: but the mouth of fooles, powreth out foolishnes.
text.NABRE: 15:2 The tongue of the wise pours out knowledge, but the mouth of fools spews folly.

text.NRSV: The eyes of the Lord are in every place, keeping watch on the evil and the good.
text.Rotherham: In every place, are the eyes of Yahweh, observing the evil and the good.
text.KJV: 15:3 The eyes of the Lord are in euery place, beholding the euill & the good.
text.NABRE: 15:3 The eyes of the LORD are in every place, keeping watch on the evil and the good.

text.NRSV: A gentle tongue is a tree of life, but perverseness in it breaks the spirit.
text.Rotherham: Gentleness of the tongue, is a tree of life, but, crookedness therein, a grievous wound in the spirit.
text.KJV: 15:4 A wholesome tongue is a tree of life: but peruersnesse therein is a breach in the spirit.
text.NABRE: 15:4 A soothing tongue is a tree of life, but a perverse one breaks the spirit.

text.NRSV: A fool despises a father’s instruction, but the one who heeds admonition is prudent.
text.Rotherham: A foolish [son], spurneth the correction of his father, but, he that heedeth a reproof, sheweth prudence.
text.KJV: 15:5 A foole despiseth his fathers instruction: but hee that regardeth reproofe, is prudent.
text.NABRE: 15:5 The fool spurns a father’s instruction, but whoever heeds reproof is prudent.

text.NRSV: In the house of the righteous there is much treasure, but trouble befalls the income of the wicked.
text.Rotherham: The household of the righteous man, is a great treasure, but, in the increase of the lawless, is disturbance.
text.KJV: 15:6 In the house of the righteous is much treasure: but in the reuenues of the wicked is trouble.
text.NABRE: 15:6 In the house of the just there are ample resources, but the harvest of the wicked is in peril.

text.NRSV: The lips of the wise spread knowledge; not so the minds of fools.
text.Rotherham: The lips of the wise, scatter abroad knowledge, but, the heart of dullards, is not right.
text.KJV: 15:7 The lippes of the wise disperse knowledge: but the heart of the foolish, doeth not so.
text.NABRE: 15:7 The lips of the wise spread knowledge, but the heart of fools is not steadfast.

text.NRSV: The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination to the Lord, but the prayer of the upright is his delight.
text.Rotherham: The sacrifice of lawless men, is an abomination to Yahweh, but, the prayer of the upright, is his delight.
text.KJV: 15:8 The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination to the Lord: but the prayer of the vpright is his delight.
text.NABRE: 15:8 The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination to the LORD, but the prayer of the upright is his delight.

text.NRSV: The way of the wicked is an abomination to the Lord, but he loves the one who pursues righteousness.
text.Rotherham: An abomination to Yahweh, is the way of the lawless, but, the pursuer of righteousness, he loveth.
text.KJV: 15:9 The way of the wicked is an abomination vnto the Lord: but he loueth him that followeth after righteousnes.
text.NABRE: 15:9 The way of the wicked is an abomination to the LORD, but he loves one who pursues justice.

text.NRSV: There is severe discipline for one who forsakes the way, but one who hates a rebuke will die.
text.Rotherham: There is, grievous correction, for him that forsaketh the right path, He that hateth rebuke, shall die.
text.KJV: 15:10 Correction is grieuous vnto him that forsaketh the way: and he that hateth reproofe, shall die.
text.NABRE: 15:10 Discipline seems bad to those going astray; one who hates reproof will die.

text.NRSV: Sheol and Abaddon lie open before the Lord; how much more human hearts!
text.Rotherham: Hades and destruction, are before Yahweh, how much more then, the hearts of the sons of men.
text.KJV: 15:11 Hell and destruction are before the Lord: how much more then, the hearts of the children of men?
text.NABRE: 15:11 Sheol and Abaddon lie open before the LORD; how much more the hearts of mortals!

text.NRSV: Scoffers do not like to be rebuked; they will not go to the wise.
text.Rotherham: A scoffer, loveth not, one who reproveth him, unto the wise, doth he not go.
text.KJV: 15:12 A scorner loueth not one that reproueth him: neither will he goe vnto the wise.
text.NABRE: 15:12 Scoffers do not love reproof; to the wise they will not go.

text.NRSV: A glad heart makes a cheerful countenance, but by sorrow of heart the spirit is broken.
text.Rotherham: A joyful heart, maketh a pleasing countenance,—but, in sorrow of heart, is a stricken spirit.
text.KJV: 15:13 A merry heart maketh a cheerefull countenance: but by sorrow of the heart, the spirit is broken.
text.NABRE: 15:13 A glad heart lights up the face, but an anguished heart breaks the spirit.

text.NRSV: The mind of one who has understanding seeks knowledge, but the mouths of fools feed on folly.
text.Rotherham: The heart of the intelligent, seeketh knowledge, but, the mouth of dullards, feedeth on folly.
text.KJV: 15:14 The heart of him that hath vnderstanding, seeketh knowledge: but the mouth of fooles feedeth on foolishnesse.
text.NABRE: 15:14 The discerning heart seeks knowledge, but the mouth of fools feeds on folly.

text.NRSV: All the days of the poor are hard, but a cheerful heart has a continual feast.
text.Rotherham: All the days of the afflicted, are sorrowful, but, a cheerful heart, is a continual banquet.
text.KJV: 15:15 All the dayes of the afflicted are euill: but he that is of a merry heart, hath a continuall feast.
text.NABRE: 15:15 All the days of the poor are evil, but a good heart is a continual feast.

text.NRSV: Better is a little with the fear of the Lord than great treasure and trouble with it.
text.Rotherham: Better is a little, with the reverence of Yahweh, than great treasure, and disquietude therewith.
text.KJV: 15:16 Better is little with the feare of the Lord, then great treasure, and trouble therewith.
text.NABRE: 15:16 Better a little with fear of the LORD than a great fortune with anxiety.

text.NRSV: Better is a dinner of vegetables where love is than a fatted ox and hatred with it.
text.Rotherham: Better is an allowance of herbs, and love, there, than a fatted ox, and hatred therewith.
text.KJV: 15:17 Better is a dinner of herbes where loue is, then a stalled oxe, and hatred therewith.
text.NABRE: 15:17 Better a dish of herbs where love is than a fatted ox and hatred with it.

text.NRSV: Those who are hot-tempered stir up strife, but those who are slow to anger calm contention.
text.Rotherham: A wrathful man, stirreth up strife, but, he that is slow to anger, calmeth contention.
text.KJV: 15:18 A wrathfull man stirreth vp strife: but he that is slow to anger, appeaseth strife.
text.NABRE: 15:18 The ill-tempered stir up strife, but the patient settle disputes.

text.NRSV: The way of the lazy is overgrown with thorns, but the path of the upright is a level highway.
text.Rotherham: The way of the sluggard, is like a thorn hedge, but, the path of the upright, is a raised road.
text.KJV: 15:19 The way of the slouthfull man is as an hedge of thornes: but the way of the righteous is made plaine.
text.NABRE: 15:19 The way of the sluggard is like a thorn hedge, but the path of the diligent is a highway.

text.NRSV: A wise child makes a glad father, but the foolish despise their mothers.
text.Rotherham: A wise son, maketh a glad father,—but, a dullard, despiseth his own mother.
text.KJV: 15:20 A wise sonne maketh a glad father: but a foolish man despiseth his mother.
text.NABRE: 15:20 A wise son gives his father joy, but a fool despises his mother.

text.NRSV: Folly is a joy to one who has no sense, but a person of understanding walks a straight course.
text.Rotherham: Folly, is joy to him that lacketh sense,—but, a man of understanding, taketh a straight course.
text.KJV: 15:21 Folly is ioy to him that is destitute of wisedome: but a man of vnderstanding walketh vprightly.
text.NABRE: 15:21 Folly is joy to the senseless, but the person of understanding goes the straight way.

text.NRSV: Without counsel, plans go wrong, but with many advisers they succeed.
text.Rotherham: Plans, are frustrated, for lack of consultation, but, by the multitude of counsellors, shall counsel, be established.
text.KJV: 15:22 Without counsell, purposes are disappointed: but in the multitude of counsellours they are established.
text.NABRE: 15:22 Plans fail when there is no counsel, but they succeed when advisers are many.

text.NRSV: To make an apt answer is a joy to anyone, and a word in season, how good it is!
text.Rotherham: A man hath joy, in the answer of his own mouth, and, a word in its season,—how good!
text.KJV: 15:23 A man hath ioy by the answere of his mouth: and a word spoken in due season, how good is it?
text.NABRE: 15:23 One has joy from an apt response; a word in season, how good it is!

text.NRSV: For the wise the path of life leads upward, so that they may avoid Sheol below.
text.Rotherham: The way of life, is upwards to the prudent, that he may depart from hades beneath.
text.KJV: 15:24 The way of life is aboue to the wise, that he may depart from hell beneath.
text.NABRE: 15:24 The path of life leads upward for the prudent, turning them from Sheol below.

text.NRSV: The Lord tears down the house of the proud, but maintains the widow’s boundaries.
text.Rotherham: The house of proud men, will Yahweh tear down, but he will maintain the boundary of the widow.
text.KJV: 15:25 The Lord will destroy the house of the proud: but he will establish the border of the widow.
text.NABRE: 15:25 The LORD pulls down the house of the proud, but preserves intact the widow’s landmark.

text.NRSV: Evil plans are an abomination to the Lord, but gracious words are pure.
text.Rotherham: An abomination to Yahweh, are the plottings of the wicked, but, with the pure, are sayings of sweetness.
text.KJV: 15:26 The thoughts of the wicked are an abomination to the Lord: but the wordes of the pure, are pleasant words.
text.NABRE: 15:26 The schemes of the wicked are an abomination to the LORD, but gracious words are pure.

text.NRSV: Those who are greedy for unjust gain make trouble for their households, but those who hate bribes will live.
text.Rotherham: He, troubleth his own house, who graspeth with greed, but, he that hateth gifts, shall live.
text.KJV: 15:27 Hee that is greedy of gaine, troubleth his owne house: but he that hateth gifts, shall liue.
text.NABRE: 15:27 The greedy tear down their own house, but those who hate bribes will live.

text.NRSV: The mind of the righteous ponders how to answer, but the mouth of the wicked pours out evil.
text.Rotherham: The heart of the righteous, studieth to answer, but, the mouth of the lawless, belcheth out mischiefs.
text.KJV: 15:28 The heart of the righteous studieth to answere: but the mouth of the wicked, powreth out euil things.
text.NABRE: 15:28 The heart of the just ponders a response, but the mouth of the wicked spews evil.

text.NRSV: The Lord is far from the wicked, but he hears the prayer of the righteous.
text.Rotherham: Far off is Yahweh, from the lawless, but, the prayer of the righteous, will he hear.
text.KJV: 15:29 The Lord is farre from the wicked: but hee heareth the prayer of the righteous.
text.NABRE: 15:29 The LORD is far from the wicked, but hears the prayer of the just.

text.NRSV: The light of the eyes rejoices the heart, and good news refreshes the body.
text.Rotherham: The sparkling of bright eyes, rejoiceth the heart, Good news, giveth marrow to the bones.
text.KJV: 15:30 The light of the eyes reioyceth the heart: and a good report maketh the bones fat.
text.NABRE: 15:30 A cheerful glance brings joy to the heart; good news invigorates the bones.

text.NRSV: The ear that heeds wholesome admonition will lodge among the wise.
text.Rotherham: The ear that heareth the reproof which giveth life, in the midst of the wise, shall remain.
text.KJV: 15:31 The eare that heareth the reproofe of life, abideth among the wise.
text.NABRE: 15:31 The ear that listens to salutary reproof is at home among the wise.

text.NRSV: Those who ignore instruction despise themselves, but those who heed admonition gain understanding.
text.Rotherham: He that declineth correction, despiseth his own soul, but, he that heareth reproof, gaineth sense.
text.KJV: 15:32 He that refuseth instruction, despiseth his owne soule: but he that heareth reproofe, getteth vnderstanding.
text.NABRE: 15:32 Those who disregard discipline hate themselves, but those who heed reproof acquire understanding.

text.NRSV: The fear of the Lord is instruction in wisdom, and humility goes before honor.
text.Rotherham: The reverence of Yahweh, is the correction of wisdom, and, before honour, is humility.
text.KJV: 15:33 The feare of the Lord is the instruction of wisedome; and before honour is humilitie.
text.NABRE: 15:33 The fear of the LORD is training for wisdom, and humility goes before honors.

text.NRSV: The plans of the mind belong to mortals, but the answer of the tongue is from the Lord.
text.Rotherham: To man, belong the preparations of the heart, but, from Yahweh, cometh the answer of the tongue.
text.KJV: 16:1 The preparations of the heart in man, and the answere of the tongue, is from the Lord.
text.NABRE: 16:1 Chapter 16 - Plans are made in human hearts, but from the LORD comes the tongue’s response.

text.NRSV: All one’s ways may be pure in one’s own eyes, but the Lord weighs the spirit.
text.Rotherham: All the ways of a man, [may be] pure in his own eyes, but, he that testeth spirits, is Yahweh.
text.KJV: 16:2 All the wayes of a man are cleane in his owne eyes: but the Lord weigheth the spirits.
text.NABRE: 16:2 All one’s ways are pure in one’s own eyes, but the measurer of motives is the LORD.

text.NRSV: Commit your work to the Lord, and your plans will be established.
text.Rotherham: Roll, upon Yahweh, thy doings, that thy plans, may be established.
text.KJV: 16:3 Commit thy workes vnto the Lord, and thy thoughts shalbe established.
text.NABRE: 16:3 Entrust your works to the LORD, and your plans will succeed.

text.NRSV: The Lord has made everything for its purpose, even the wicked for the day of trouble.
text.Rotherham: Everything, hath Yahweh made for its own purpose, yea, even the lawless one, for the day of calamity.
text.KJV: 16:4 The Lord hath made all things for himselfe: yea, euen the wicked for the day of euill.
text.NABRE: 16:4 The LORD has made everything for a purpose, even the wicked for the evil day.

text.NRSV: All those who are arrogant are an abomination to the Lord; be assured, they will not go unpunished.
text.Rotherham: An abomination to Yahweh, is every one who is haughty in heart, hand to hand, he shall not be held innocent.
text.KJV: 16:5 Euery one that is proud in heart, is an abomination to the Lord: though hand ioyne in hand, he shall not be vnpunished.
text.NABRE: 16:5 Every proud heart is an abomination to the LORD; be assured that none will go unpunished.

text.NRSV: By loyalty and faithfulness iniquity is atoned for, and by the fear of the Lord one avoids evil.
text.Rotherham: By lovingkindness and fidelity, shall iniquity be covered, and, in the revering of Yahweh, is a turning away from wrong.
text.KJV: 16:6 By mercy and trueth iniquitie is purged: and by the feare of the Lord, men depart from euill.
text.NABRE: 16:6 By steadfast loyalty guilt is expiated, and by the fear of the LORD evil is avoided.

text.NRSV: When the ways of people please the Lord, he causes even their enemies to be at peace with them.
text.Rotherham: When, acceptable to Yahweh, are the ways of a man, even his enemies, doth he cause to make peace with him.
text.KJV: 16:7 When a mans wayes please the Lord, he maketh euen his enemies to be at peace with him.
text.NABRE: 16:7 When the LORD is pleased with someone’s ways, he makes even enemies be at peace with them.

text.NRSV: Better is a little with righteousness than large income with injustice.
text.Rotherham: Better a little with righteousness, than large revenues, without justice.
text.KJV: 16:8 Better is a little with righteousnesse, then great reuenewes without right.
text.NABRE: 16:8 Better a little with justice, than a large income with injustice.

text.NRSV: The human mind plans the way, but the Lord directs the steps.
text.Rotherham: A man’s heart, deviseth his way, but, Yahweh, directeth his steps.
text.KJV: 16:9 A mans heart deuiseth his way: but the Lord directeth his steps.
text.NABRE: 16:9 The human heart plans the way, but the LORD directs the steps.

text.NRSV: Inspired decisions are on the lips of a king; his mouth does not sin in judgment.
text.Rotherham: An oracle, is on the lips of a king, in giving sentence, his mouth must not be unfaithful.
text.KJV: 16:10 A diuine sentence is in the lips of the king: his mouth transgresseth not in iudgement.
text.NABRE: 16:10 An oracle is upon the king’s lips, no judgment of his mouth is false.

text.NRSV: Honest balances and scales are the Lord’s; all the weights in the bag are his work.
text.Rotherham: The balance and scales of justice, belong to Yahweh, and, his handiwork, are all the weights of the bag.
text.KJV: 16:11 A iust weight and ballance are the Lords: all the weights of the bagge are his worke.
text.NABRE: 16:11 Balance and scales belong to the LORD; every weight in the sack is his concern.

text.NRSV: It is an abomination to kings to do evil, for the throne is established by righteousness.
text.Rotherham: The abomination of kings, is to work lawlessness, for, by righteousness, is established a throne.
text.KJV: 16:12 It is an abomination to kings to commit wickednesse: for the throne is established by righteousnesse.
text.NABRE: 16:12 Wrongdoing is an abomination to kings, for by justice the throne endures.

text.NRSV: Righteous lips are the delight of a king, and he loves those who speak what is right.
text.Rotherham: The delight of kings, are lips of righteousness,—and, the words of uprightness, he loveth.
text.KJV: 16:13 Righteous lips are the delight of kings: and they loue him that speaketh right.
text.NABRE: 16:13 The king takes delight in honest lips, and whoever speaks what is right he loves.

text.NRSV: A king’s wrath is a messenger of death, and whoever is wise will appease it.
text.Rotherham: The wrath of a king, [meaneth] messengers of death, but, a man who is wise, will appease it.
text.KJV: 16:14 The wrath of a king is as messengers of death: but a wise man will pacifie it.
text.NABRE: 16:14 The king’s wrath is a messenger of death, but a wise person can pacify it.

text.NRSV: In the light of a king’s face there is life, and his favor is like the clouds that bring the spring rain.
text.Rotherham: In the light of a king’s countenance, is life, and, his good-pleasure, is like the cloud of harvest-rain.
text.KJV: 16:15 In the light of the kings countenance is life, and his fauour is as a cloude of the latter raine.
text.NABRE: 16:15 A king’s smile means life, and his favor is like a rain cloud in spring.

text.NRSV: How much better to get wisdom than gold! To get insight is to be chosen rather than silver.
text.Rotherham: To acquire wisdom, how much better than gold! and, to get hold of understanding, more choice than silver!
text.KJV: 16:16 How much better is it to get wisedome, then gold? and to get vnderstanding, rather to be chosen then siluer?
text.NABRE: 16:16 How much better to get wisdom than gold! To get understanding is preferable to silver.

text.NRSV: The highway of the upright avoids evil; those who guard their way preserve their lives.
text.Rotherham: The highway of the upright, is to avoid evil, He preserveth his soul, who guardeth his way.
text.KJV: 16:17 The high way of the vpright is to depart from euill: hee that keepeth his way, preserueth his soule.
text.NABRE: 16:17 The path of the upright leads away from misfortune; those who attend to their way guard their lives.

text.NRSV: Pride goes before destruction, and a haughty spirit before a fall.
text.Rotherham: Before grievous injury, pride! and, before a fall, haughtiness of spirit!
text.KJV: 16:18 Pride goeth before destruction: and an hautie spirit before a fall.
text.NABRE: 16:18 Pride goes before disaster, and a haughty spirit before a fall.

text.NRSV: It is better to be of a lowly spirit among the poor than to divide the spoil with the proud.
text.Rotherham: Better is lowliness of spirit, with the patient, than a portion of spoil, with the proud.
text.KJV: 16:19 Better it is to be of an humble spirit with the lowly, then to diuide the spoile with the proud.
text.NABRE: 16:19 It is better to be humble with the poor than to share plunder with the proud.

text.NRSV: Those who are attentive to a matter will prosper, and happy are those who trust in the Lord.
text.Rotherham: He that showeth discretion concerning a matter, shall find good, and, he that trusteth in Yahweh, how happy is he!
text.KJV: 16:20 He that handleth a matter wisely, shall finde good: and who so trusteth in the Lord, happy is hee.
text.NABRE: 16:20 Whoever ponders a matter will be successful; happy the one who trusts in the LORD!

text.NRSV: The wise of heart is called perceptive, and pleasant speech increases persuasiveness.
text.Rotherham: The wise in heart, shall be called intelligent, and, sweetness of lips, increaseth persuasiveness.
text.KJV: 16:21 The wise in heart shall be called prudent; and the sweetnesse of the lips increaseth learning.
text.NABRE: 16:21 The wise of heart is esteemed for discernment, and pleasing speech gains a reputation for learning.

text.NRSV: Wisdom is a fountain of life to one who has it, but folly is the punishment of fools.
text.Rotherham: A well-spring of life, is discretion to its owner, but, the correction of the foolish, is folly.
text.KJV: 16:22 Understanding is a well-spring of life vnto him that hath it: but the instruction of fooles is folly.
text.NABRE: 16:22 Good sense is a fountain of life to those who have it, but folly is the training of fools.

text.NRSV: The mind of the wise makes their speech judicious, and adds persuasiveness to their lips.
text.Rotherham: The heart of the wise, giveth discretion to his mouth, and, upon his lips, increaseth persuasiveness.
text.KJV: 16:23 The heart of the wise teacheth his mouth, and addeth learning to his lippes.
text.NABRE: 16:23 The heart of the wise makes for eloquent speech, and increases the learning on their lips.

text.NRSV: Pleasant words are like a honeycomb, sweetness to the soul and health to the body.
text.Rotherham: A comb of honey, are pleasant words, sweet to the taste and healing to the bone.
text.KJV: 16:24 Pleasant words are as an honycombe, sweete to the soule, and health to the bones.
text.NABRE: 16:24 Pleasing words are a honeycomb, sweet to the taste and invigorating to the bones.

text.NRSV: There is a way that seems right to a person, but its end is the way to death.
text.Rotherham: There is a way that enticeth a man, but, at the latter end thereof, are the ways of death.
text.KJV: 16:25 There is a way that seemeth right vnto a man; but the end thereof are the wayes of death.
text.NABRE: 16:25 Sometimes a way seems right, but the end of it leads to death!

text.NRSV: The appetite of workers works for them; their hunger urges them on.
text.Rotherham: The appetite of the toiler, hath toiled for him, for his mouth, hath urged him on.
text.KJV: 16:26 Hee that laboureth, laboureth for himselfe; for his mouth craueth it of him.
text.NABRE: 16:26 The appetite of workers works for them, for their mouths urge them on.

text.NRSV: Scoundrels concoct evil, and their speech is like a scorching fire.
text.Rotherham: An abandoned man, diggeth up mischief,—and, upon his lips, as it were a fire is scorching.
text.KJV: 16:27 An vngodly man diggeth vp euill: and in his lips there is as a burning fire.
text.NABRE: 16:27 Scoundrels are a furnace of evil, and their lips are like a scorching fire.

text.NRSV: A perverse person spreads strife, and a whisperer separates close friends.
text.Rotherham: A perverse man, sendeth forth strife, and, a tattler, separateth intimate friends.
text.KJV: 16:28 A froward man soweth strife; & a whisperer separateth chiefe friends.
text.NABRE: 16:28 Perverse speech sows discord, and talebearing separates bosom friends.

text.NRSV: The violent entice their neighbors, and lead them in a way that is not good.
text.Rotherham: A ruthless man, enticeth his neighbour, and leadeth him in a way, not good.
text.KJV: 16:29 A violent man enticeth his neighbour, and leadeth him into the way that is not good.
text.NABRE: 16:29 The violent deceive their neighbors, and lead them into a way that is not good.

text.NRSV: One who winks the eyes plans perverse things; one who compresses the lips brings evil to pass.
text.Rotherham: Closing his eyes, to devise perverse things, biting his lips, he hath plotted mischief.
text.KJV: 16:30 He shutteth his eyes to deuise froward things: moouing his lips he bringeth euill to passe.
text.NABRE: 16:30 Whoever winks an eye plans perversity; whoever purses the lips does evil.

text.NRSV: Gray hair is a crown of glory; it is gained by a righteous life.
text.Rotherham: A crown of adorning, is a hoary head, in the way of righteousness, it should be found.
text.KJV: 16:31 The hoary head is a crowne of glory, if it be found in the way of righteousnesse.
text.NABRE: 16:31 Gray hair is a crown of glory; it is gained by a life that is just.

text.NRSV: One who is slow to anger is better than the mighty, and one whose temper is controlled than one who captures a city.
text.Rotherham: Better he that is slow to anger, than a hero, and he that ruleth his spirit, than he that captureth a city.
text.KJV: 16:32 He that is slow to anger, is better then the mighty: and he that ruleth his spirit, then he that taketh a citie.
text.NABRE: 16:32 The patient are better than warriors, and those who rule their temper, better than the conqueror of a city.

text.NRSV: The lot is cast into the lap, but the decision is the Lord’s alone.
text.Rotherham: Into the lap, is cast the lot, but, from Yahweh, is its every decision.
text.KJV: 16:33 The lot is cast into the lap: but the whole disposing thereof is of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 16:33 Into the bag the lot is cast, but from the LORD comes every decision.

text.NRSV: Better is a dry morsel with quiet than a house full of feasting with strife.
text.Rotherham: Better a dry morsel, and peace therewith, than a house full of contentious sacrifices.
text.KJV: 17:1 Better is a drie morsell, and quietnesse therewith; then an house full of sacrifices with strife.
text.NABRE: 17:1 Chapter 17 - Better a dry crust with quiet than a house full of feasting with strife.

text.NRSV: A clever servant will rule over a son who acts shamefully, and will share the inheritance as one of the family.
text.Rotherham: A prudent servant, shall rule over a son who causeth shame, and, in the midst of brothers, shall he share the inheritance.
text.KJV: 17:2 A wise seruant shall haue rule ouer a son that causeth shame: and shall haue part of the inheritance among the brethren.
text.NABRE: 17:2 A wise servant will rule over an unworthy son, and will share the inheritance of the children.

text.NRSV: The crucible is for silver, and the furnace is for gold, but the Lord tests the heart.
text.Rotherham: Fining-pot for silver, crucible for gold, but, he that trieth hearts, is Yahweh.
text.KJV: 17:3 The fining pot is for siluer, and the furnace for gold: but the Lord trieth the hearts.
text.NABRE: 17:3 The crucible for silver, and the furnace for gold, but the tester of hearts is the LORD.

text.NRSV: An evildoer listens to wicked lips; and a liar gives heed to a mischievous tongue.
text.Rotherham: Discord, giveth heed to the aggrieving lip,—Falsehood, giveth ear to the destroying tongue.
text.KJV: 17:4 A wicked doer giueth heed to false lips: and a liar giueth eare to a naughtie tongue.
text.NABRE: 17:4 The evildoer gives heed to wicked lips, the liar, to a mischievous tongue.

text.NRSV: Those who mock the poor insult their Maker; those who are glad at calamity will not go unpunished.
text.Rotherham: He that mocketh the poor, hath reproached his Maker, He that maketh merry at distress, shall not be held innocent.
text.KJV: 17:5 Whoso mocketh the poore, reproacheth his maker: and he that is glad at calamities, shall not be vnpunished.
text.NABRE: 17:5 Whoever mocks the poor reviles their Maker; whoever rejoices in their misfortune will not go unpunished.

text.NRSV: Grandchildren are the crown of the aged, and the glory of children is their parents.
text.Rotherham: The crown of old men, consists of children’s children, and the adornment of children, is their fathers.
text.KJV: 17:6 Childrens children are the crowne of old men: and the glory of children are their fathers.
text.NABRE: 17:6 Children’s children are the crown of the elderly, and the glory of children is their parentage.

text.NRSV: Fine speech is not becoming to a fool; still less is false speech to a ruler.
text.Rotherham: Unseemly in an unworthy man, is the lip of excellence, much more, in one of noble mind, the lip of falsehood.
text.KJV: 17:7 Excellent speech becommeth not a foole: much lesse doe lying lippes a prince.
text.NABRE: 17:7 Fine words ill fit a fool; how much more lying lips, a noble!

text.NRSV: A bribe is like a magic stone in the eyes of those who give it; wherever they turn they prosper.
text.Rotherham: A gift, in the eyes of its owner, is, a stone of beauty, whithersoever it turneth, it bringeth prosperity.
text.KJV: 17:8 A gift is as a precious stone in the eyes of him that hath it: whithersoeuer it turneth, it prospereth.
text.NABRE: 17:8 A bribe seems a charm to its user; at every turn it brings success.

text.NRSV: One who forgives an affront fosters friendship, but one who dwells on disputes will alienate a friend.
text.Rotherham: He that hideth a transgression, seeketh love, but, he that repeateth a matter, separateth intimate friends.
text.KJV: 17:9 He that couereth a transgression, seeketh loue; but he that repeateth a matter, separateth very friends.
text.NABRE: 17:9 Whoever overlooks an offense fosters friendship, but whoever gossips about it separates friends.

text.NRSV: A rebuke goes deeper into one who has understanding than a hundred blows into a fool.
text.Rotherham: A reproof sinketh more deeply into an intelligent man than a hundred stripes, into a dullard!
text.KJV: 17:10 A reproofe entreth more into a wise man, then an hundred stripes into a foole.
text.NABRE: 17:10 A single reprimand does more for a discerning person than a hundred lashes for a fool.

text.NRSV: Evil people seek only rebellion, and a cruel messenger will be sent against them.
text.Rotherham: Nothing less than rebellion, doth a wicked man seek, and, a relentless messenger, shall be sent out against him.
text.KJV: 17:11 An euill man seeketh onely rebellion; therefore a cruell messenger shall be sent against him.
text.NABRE: 17:11 The wicked pursue only rebellion, and a merciless messenger is sent against them.

text.NRSV: Better to meet a she-bear robbed of its cubs than to confront a fool immersed in folly.
text.Rotherham: Let a bereaved bear encounter man, rather than a dullard, with his folly!
text.KJV: 17:12 Let a beare robbed of her whelps meet a man, rather then a foole in his folly.
text.NABRE: 17:12 Face a bear robbed of her cubs, but never fools in their folly!

text.NRSV: Evil will not depart from the house of one who returns evil for good.
text.Rotherham: He that returneth evil for good, evil shall not depart from his house.
text.KJV: 17:13 Whoso rewardeth euill for good, euill shall not depart from his house.
text.NABRE: 17:13 If you return evil for good, evil will not depart from your house.

text.NRSV: The beginning of strife is like letting out water; so stop before the quarrel breaks out.
text.Rotherham: A letting forth of water, is the beginning of strife, therefore, before it breaketh out, abandon, contention.
text.KJV: 17:14 The beginning of strife is as when one letteth out water: therfore leaue off contention, before it be medled with.
text.NABRE: 17:14 The start of strife is like the opening of a dam; check a quarrel before it bursts forth!

text.NRSV: One who justifies the wicked and one who condemns the righteous are both alike an abomination to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: He that justifieth the lawless, and he that condemneth the righteous, an abomination to Yahweh, are they, both.
text.KJV: 17:15 He that iustifieth the wicked, and he that condemneth the iust: euen they both are abomination to the Lord.
text.NABRE: 17:15 Whoever acquits the wicked, whoever condemns the just— both are an abomination to the LORD.

text.NRSV: Why should fools have a price in hand to buy wisdom, when they have no mind to learn?
text.Rotherham: Wherefore is there a price in the hand of a dullard? that he who is without sense, may acquire wisdom.
text.KJV: 17:16 Wherfore is there a price in the hand of a foole to get wisedome, seeing he hath no heart to it?
text.NABRE: 17:16 Of what use is money in the hands of fools when they have no heart to acquire wisdom?

text.NRSV: A friend loves at all times, and kinsfolk are born to share adversity.
text.Rotherham: At all times, doth a friend love, and, a brother for distress, must be born.
text.KJV: 17:17 A friend loueth at all times, and a brother is borne for aduersitie.
text.NABRE: 17:17 A friend is a friend at all times, and a brother is born for the time of adversity.

text.NRSV: It is senseless to give a pledge, to become surety for a neighbor.
text.Rotherham: A man lacking sense, is one who striketh hands, giving security, before his neighbour.
text.KJV: 17:18 A man void of vnderstanding striketh hands, and becommeth suretie in the presence of his friend.
text.NABRE: 17:18 Those without sense give their hands in pledge, becoming surety for their neighbors.

text.NRSV: One who loves transgression loves strife; one who builds a high threshold invites broken bones.
text.Rotherham: A lover of transgression, is one who loveth strife, he that heighteneth his door, seeketh grievous harm.
text.KJV: 17:19 He loueth transgression, that loueth strife: and he that exalteth his gate, seeketh destruction.
text.NABRE: 17:19 Those who love an offense love a fight; those who build their gate high court disaster.

text.NRSV: A crooked mind does not prosper, and one with a perverse tongue falls into calamity.
text.Rotherham: The crooked in heart, shall not find good, and, he that is perverse with his tongue, shall fall into wickedness.
text.KJV: 17:20 He that hath a froward heart, findeth no good, and he that hath a peruerse tongue, falleth into mischiefe.
text.NABRE: 17:20 The perverse in heart come to no good, and the double-tongued fall into trouble.

text.NRSV: A fool born to anyone is a sorrow, and the father of a fool has no joy.
text.Rotherham: He that begetteth a dullard, it is to his own grief, neither can the father of the base, rejoice.
text.KJV: 17:21 He that begetteth a foole, doth it to his sorrow: and the father of a foole hath no ioy.
text.NABRE: 17:21 Whoever conceives a fool has grief; the father of a numskull has no joy.

text.NRSV: A cheerful heart is a good medicine, but a downcast spirit dries up the bones.
text.Rotherham: A joyful heart, worketh an excellent cure,—but, a stricken spirit, drieth up the bone.
text.KJV: 17:22 A merrie heart doth good like a medicine: but a broken spirit drieth the bones.
text.NABRE: 17:22 A joyful heart is the health of the body, but a depressed spirit dries up the bones.

text.NRSV: The wicked accept a concealed bribe to pervert the ways of justice.
text.Rotherham: A bribe out of his bosom, doth a lawless man take, to pervert the ways of justice.
text.KJV: 17:23 A wicked man taketh a gift out of the bosome, to peruert the wayes of iudgement.
text.NABRE: 17:23 A guilty person takes out a bribe from the pocket, thus perverting the course of justice.

text.NRSV: The person of understanding sets his face toward wisdom, but the eyes of a fool are on the ends of the earth.
text.Rotherham: Before the face of the discerning, is wisdom, but, the eyes of a dullard, are in the ends of the earth.
text.KJV: 17:24 Wisedome is before him that hath vnderstanding: but the eyes of a foole are in the ends of the earth.
text.NABRE: 17:24 On the countenance of a discerning person is wisdom, but the eyes of a fool are on the ends of the earth.

text.NRSV: A foolish child is a grief to a father and bitterness to her who bore him.
text.Rotherham: A vexation to his father, is the son that is a dullard, and a bitterness, to her that bare him.
text.KJV: 17:25 A foolish sonne is a griefe to his father, & bitternes to her that bare him.
text.NABRE: 17:25 A foolish son is vexation to his father, and bitter sorrow to her who bore him.

text.NRSV: To impose a fine on the innocent is not right, or to flog the noble for their integrity.
text.Rotherham: Surely, to chastise the righteous, is not good, to smite the noble-minded for equity.
text.KJV: 17:26 Also to punish the iust is not good, nor to strike princes for equitie.
text.NABRE: 17:26 It is wrong to fine an innocent person, but beyond reason to scourge nobles.

text.NRSV: One who spares words is knowledgeable; one who is cool in spirit has understanding.
text.Rotherham: Sparing of his words, is one who valueth knowledge, and, of a thoughtful spirit, is a man of intelligence.
text.KJV: 17:27 He that hath knowledge, spareth his words: and a man of vnderstanding is of an excellent spirit.
text.NABRE: 17:27 Those who spare their words are truly knowledgeable, and those who are discreet are intelligent.

text.NRSV: Even fools who keep silent are considered wise; when they close their lips, they are deemed intelligent.
text.Rotherham: Even a fool, holding his peace, is accounted, wise,—He that closeth his lips, [is thought] to have understanding.
text.KJV: 17:28 Euen a foole, when he holdeth his peace, is counted wise: and he that shutteth his lips, is esteemed a man of vnderstanding.
text.NABRE: 17:28 Even fools, keeping silent, are considered wise; if they keep their lips closed, intelligent.

text.NRSV: The one who lives alone is self-indulgent, showing contempt for all who have sound judgment.
text.Rotherham: A man seeketh, for satisfaction, going his own way, through all safe counsel, he breaketh.
text.KJV: 18:1 Through desire a man hauing separated himselfe, seeketh and intermedleth with all wisedome.
text.NABRE: 18:1 Chapter 18 - One who is alienated seeks a pretext, with all persistence picks a quarrel.

text.NRSV: A fool takes no pleasure in understanding, but only in baring his mind.
text.Rotherham: A dullard, delighteth not, in understanding, in nothing save the exposing of his own heart.
text.KJV: 18:2 A foole hath no delight in vnderstanding, but that his heart may discouer it selfe.
text.NABRE: 18:2 Fools take no delight in understanding, but only in displaying what they think.

text.NRSV: When wickedness comes, contempt comes also; and with dishonor comes disgrace.
text.Rotherham: When the lawless man cometh in, then cometh also contempt, and, with shame, reproach.
text.KJV: 18:3 When the wicked commeth, then commeth also contempt, and with ignominie, reproch.
text.NABRE: 18:3 With wickedness comes contempt, and with disgrace, scorn.

text.NRSV: The words of the mouth are deep waters; the fountain of wisdom is a gushing stream.
text.Rotherham: Deep waters, are the words of a man’s mouth,—a bubbling brook, the well-spring of wisdom.
text.KJV: 18:4 The words of a mans mouth, are as deepe waters, and the well-spring of wisedome as a flowing brooke.
text.NABRE: 18:4 The words of one’s mouth are deep waters, the spring of wisdom, a running brook.

text.NRSV: It is not right to be partial to the wicked, or to subvert the innocent in judgment.
text.Rotherham: To prefer a lawless man, is not good, thrusting away the righteous, in judgment.
text.KJV: 18:5 It is not good to accept the person of the wicked, to ouerthrowe the righteous in iudgement.
text.NABRE: 18:5 It is not good to favor the guilty, nor to reject the claim of the just.

text.NRSV: A fool’s lips bring strife, and a fool’s mouth invites a flogging.
text.Rotherham: the lips of a dullard, enter into contention, and his mouth, for blows, crieth out.
text.KJV: 18:6 A fooles lips enter into contention, and his mouth calleth for strokes.
text.NABRE: 18:6 The lips of fools walk into a fight, and their mouths are asking for a beating.

text.NRSV: The mouths of fools are their undoing, and their lips are a snare to themselves.
text.Rotherham: The mouth of a dullard, is his ruin, and, his lips, are a snare to his soul.
text.KJV: 18:7 A fooles mouth is his destruction, and his lips are the snare of his soule.
text.NABRE: 18:7 The mouths of fools are their ruin; their lips are a deadly snare.

text.NRSV: The words of a whisperer are like delicious morsels; they go down into the inner parts of the body.
text.Rotherham: the words of a tattler, are dainties, they, therefore go down into the chambers of the inner man.
text.KJV: 18:8 The words of a tale bearer are as wounds, and they goe downe into the innermost parts of the belly.
text.NABRE: 18:8 The words of a talebearer are like dainty morsels: they sink into one’s inmost being.

text.NRSV: One who is slack in work is close kin to a spoiler.
text.Rotherham: Surely he that is slothful in his work, brother, is he to a master at laying waste.
text.KJV: 18:9 Hee also that is slouthful in his worke, is brother to him that is a great waster.
text.NABRE: 18:9 Those slack in their work are kin to the destroyer.

text.NRSV: The name of the Lord is a strong tower; the righteous run into it and are safe.
text.Rotherham: A tower of strength, is the Name of Yahweh, thereinto, runneth the righteous, and is safe.
text.KJV: 18:10 The name of the Lord is a a strong tower: the righteous runneth into it, and is safe.
text.NABRE: 18:10 The name of the LORD is a strong tower; the just run to it and are safe.

text.NRSV: The wealth of the rich is their strong city; in their imagination it is like a high wall.
text.Rotherham: The substance of a rich man, is his strong city, and like a high wall, in his imagination.
text.KJV: 18:11 The rich mans wealth is his strong citie: and as an high wall in his owne conceit.
text.NABRE: 18:11 The wealth of the rich is their strong city; they fancy it a high wall.

text.NRSV: Before destruction one’s heart is haughty, but humility goes before honor.
text.Rotherham: Before grievous injury, a man’s heart is haughty, and, before honour, is humility.
text.KJV: 18:12 Before destruction the heart of man is haughtie, and before honour is humilitie.
text.NABRE: 18:12 Before disaster the heart is haughty, but before honor is humility.

text.NRSV: If one gives answer before hearing, it is folly and shame.
text.Rotherham: He that answereth before he heareth, a folly, it is to him, and, a reproach.
text.KJV: 18:13 He that answereth a matter before he heareth it, it is folly and shame vnto him.
text.NABRE: 18:13 Whoever answers before listening, theirs is folly and shame.

text.NRSV: The human spirit can endure a physical sickness, but a broken spirit who can bear?
text.Rotherham: The spirit of a man, sustaineth his sickness, but, a dejected spirit, who can bear it?
text.KJV: 18:14 The spirit of a man will sustaine his infirmitie: but a wounded spirit who can beare?
text.NABRE: 18:14 One’s spirit supports one when ill, but a broken spirit who can bear?

text.NRSV: An intelligent mind acquires knowledge, and the ear of the wise seeks knowledge.
text.Rotherham: the heart of the intelligent, acquireth knowledge, yea, the ear of the wise, seeketh knowledge.
text.KJV: 18:15 The heart of the prudent getteth knowledge; and the eare of the wise seeketh knowledge.
text.NABRE: 18:15 The heart of the intelligent acquires knowledge, and the ear of the wise seeks knowledge.

text.NRSV: A gift opens doors; it gives access to the great.
text.Rotherham: The gift of a man, maketh room for him, and, before great men, setteth him down.
text.KJV: 18:16 A mans gift maketh roome for him, & bringeth him before great men.
text.NABRE: 18:16 Gifts clear the way for people, winning access to the great.

text.NRSV: The one who first states a case seems right, until the other comes and cross-examines.
text.Rotherham: Righteous is he that is first in his own cause, then cometh his neighbour, and thoroughly searcheth him.
text.KJV: 18:17 He that is first in his owne cause, seemeth iust; but his neighbour commeth and searcheth him.
text.NABRE: 18:17 Those who plead the case first seem to be in the right; then the opponent comes and cross-examines them.

text.NRSV: Casting the lot puts an end to disputes and decides between powerful contenders.
text.Rotherham: The lot causeth, contentions, to cease, and, the mighty, it parteth.
text.KJV: 18:18 The lot causeth contentions to cease, and parteth betweene the mighty.
text.NABRE: 18:18 The lot puts an end to disputes, and decides a controversy between the mighty.

text.NRSV: An ally offended is stronger than a city; such quarreling is like the bars of a castle.
text.Rotherham: A brother estranged, [is worse] than a strong city,—and, contentions, are as the bar of a citadel.
text.KJV: 18:19 A brother offended is harder to be wonne then a strong citie: and their contentions are like the barres of a castle.
text.NABRE: 18:19 A brother offended is more unyielding than a stronghold; such strife is more daunting than castle gates.

text.NRSV: From the fruit of the mouth one’s stomach is satisfied; the yield of the lips brings contentment.
text.Rotherham: Of the fruit of a man’s mouth, shall his inmost mind be satisfied, with the product of his lips, shall he be satisfied.
text.KJV: 18:20 A mans belly shall be satisfied with the fruite of his mouth; and with the increase of his lippes shall he be filled.
text.NABRE: 18:20 With the fruit of one’s mouth one’s belly is filled, with the produce of one’s lips one is sated.

text.NRSV: Death and life are in the power of the tongue, and those who love it will eat its fruits.
text.Rotherham: Death and life, are in the power of the tongue, and, its friends, shall eat its fruits.
text.KJV: 18:21 Death and life are in the power of the tongue; and they that loue it shall eate the fruite thereof.
text.NABRE: 18:21 Death and life are in the power of the tongue; those who choose one shall eat its fruit.

text.NRSV: He who finds a wife finds a good thing, and obtains favor from the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Who hath found a wife, hath found a blessing, and hath obtained favour from Yahweh.
text.KJV: 18:22 Who so findeth a wife, findeth a good thing, and obtaineth fauour of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 18:22 To find a wife is to find happiness, a favor granted by the LORD.

text.NRSV: The poor use entreaties, but the rich answer roughly.
text.Rotherham: Supplications, doth the poor man utter, but, the rich, answereth fiercely.
text.KJV: 18:23 The poore vseth intreaties, but the rich answereth roughly.
text.NABRE: 18:23 The poor implore, but the rich answer harshly.

text.NRSV: Some friends play at friendship but a true friend sticks closer than one’s nearest kin.
text.Rotherham: A man having [many] friends, shall come to ruin, but there is a loving one, who sticketh closer than a brother.
text.KJV: 18:24 A man that hath friends must shewe himselfe friendly: and there is a friend that sticketh closer then a brother.
text.NABRE: 18:24 There are friends who bring ruin, but there are true friends more loyal than a brother.

text.NRSV: Better the poor walking in integrity than one perverse of speech who is a fool.
text.Rotherham: Better is a poor man, walking in his integrity, than one of perverse lips, and, he, a dullard.
text.KJV: 19:1 Better is the poore that walketh in his integrity, then he that is peruerse in his lippes, and is a foole.
text.NABRE: 19:1 Chapter 19 - Better to be poor and walk in integrity than rich and crooked in one’s ways.

text.NRSV: Desire without knowledge is not good, and one who moves too hurriedly misses the way.
text.Rotherham: Surely, for the soul to be, without knowledge, is not good, and, he that hasteth with his feet, strayeth.
text.KJV: 19:2 Also, that the soule be without knowledge, it is not good, and hee that hasteth with his feete, sinneth.
text.NABRE: 19:2 Desire without knowledge is not good; and whoever acts hastily, blunders.

text.NRSV: One’s own folly leads to ruin, yet the heart rages against the Lord.
text.Rotherham: The folly of a man, subverteth his way, and, against Yahweh, his heart is angry.
text.KJV: 19:3 The foolishnesse of man peruerteth his way: and his heart fretteth against the Lord.
text.NABRE: 19:3 Their own folly leads people astray; in their hearts they rage against the LORD.

text.NRSV: Wealth brings many new friends, but the poor are left friendless.
text.Rotherham: Wealth, addeth many friends, but, the poor man, from his own friend, is parted.
text.KJV: 19:4 Wealth maketh many friends: but the poore is separated from his neighbour.
text.NABRE: 19:4 Wealth adds many friends, but the poor are left friendless.

text.NRSV: A false witness will not go unpunished, and one who utters lies will not escape.
text.Rotherham: A false witness, shall not be held innocent, and, he that uttereth lies, shall not escape.
text.KJV: 19:5 A false witnesse shall not be vnpunished: and he that speaketh lyes, shal not escape.
text.NABRE: 19:5 The false witness will not go unpunished, and whoever utters lies will not escape.

text.NRSV: Many seek the favor of a generous person, and everyone is a friend to a giver of gifts.
text.Rotherham: Many, will entreat the favour of a noble, and, every one, is a friend to a man abounding in gifts.
text.KJV: 19:6 Many will entreate the fauour of the Prince: and euery man is a friend to him that giueth gifts.
text.NABRE: 19:6 Many curry favor with a noble; everybody is a friend of a gift giver.

text.NRSV: If all the kindred of the poor hate them, how much more do their friends avoid them! They may help with words, but such are not to be found.
text.Rotherham: All the brethren of a poor man, hate him, how much more have, his friends, gone far from him, he may hunt up promises—there are none.
text.KJV: 19:7 All the brethren of the poore doe hate him: howe much more doe his friends goe farre from him? Hee pursueth them with words, yet they are wanting to him.
text.NABRE: 19:7 All the kin of the poor despise them; how much more do their friends shun them!

text.NRSV: To get wisdom is to love oneself; to keep understanding is to prosper.
text.Rotherham: He that acquireth sense, loveth his own soul, he that guardeth understanding, shall find blessing.
text.KJV: 19:8 He that getteth wisedome loueth his owne soule: he that keepeth vnderstanding shall find good.
text.NABRE: 19:8 Those who gain sense truly love themselves; those who preserve understanding will find success.

text.NRSV: A false witness will not go unpunished, and one who utters lies will perish.
text.Rotherham: A false witness, shall not be held innocent, and, he that uttereth lies, shall perish.
text.KJV: 19:9 A false witnesse shall not be vnpunished, and hee that speaketh lyes, shall perish.
text.NABRE: 19:9 The false witness will not go unpunished, and whoever utters lies will perish.

text.NRSV: It is not fitting for a fool to live in luxury, much less for a slave to rule over princes.
text.Rotherham: Unseemly for a dullard, is delicate living, how much more for, a servant, to bear rule over princes.
text.KJV: 19:10 Delight is not seemely for a foole: much lesse for a seruant to haue rule ouer princes.
text.NABRE: 19:10 Luxury is not befitting a fool; much less should a slave rule over princes.

text.NRSV: Those with good sense are slow to anger, and it is their glory to overlook an offense.
text.Rotherham: The discretion of a man, deferreth his anger, and, his adorning, is to pass over transgression.
text.KJV: 19:11 The discretion of a man deferreth his anger: and it is his glory to passe ouer a transgression.
text.NABRE: 19:11 It is good sense to be slow to anger, and an honor to overlook an offense.

text.NRSV: A king’s wrath is like the growling of a lion, but his favor is like dew on the grass.
text.Rotherham: The growl as of a young lion, is the rage of a king, but, like dew upon the grass, is his good pleasure.
text.KJV: 19:12 The kings wrath is as the roaring of a lyon: but his fauour is as dewe vpon the grasse.
text.NABRE: 19:12 The king’s wrath is like the roar of a lion, but his favor, like dew on the grass.

text.NRSV: A foolish child is ruin to a father, and a wife’s quarreling is a continual dripping of rain.
text.Rotherham: Engulfing ruin to his father, is a son that is a dullard,—and, a continuous dripping, are the contentions of a wife.
text.KJV: 19:13 A foolish sonne is the calamity of his father; and the contentions of a wife are a continuall dropping.
text.NABRE: 19:13 The foolish son is ruin to his father, and a quarrelsome wife is water constantly dripping.

text.NRSV: House and wealth are inherited from parents, but a prudent wife is from the Lord.
text.Rotherham: House and substance, are an inheritance from one’s fathers, but, from Yahweh, cometh a wife who is prudent.
text.KJV: 19:14 House and riches, are the inheritance of fathers; and a prudent wife is from the Lord.
text.NABRE: 19:14 Home and possessions are an inheritance from parents, but a prudent wife is from the LORD.

text.NRSV: Laziness brings on deep sleep; an idle person will suffer hunger.
text.Rotherham: Sloth, falleth into a deep sleep, and, a soul that is indolent, shall famish.
text.KJV: 19:15 Slouthfulnesse casteth into a deep sleepe: and an idle soule shall suffer hunger.
text.NABRE: 19:15 Laziness brings on deep sleep, and the sluggard goes hungry.

text.NRSV: Those who keep the commandment will live; those who are heedless of their ways will die.
text.Rotherham: One who guardeth the commandment, guardeth his life, he that is reckless in his ways, shall die.
text.KJV: 19:16 He that keepeth the commandement, keepeth his owne soule: but hee that despiseth his wayes, shall die.
text.NABRE: 19:16 Those who keep commands keep their lives, but those who despise these ways will die.

text.NRSV: Whoever is kind to the poor lends to the Lord, and will be repaid in full.
text.Rotherham: A lender to Yahweh, is one who sheweth favour to the poor, and, his good deed, He will pay him back.
text.KJV: 19:17 Hee that hath pity vpon the poore, lendeth vnto the Lord; and that which he hath giuen, will he pay him againe.
text.NABRE: 19:17 Whoever cares for the poor lends to the LORD, who will pay back the sum in full.

text.NRSV: Discipline your children while there is hope; do not set your heart on their destruction.
text.Rotherham: Correct thy son, because there is hope, yet, not so as to slay him, let thy passion be excited.
text.KJV: 19:18 Chasten thy sonne while there is hope; and let not thy soule spare for his crying.
text.NABRE: 19:18 Discipline your son, for there is hope; but do not be intent on his death.

text.NRSV: A violent tempered person will pay the penalty; for if you effect a rescue, you will only have to do it again.
text.Rotherham: A man in a rage, taketh away the penalty,—nevertheless, if thou let him go free, the more [stripes] must thou add.
text.KJV: 19:19 A man of great wrath shall suffer punishment: for if thou deliuer him, yet thou must doe it againe.
text.NABRE: 19:19 A wrathful person bears the penalty; after one rescue, you will have it to do again.

text.NRSV: Listen to advice and accept instruction, that you may gain wisdom for the future.
text.Rotherham: Hear counsel, receive correction, that thou mayest be wise in thine after-life.
text.KJV: 19:20 Heare counsell, and receiue instruction, that thou mayest be wise in thy latter end.
text.NABRE: 19:20 Listen to counsel and receive instruction, that you may eventually become wise.

text.NRSV: The human mind may devise many plans, but it is the purpose of the Lord that will be established.
text.Rotherham: Many are the devices in a man’s heart, but, the counsel of Yahweh, that, shall stand.
text.KJV: 19:21 There are many deuices in a mans heart: neuerthelesse the counsell of the Lord, that shall stand.
text.NABRE: 19:21 Many are the plans of the human heart, but it is the decision of the LORD that endures.

text.NRSV: What is desirable in a person is loyalty, and it is better to be poor than a liar.
text.Rotherham: The charm of a man, is his lovingkindness,—and better a poor man, than one who deceiveth.
text.KJV: 19:22 The desire of a man is his kindnesse: and a poore man is better then a lyar.
text.NABRE: 19:22 What is desired of a person is fidelity; rather be poor than a liar.

text.NRSV: The fear of the Lord is life-giving; one rests satisfied and untouched by harm.
text.Rotherham: The reverence of Yahweh, leadeth to life, satisfied then, let a man remain—let him not be visited by calamity.
text.KJV: 19:23 The feare of the Lord tendeth to life, and he that hath it shall abide satisfied: he shall not be visited with euill.
text.NABRE: 19:23 The fear of the LORD leads to life; one eats and sleeps free from any harm.

text.NRSV: The lazy person buries a hand in the dish, and will not even bring it back to the mouth.
text.Rotherham: A sluggard burieth his hand in the dish, even unto his own mouth, will he not bring it back.
text.KJV: 19:24 A slouthfull man hideth his hand in his bosome, and wil not so much as bring it to his mouth againe.
text.NABRE: 19:24 The sluggard buries a hand in the dish; not even lifting it to the mouth.

text.NRSV: Strike a scoffer, and the simple will learn prudence; reprove the intelligent, and they will gain knowledge.
text.Rotherham: The scoffer, thou shalt smite, and, the simple, will beware, but correct a man of intelligence—he will understand teaching.
text.KJV: 19:25 Smite a scorner, and the simple will beware; and reprooue one that hath vnderstanding, and he will vnderstand knowledge.
text.NABRE: 19:25 Beat a scoffer and the naive learn a lesson; rebuke the intelligent and they gain knowledge.

text.NRSV: Those who do violence to their father and chase away their mother are children who cause shame and bring reproach.
text.Rotherham: He that ruineth his father, and chaseth away his mother, is a son causing shame and reproach.
text.KJV: 19:26 He that wasteth his father, and chaseth away his mother, is a sonne that causeth shame, and bringeth reproch.
text.NABRE: 19:26 Whoever mistreats a father or drives away a mother, is a shameless and disgraceful child.

text.NRSV: Cease straying, my child, from the words of knowledge, in order that you may hear instruction.
text.Rotherham: Cease, my son, to hear instruction that would cause thee to wander from the sayings of knowledge.
text.KJV: 19:27 Cease, my sonne, to heare the instruction, that causeth to erre from the words of knowledge.
text.NABRE: 19:27 My son, stop attending to correction; start straying from words of knowledge.

text.NRSV: A worthless witness mocks at justice, and the mouth of the wicked devours iniquity.
text.Rotherham: An abandoned witness, scorneth justice, and, the mouth of lawless men, swalloweth iniquity.
text.KJV: 19:28 An vngodly witnesse scorneth iudgement: and the mouth of the wicked deuoureth iniquitie.
text.NABRE: 19:28 An unprincipled witness scoffs at justice, and the mouth of the wicked pours out iniquity.

text.NRSV: Condemnation is ready for scoffers, and flogging for the backs of fools.
text.Rotherham: Prepared for scoffers, are punishments, and stripes, for the back of dullards.
text.KJV: 19:29 Iudgements are prepared for scorners, and stripes for the backe of fooles.
text.NABRE: 19:29 Rods are prepared for scoffers, and blows for the backs of fools.

text.NRSV: Wine is a mocker, strong drink a brawler, and whoever is led astray by it is not wise.
text.Rotherham: Wine is, a scoffer, and strong drink, a brawler, every one therefore who erreth therein, is unwise.
text.KJV: 20:1 Wine is a mocker, strong drinke is raging: and whosoeuer is deceiued thereby, is not wise.
text.NABRE: 20:1 Chapter 20 - Wine is arrogant, strong drink is riotous; none who are intoxicated by them are wise.

text.NRSV: The dread of a king is like the growling of a lion; anyone who provokes him to anger forfeits life itself.
text.Rotherham: The growl as of a young lion, is the dread inspired by a king, he that provoketh him, endangereth his own life.
text.KJV: 20:2 The feare of a king, is as the roaring of a Lion: who so prouoketh him to anger, sinneth against his owne soule.
text.NABRE: 20:2 The terror of a king is like the roar of a lion; those who incur his anger forfeit their lives.

text.NRSV: It is an honor to abstain from strife, but every fool is quick to quarrel.
text.Rotherham: Honour, hath the man who sitteth away from strife, but, any fool, may break through.
text.KJV: 20:3 It is an honour for a man to cease from strife: but euery foole will be medling.
text.NABRE: 20:3 A person gains honor by avoiding strife, while every fool starts a quarrel.

text.NRSV: The lazy person does not plow in season; harvest comes, and there is nothing to be found.
text.Rotherham: By reason of the autumn, a sluggard will not plough, therefore shall he beg in harvest, and there be nothing.
text.KJV: 20:4 The sluggard will not plow by reason of the cold; therefore shall he begge in haruest, and haue nothing.
text.NABRE: 20:4 In seedtime sluggards do not plow; when they look for the harvest, it is not there.

text.NRSV: The purposes in the human mind are like deep water, but the intelligent will draw them out.
text.Rotherham: Deep water, is counsel in a man’s heart, but, a man of understanding, will draw it out.
text.KJV: 20:5 Counsell in the heart of man is like deepe water: but a man of vnderstanding will draw it out.
text.NABRE: 20:5 The intention of the human heart is deep water, but the intelligent draw it forth.

text.NRSV: Many proclaim themselves loyal, but who can find one worthy of trust?
text.Rotherham: A kind man one may call, a great man,—but, a faithful man, who can find?
text.KJV: 20:6 Most men will proclaime euery one his owne goodnes: but a faithfull man who can finde?
text.NABRE: 20:6 Many say, “My loyal friend,” but who can find someone worthy of trust?

text.NRSV: The righteous walk in integrity—happy are the children who follow them!
text.Rotherham: As for a righteous man, walking in his integrity, how happy are his children after him!
text.KJV: 20:7 The iust man walketh in his integritie: his children are blessed after him.
text.NABRE: 20:7 The just walk in integrity; happy are their children after them!

text.NRSV: A king sitting on the throne of judgment winnows all evil with his eyes.
text.Rotherham: A king sitting on the throne of judgment, scattereth, with his eyes, all wrong.
text.KJV: 20:8 A king that sitteth in the throne of iudgement, scattereth away all euill with his eyes.
text.NABRE: 20:8 A king seated on the throne of judgment dispels all evil with his glance.

text.NRSV: Who can say, “I have made my heart clean; I am pure from my sin”?
text.Rotherham: Who can say, I have cleansed my heart, I am pure from my sin?
text.KJV: 20:9 Who can say, I haue made my heart cleane, I am pure from my sinne?
text.NABRE: 20:9 Who can say, “I have made my heart clean, I am cleansed of my sin”?

text.NRSV: Diverse weights and diverse measures are both alike an abomination to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Divers weights, and divers measures, an abomination to Yahweh, are they, both.
text.KJV: 20:10 Diuers weights, and diuers measures, both of them are alike abomination to the Lord.
text.NABRE: 20:10 Varying weights, varying measures, are both an abomination to the LORD.

text.NRSV: Even children make themselves known by their acts, by whether what they do is pure and right.
text.Rotherham: Even, by his doings, doth a youth make himself known, whether, pure and upright, be his work.
text.KJV: 20:11 Euen a childe is knowen by his doings, whether his worke be pure, and whether it be right.
text.NABRE: 20:11 In their actions even children can playact though their deeds be blameless and right.

text.NRSV: The hearing ear and the seeing eye—the Lord has made them both.
text.Rotherham: The hearing ear, and the seeing eye, Yahweh hath made them, both.
text.KJV: 20:12 The hearing eare, and the seeing eye, the Lord hath made euen both of them.
text.NABRE: 20:12 The ear that hears, the eye that sees— the LORD has made them both.

text.NRSV: Do not love sleep, or else you will come to poverty; open your eyes, and you will have plenty of bread.
text.Rotherham: Do not love sleep, lest thou come to poverty, open thine eyes, be satisfied with bread.
text.KJV: 20:13 Loue not sleepe, lest thou come to pouertie: open thine eyes, and thou shalt be satisfied with bread.
text.NABRE: 20:13 Do not love sleep lest you be reduced to poverty; keep your eyes open, have your fill of food.

text.NRSV: “Bad, bad,” says the buyer, then goes away and boasts.
text.Rotherham: Bad! bad! saith the buyer, but, going his way, then, he boasteth.
text.KJV: 20:14 It is nought, it is nought (saith the buyer:) but when he is gone his way, then he boasteth.
text.NABRE: 20:14 “Bad, bad!” says the buyer, then goes away only to boast.

text.NRSV: There is gold, and abundance of costly stones; but the lips of knowledge are a precious jewel.
text.Rotherham: There are gold, and an abundance of corals, but, precious jewels, are the lips of knowledge.
text.KJV: 20:15 There is gold, and a multitude of Rubies: but the lips of knowledge are a precious iewell.
text.NABRE: 20:15 One can put on gold and abundant jewels, but wise lips are the most precious ornament.

text.NRSV: Take the garment of one who has given surety for a stranger; seize the pledge from those who are foreigners.
text.Rotherham: Take his garment who is pledge for a stranger,—then, for a woman unknown, accept him as surety.
text.KJV: 20:16 Take his garment that is suerty for a stranger: and take a pledge of him for a strange woman.
text.NABRE: 20:16 Take the garment of the one who became surety for a stranger; if for foreigners, exact the pledge!

text.NRSV: Bread gained by deceit is sweet, but afterward the mouth will be full of gravel.
text.Rotherham: Sweet to a man, may be the bread of falsehood, but, afterward, shall his mouth be filled with gravel.
text.KJV: 20:17 Bread of deceit is sweet to a man: but afterwards his mouth shall be filled with grauell.
text.NABRE: 20:17 Bread earned by deceit is sweet, but afterward the mouth is filled with gravel.

text.NRSV: Plans are established by taking advice; wage war by following wise guidance.
text.Rotherham: Plans—by counsel, shalt thou establish, and, with concerted measures, make thou war.
text.KJV: 20:18 Euery purpose is established by counsell: and with good aduice make warre.
text.NABRE: 20:18 Plans made with advice succeed; with wise direction wage your war.

text.NRSV: A gossip reveals secrets; therefore do not associate with a babbler.
text.Rotherham: A revealer of secrets, is one who goeth about talebearing, therefore, with him who openeth his lips, shalt thou not have fellowship.
text.KJV: 20:19 He that goeth about as a tale-bearer, reueileth secrets; therefore meddle not with him that flattereth with his lippes.
text.NABRE: 20:19 A slanderer reveals secrets; so have nothing to do with a babbler!

text.NRSV: If you curse father or mother, your lamp will go out in utter darkness.
text.Rotherham: Whoso revileth his father or his mother, his lamp shall be put out in deep darkness.
text.KJV: 20:20 Who so curseth his father or his mother, his lampe shall be put out in obscure darkenesse.
text.NABRE: 20:20 Those who curse father or mother— their lamp will go out in the dead of night.

text.NRSV: An inheritance quickly acquired at the beginning will not be blessed in the end.
text.Rotherham: An inheritance hastily gotten at the beginning, the latter end thereof, shall not bring blessing.
text.KJV: 20:21 An inheritance may be gotten hastily at the beginning: but the ende thereof shall not be blessed.
text.NABRE: 20:21 Possessions greedily guarded at the outset will not be blessed in the end.

text.NRSV: Do not say, “I will repay evil”; wait for the Lord, and he will help you.
text.Rotherham: Do not say, I will requite wrong! Wait thou for Yahweh that he may save thee.
text.KJV: 20:22 Say not thou, I will recompence euil: but wait on the Lord, and he shall saue thee.
text.NABRE: 20:22 Do not say, “I will repay evil!” Wait for the LORD, who will help you.

text.NRSV: Diverse weights are an abomination to the Lord, and false scales are not good.
text.Rotherham: An abomination to Yahweh, are divers weights, and, deceptive balances, are not good.
text.KJV: 20:23 Diuers waights are an abomination vnto the Lord: and a false ballance is not good.
text.NABRE: 20:23 Varying weights are an abomination to the LORD, and false scales are not good.

text.NRSV: All our steps are ordered by the Lord; how then can we understand our own ways?
text.Rotherham: From Yahweh, are a man’s steps, a son of earth, then—how can he discern his way?
text.KJV: 20:24 Mans goings are of the Lord; how can a man then vnderstand his owne way?
text.NABRE: 20:24 Our steps are from the LORD; how, then, can mortals understand their way?

text.NRSV: It is a snare for one to say rashly, “It is holy,” and reflect only after making a vow.
text.Rotherham: It is a snare to a man, that he should rashly cry Holy! and, after making vows, to reflect!
text.KJV: 20:25 It is a snare to the man who deuoureth that which is holy: and after vowes, to make inquirie.
text.NABRE: 20:25 It is a trap to pledge rashly a sacred gift, and after a vow, then to reflect.

text.NRSV: A wise king winnows the wicked, and drives the wheel over them.
text.Rotherham: A wise king, winnoweth out the lawless, when he hath turned over them the wheel.
text.KJV: 20:26 A wise king scattereth the wicked, & bringeth the wheele ouer them.
text.NABRE: 20:26 A wise king winnows the wicked, and threshes them under the cartwheel.

text.NRSV: The human spirit is the lamp of the Lord, searching every inmost part.
text.Rotherham: The lamp of Yahweh, is the spirit of a son of earth, searching all the chambers of the inner man.
text.KJV: 20:27 The spirit of man is the candle of the Lord, searching all the inward parts of the belly.
text.NABRE: 20:27 A lamp from the LORD is human life-breath; it searches through the inmost being.

text.NRSV: Loyalty and faithfulness preserve the king, and his throne is upheld by righteousness.
text.Rotherham: Lovingkindness and faithfulness, will guard a king,—therefore should he support, with lovingkindness, his throne.
text.KJV: 20:28 Mercy and trueth preserue the king: and his throne is vpholden by mercy.
text.NABRE: 20:28 His steadfast loyalty safeguards the king, and he upholds his throne by justice.

text.NRSV: The glory of youths is their strength, but the beauty of the aged is their gray hair.
text.Rotherham: The beauty of young men, is their strength, and, the ornament of old men, a hoary head.
text.KJV: 20:29 The glory of yong men is their strength: and the beautie of old men is the gray head.
text.NABRE: 20:29 The glory of the young is their strength, and the dignity of the old is gray hair.

text.NRSV: Blows that wound cleanse away evil; beatings make clean the inmost parts.
text.Rotherham: Blows that cut in, cleanse away wickedness, and, smitings, [enter] the chambers of the inner man.
text.KJV: 20:30 The blewnes of a wound cleanseth away euill: so doe stripes the inward parts of the belly.
text.NABRE: 20:30 Evil is cleansed away by bloody lashes, and a scourging to the inmost being.

text.NRSV: The king’s heart is a stream of water in the hand of the Lord; he turns it wherever he will.
text.Rotherham: [Like] channels of water, is the heart of a king, in the hand of Yahweh,—whithersoever he will, he turneth it.
text.KJV: 21:1 The kings heart is in the hand of the Lord, as the riuers of water: hee turneth it whithersoeuer he will.
text.NABRE: 21:1 Chapter 21 - A king’s heart is channeled water in the hand of the LORD; God directs it where he pleases.

text.NRSV: All one’s ways may be right in one’s own eyes, but the Lord weighs the heart.
text.Rotherham: Every way of a man, may be right in his own eyes, but, he that testeth hearts, is Yahweh.
text.KJV: 21:2 Euery way of a man is right in his owne eyes: but the Lord pondereth the hearts.
text.NABRE: 21:2 All your ways may be straight in your own eyes, but it is the LORD who weighs hearts.

text.NRSV: To do righteousness and justice is more acceptable to the Lord than sacrifice.
text.Rotherham: To do righteousness and justice, is more choice to Yahweh than sacrifice.
text.KJV: 21:3 To doe iustice and iudgement, is more acceptable to the Lord, then sacrifice.
text.NABRE: 21:3 To do what is right and just is more acceptable to the LORD than sacrifice.

text.NRSV: Haughty eyes and a proud heart—the lamp of the wicked—are sin.
text.Rotherham: Loftiness of eyes, and ambition of heart—the lamp of the lawless, are sin.
text.KJV: 21:4 An high looke, and a proud heart, and the plowing of the wicked, is sinne.
text.NABRE: 21:4 Haughty eyes and a proud heart— the lamp of the wicked will fail.

text.NRSV: The plans of the diligent lead surely to abundance, but everyone who is hasty comes only to want.
text.Rotherham: The plans of the diligent, tend only to abundance, but, of every one that is urgent, only to want.
text.KJV: 21:5 The thoughts of the diligent tend onely to plenteousnes: but of euery one that is hastie, onely to want.
text.NABRE: 21:5 The plans of the diligent end in profit, but those of the hasty end in loss.

text.NRSV: The getting of treasures by a lying tongue is a fleeting vapor and a snare of death.
text.Rotherham: The gaining of treasures with a tongue of falsehood, is a vapour driven away, [they who seek them] seek death,
text.KJV: 21:6 The getting of treasures by a lying tongue, is a vanitie tossed to and fro of them that seeke death.
text.NABRE: 21:6 Trying to get rich by lying is chasing a bubble over deadly snares.

text.NRSV: The violence of the wicked will sweep them away, because they refuse to do what is just.
text.Rotherham: The violence of the lawless, shall drag them away,—because they have refused to do justice.
text.KJV: 21:7 The robbery of the wicked shall destroy them; because they refuse to doe iudgement.
text.NABRE: 21:7 The violence of the wicked will sweep them away, because they refuse to do what is right.

text.NRSV: The way of the guilty is crooked, but the conduct of the pure is right.
text.Rotherham: Crooked is the way of a guilty man, but, as for the pure, straight is his dealing.
text.KJV: 21:8 The way of man is froward and strange: but as for the pure; his worke is right.
text.NABRE: 21:8 One’s path may be winding and unfamiliar, but one’s conduct is blameless and right.

text.NRSV: It is better to live in a corner of the housetop than in a share of a house with a contentious woman.
text.Rotherham: Better to dwell on the corner of the roof, than a quarrelsome wife, and a house in common.
text.KJV: 21:9 It is better to dwell in a corner of the house top; then with a brawling woman in a wide house.
text.NABRE: 21:9 It is better to dwell in a corner of the housetop than in a mansion with a quarrelsome woman.

text.NRSV: The souls of the wicked desire evil; their neighbors find no mercy in their eyes.
text.Rotherham: The soul of the lawless man, craveth mischief, his own friend, findeth no favour in his eyes.
text.KJV: 21:10 The soule of the wicked desireth euill: his neighbour findeth no fauour in his eyes.
text.NABRE: 21:10 The soul of the wicked desires evil; their neighbor finds no pity in their eyes.

text.NRSV: When a scoffer is punished, the simple become wiser; when the wise are instructed, they increase in knowledge.
text.Rotherham: When the scoffer is punished, the simple, becometh wise, when the wise is instructed, he receiveth knowledge.
text.KJV: 21:11 When the scorner is punished, the simple is made wise: and when the wise is instructed, he receiueth knowledge.
text.NABRE: 21:11 When scoffers are punished the naive become wise; when the wise succeed, they gain knowledge.

text.NRSV: The Righteous One observes the house of the wicked; he casts the wicked down to ruin.
text.Rotherham: The Righteous One observeth the house of the lawless,—He is ready to cast down lawless men into misfortune.
