text.KJV: 21:12 The righteous man wisely considereth the house of the wicked: but God ouerthroweth the wicked for their wickednesse.
text.NABRE: 21:12 The Righteous One appraises the house of the wicked, bringing down the wicked to ruin.

text.NRSV: If you close your ear to the cry of the poor, you will cry out and not be heard.
text.Rotherham: He that shutteth his ear from the cry of the poor, even he, shall call, and not be answered.
text.KJV: 21:13 Whoso stoppeth his eares at the cry of the poore, hee also shall cry himselfe, but shall not be heard.
text.NABRE: 21:13 Those who shut their ears to the cry of the poor will themselves call out and not be answered.

text.NRSV: A gift in secret averts anger; and a concealed bribe in the bosom, strong wrath.
text.Rotherham: A gift in secret, quencheth anger, and, a present in the bosom, mighty wrath.
text.KJV: 21:14 A gift in secret pacifieth anger; and a reward in the bosome, strong wrath.
text.NABRE: 21:14 A secret gift allays anger, and a present concealed, violent wrath.

text.NRSV: When justice is done, it is a joy to the righteous, but dismay to evildoers.
text.Rotherham: It is a joy, to a righteous man, to do justice, but, dismay, to the workers of iniquity.
text.KJV: 21:15 It is ioy to the iust to doe iudgement: but destruction shalbe to the workers of iniquitie.
text.NABRE: 21:15 When justice is done it is a joy for the just, downfall for evildoers.

text.NRSV: Whoever wanders from the way of understanding will rest in the assembly of the dead.
text.Rotherham: The man who wandereth from the way of discretion, in the gathered host of the shades, shall settle down.
text.KJV: 21:16 The man that wandreth out of the way of vnderstanding, shall remaine in the congregation of the dead.
text.NABRE: 21:16 Whoever strays from the way of good sense will abide in the assembly of the shades.

text.NRSV: Whoever loves pleasure will suffer want; whoever loves wine and oil will not be rich.
text.Rotherham: A needy man, shall he be that loveth merriment, the lover of wine and oil, shall not become rich.
text.KJV: 21:17 He that loueth pleasure, shall be a poore man: hee that loueth wine and oyle, shall not be rich.
text.NABRE: 21:17 The lover of pleasure will suffer want; the lover of wine and perfume will never be rich.

text.NRSV: The wicked is a ransom for the righteous, and the faithless for the upright.
text.Rotherham: A ransom for the righteous, is the lawless, and, instead of upright men, the traitor.
text.KJV: 21:18 The wicked shalbe a ransome for the righteous; and the transgressour for the vpright.
text.NABRE: 21:18 The wicked serve as ransom for the just, and the faithless for the upright.

text.NRSV: It is better to live in a desert land than with a contentious and fretful woman.
text.Rotherham: Better to dwell in a desert land, than with a woman, quarrelsome and provoking.
text.KJV: 21:19 It is better to dwell in the wildernesse, then with a contentious and an angry woman.
text.NABRE: 21:19 It is better to dwell in a wilderness than with a quarrelsome wife and trouble.

text.NRSV: Precious treasure and oil are in the house of the wise, but the fool devours them.
text.Rotherham: Desirable treasure and oil, are in the home of the wise, but, a man who is a dullard, will destroy it.
text.KJV: 21:20 There is treasure to be desired, and oyle in the dwelling of the wise: but a foolish man spendeth it vp.
text.NABRE: 21:20 Precious treasure and oil are in the house of the wise, but the fool consumes them.

text.NRSV: Whoever pursues righteousness and kindness will find life and honor.
text.Rotherham: He that pursueth righteousness and lovingkindness, shall find life, righteousness and honour.
text.KJV: 21:21 Hee that followeth after righteousnesse and mercy, findeth life, righteousnesse and honour.
text.NABRE: 21:21 Whoever pursues justice and kindness will find life and honor.

text.NRSV: One wise person went up against a city of the mighty and brought down the stronghold in which they trusted.
text.Rotherham: A city of heroes, doth a wise man scale, and bringeth down the strength of its confidence.
text.KJV: 21:22 A wise man scaleth the citie of the mightie, and casteth downe the strength of the confidence thereof.
text.NABRE: 21:22 The wise person storms the city of the mighty, and overthrows the stronghold in which they trust.

text.NRSV: To watch over mouth and tongue is to keep out of trouble.
text.Rotherham: He that keepeth his mouth and his tongue, keepeth, out of distresses, his soul.
text.KJV: 21:23 Whoso keepeth his mouth and his tongue, keepeth his soule from troubles.
text.NABRE: 21:23 Those who guard mouth and tongue guard themselves from trouble.

text.NRSV: “Proud,” “Haughty,” “Scoffer” are the names of those who act with overbearing pride.
text.Rotherham: A haughty insolent one—Scoffer is his name, is he that acteth in a transport of pride.
text.KJV: 21:24 Proud and haughtie scorner, is his name, who dealeth in proud wrath.
text.NABRE: 21:24 Proud, boastful—scoffer is the name: those who act with overbearing pride.

text.NRSV: The craving of the lazy person is fatal, for lazy hands refuse to labor.
text.Rotherham: The craving of the sluggard, killeth him, for his hands have refused to work.
text.KJV: 21:25 The desire of the slouthfull killeth him: for his hands refuse to labour.
text.NABRE: 21:25 The desire of sluggards will slay them, for their hands refuse to work.

text.NRSV: All day long the wicked covet, but the righteous give and do not hold back.
text.Rotherham: All the day, he greatly craveth, whereas, the righteous, giveth, and doth not spare.
text.KJV: 21:26 Hee coueteth greedily all the day long: but the righteous giueth and spareth not.
text.NABRE: 21:26 Some are consumed with avarice all the day, but the just give unsparingly.

text.NRSV: The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination; how much more when brought with evil intent.
text.Rotherham: The sacrifice of the lawless, is an abomination, how much more, when, with wickedness, he bringeth it in!
text.KJV: 21:27 The sacrifice of the wicked is abomination: how much more, when he bringeth it with a wicked minde?
text.NABRE: 21:27 The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination, the more so when they offer it with bad intent.

text.NRSV: A false witness will perish, but a good listener will testify successfully.
text.Rotherham: A false witness, shall perish, but, the man who hearkeneth, with abiding effect, shall speak.
text.KJV: 21:28 A false witnesse shall perish: but the man that heareth, speaketh constantly.
text.NABRE: 21:28 The false witness will perish, but one who listens will give lasting testimony.

text.NRSV: The wicked put on a bold face, but the upright give thought to their ways.
text.Rotherham: A lawless man emboldeneth his face, but, as for the upright, he, directeth his ways.
text.KJV: 21:29 A wicked man hardeneth his face: but as for the vpright, he directeth his way.
text.NABRE: 21:29 The face of the wicked hardens, but the upright maintains a straight course.

text.NRSV: No wisdom, no understanding, no counsel, can avail against the Lord.
text.Rotherham: There is no wisdom, nor understanding, nor counsel, to confront Yahweh.
text.KJV: 21:30 There is no wisedome, nor vnderstanding, nor counsell against the Lord.
text.NABRE: 21:30 No wisdom, no understanding, no counsel prevail against the LORD.

text.NRSV: The horse is made ready for the day of battle, but the victory belongs to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: The horse, is prepared for the day of battle, but, to Yahweh, pertaineth the victory.
text.KJV: 21:31 The horse is prepared against the day of battell: but safetie is of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 21:31 The horse is equipped for the day of battle, but victory is the LORD’s.

text.NRSV: A good name is to be chosen rather than great riches, and favor is better than silver or gold.
text.Rotherham: More choice, is a name, than great riches, beyond silver and gold, is pleasant grace.
text.KJV: 22:1 A Good name is rather to be chosen then great riches, and louing fauour rather then siluer & golde.
text.NABRE: 22:1 Chapter 22 - A good name is more desirable than great riches, and high esteem, than gold and silver.

text.NRSV: The rich and the poor have this in common: the Lord is the maker of them all.
text.Rotherham: The rich and the poor, meet together, the maker of them all, is Yahweh.
text.KJV: 22:2 The rich and poore meet together: the Lord is the maker of them all.
text.NABRE: 22:2 Rich and poor have a common bond: the LORD is the maker of them all.

text.NRSV: The clever see danger and hide; but the simple go on, and suffer for it.
text.Rotherham: A prudent man, seeth calamity, and hideth himself, but, the simple, pass on, and suffer.
text.KJV: 22:3 A prudent man foreseeth the euill, and hideth himselfe: but the simple passe on, and are punished.
text.NABRE: 22:3 The astute see an evil and hide, while the naive continue on and pay the penalty.

text.NRSV: The reward for humility and fear of the Lord is riches and honor and life.
text.Rotherham: The reward of humility, is the reverence of Yahweh, riches, and honour, and life.
text.KJV: 22:4 By humilitie and the feare of the Lord, are riches, and honour, and life.
text.NABRE: 22:4 The result of humility and fear of the LORD is riches, honor and life.

text.NRSV: Thorns and snares are in the way of the crooked; those who would keep their lives will keep far from them.
text.Rotherham: Thorns and snares, are in the way of the perverse,—he that guardeth his soul, shall be far from them.
text.KJV: 22:5 Thornes and snares are in the way of the froward: he that doeth keepe his soule, shalbe farre from them.
text.NABRE: 22:5 Thorns and snares are on the path of the crooked; those who would safeguard their lives will avoid them.

text.NRSV: Train children in the right way, and when old, they will not stray.
text.Rotherham: Train up a youth, in the direction of his duty, even when he becometh old, he will not depart from it.
text.KJV: 22:6 Traine vp a childe in the way he should goe: and when he is olde, hee will not depart from it.
text.NABRE: 22:6 Train the young in the way they should go; even when old, they will not swerve from it.

text.NRSV: The rich rule over the poor, and the borrower is the slave of the lender.
text.Rotherham: The rich, over the poor, beareth rule,—and, slave, is the borrower to the lender.
text.KJV: 22:7 The rich ruleth ouer the poore, and the borrower is seruant to the lender.
text.NABRE: 22:7 The rich rule over the poor, and the borrower is the slave of the lender.

text.NRSV: Whoever sows injustice will reap calamity, and the rod of anger will fail.
text.Rotherham: He that soweth perversity, shall reap trouble, and, the rod of his wrath, shall be ready.
text.KJV: 22:8 Hee that soweth iniquitie, shall reape vanitie: and the rodde of his anger shall faile.
text.NABRE: 22:8 Those who sow iniquity reap calamity, and the rod used in anger will fail.

text.NRSV: Those who are generous are blessed, for they share their bread with the poor.
text.Rotherham: A benevolent eye, the same, shall be blessed,—because he hath given of his bread to the poor.
text.KJV: 22:9 Hee that hath a bountifull eye, shall bee blessed: for hee giueth of his bread to the poore.
text.NABRE: 22:9 The generous will be blessed, for they share their food with the poor.

text.NRSV: Drive out a scoffer, and strife goes out; quarreling and abuse will cease.
text.Rotherham: Drive away the scoffer, that strife may depart, and quarrel, and contempt may cease.
text.KJV: 22:10 Cast out the scorner, and contention shall goe out; yea strife, and reproch shall cease.
text.NABRE: 22:10 Expel the arrogant and discord goes too; strife and insult cease.

text.NRSV: Those who love a pure heart and are gracious in speech will have the king as a friend.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh loveth the pure in heart, gracious are his lips, his friend is the king!
text.KJV: 22:11 He that loueth purenesse of heart, for the grace of his lips the king shall be his friend.
text.NABRE: 22:11 The LORD loves the pure of heart; the person of winning speech has a king for a friend.

text.NRSV: The eyes of the Lord keep watch over knowledge, but he overthrows the words of the faithless.
text.Rotherham: The eyes of Yahweh, watch over knowledge, therefore hath he overturned the words of the treacherous.
text.KJV: 22:12 The eyes of the Lord preserue knowledge, and he ouerthroweth the words of the transgressour.
text.NABRE: 22:12 The eyes of the LORD watch over the knowledgeable, but he defeats the projects of the faithless.

text.NRSV: The lazy person says, “There is a lion outside! I shall be killed in the streets!”
text.Rotherham: Saith the sluggard, A lion outside! Amidst the broadways, shall I be slain.
text.KJV: 22:13 The slothfull man sayth, There is a lyon without, I shall be slaine in the streetes.
text.NABRE: 22:13 The sluggard says, “A lion is outside; I might be slain in the street.”

text.NRSV: The mouth of a loose woman is a deep pit; he with whom the Lord is angry falls into it.
text.Rotherham: A deep chasm, is the mouth of strange women, he with whom Yahweh is indignant, falleth there.
text.KJV: 22:14 The mouth of strange women is a deepe pit: he that is abhorred of the Lord shall fall therein.
text.NABRE: 22:14 The mouth of the foreign woman is a deep pit; whoever incurs the LORD’s anger will fall into it.

text.NRSV: Folly is bound up in the heart of a boy, but the rod of discipline drives it far from him.
text.Rotherham: Foolishness, is bound up in the heart of a youth, the rod of correction, shall drive it far from him.
text.KJV: 22:15 Foolishnesse is bound in the heart of a child: but the rod of correction shal driue it farre from him.
text.NABRE: 22:15 Folly is bound to the heart of a youth, but the rod of discipline will drive it out.

text.NRSV: Oppressing the poor in order to enrich oneself, and giving to the rich, will lead only to loss.
text.Rotherham: He that oppresseth the poor, to make increase for himself, giving to the rich, shall surely come to want.
text.KJV: 22:16 Hee that oppresseth the poore to increase his riches, and he that giueth to the rich, shall surely come to want.
text.NABRE: 22:16 Oppressing the poor for enrichment, giving to the rich: both are sheer loss.

text.NRSV: The words of the wise: Incline your ear and hear my words, and apply your mind to my teaching;
text.Rotherham: Incline thine ear and hear the words of wise men, then, thy heart, wilt thou apply to my teaching;
text.KJV: 22:17 Bow downe thine eare, and heare the words of the wise, and apply thine heart vnto my knowledge.
text.NABRE: 22:17 IV. Sayings of the Wise - The Words of the Wise: Incline your ear, and hear my words, and let your mind attend to my teaching;

text.NRSV: for it will be pleasant if you keep them within you, if all of them are ready on your lips.
text.Rotherham: For sweet shall they be, when thou shalt keep them in thine inmost mind, they shall fit well together, upon thy lips.
text.KJV: 22:18 For it is a pleasant thing, if thou keepe them within thee; they shall withall be fitted in thy lippes.
text.NABRE: 22:18 For it will be well if you hold them within you, if they all are ready on your lips.

text.NRSV: So that your trust may be in the Lord, I have made them known to you today—yes, to you.
text.Rotherham: That in Yahweh may be thy trust, I have made them known to thee to-day, even to thee.
text.KJV: 22:19 That thy trust may bee in the Lord, I haue made knowen to thee this day, euen to thee.
text.NABRE: 22:19 That your trust may be in the LORD, I make them known to you today—yes, to you.

text.NRSV: Have I not written for you thirty sayings of admonition and knowledge,
text.Rotherham: Have I not written for thee noble things, with counsels and knowledge:
text.KJV: 22:20 Haue not I written to thee excellent things in counsailes and knowledge:
text.NABRE: 22:20 Have I not written for you thirty sayings, containing counsels and knowledge,

text.NRSV: to show you what is right and true, so that you may give a true answer to those who sent you?
text.Rotherham: To cause thee to know the meaning of faithful sayings, that thou mayest give back faithful sayings to them who ask thee.
text.KJV: 22:21 That I might make thee knowe the certainty of the words of truth; that thou mightest answere the words of trueth to them that send vnto thee?
text.NABRE: 22:21 To teach you truly how to give a dependable report to one who sends you?

text.NRSV: Do not rob the poor because they are poor, or crush the afflicted at the gate;
text.Rotherham: Do not rob the poor, because he is poor, neither crush thou the oppressed in the gate;
text.KJV: 22:22 Rob not the poore because he is poore, neither oppresse the afflicted in the gate.
text.NABRE: 22:22 Do not rob the poor because they are poor, nor crush the needy at the gate;

text.NRSV: for the Lord will plead their cause and despoil of life those who despoil them.
text.Rotherham: For, Yahweh, will plead their cause, and will despoil their despoilers of life.
text.KJV: 22:23 For the Lord will plead their cause, and spoile the soule of those that spoiled them.
text.NABRE: 22:23 For the LORD will defend their cause, and will plunder those who plunder them.

text.NRSV: Make no friends with those given to anger, and do not associate with hotheads,
text.Rotherham: Do not have friendship with one given to anger, and, with a wrathful man, shall thou not enter in:
text.KJV: 22:24 Make no friendship with an angrie man: and with a furious man thou shalt not goe;
text.NABRE: 22:24 Do not be friendly with hotheads, nor associate with the wrathful,

text.NRSV: or you may learn their ways and entangle yourself in a snare.
text.Rotherham: Lest thou learn his ways, and take a snare to thy soul.
text.KJV: 22:25 Lest thou learne his wayes, and get a snare to thy soule.
text.NABRE: 22:25 Lest you learn their ways, and become ensnared.

text.NRSV: Do not be one of those who give pledges, who become surety for debts.
text.Rotherham: Be not of them who strike hands, of them who are sureties for debts:
text.KJV: 22:26 Be not thou one of them that strike hands, or of them that are sureties for debts.
text.NABRE: 22:26 Do not be one of those who give their hand in pledge, those who become surety for debts;

text.NRSV: If you have nothing with which to pay, why should your bed be taken from under you?
text.Rotherham: If thou hast nothing to pay, why should one take away thy bed from under thee?
text.KJV: 22:27 If thou hast nothing to pay, why should he take away thy bed from vnder thee?
text.NABRE: 22:27 For if you are unable to pay, your bed will be taken from under you.

text.NRSV: Do not remove the ancient landmark that your ancestors set up.
text.Rotherham: Do not move back the ancient boundary, which thy father’s made.
text.KJV: 22:28 Remoue not the ancient land marke, which thy fathers haue set.
text.NABRE: 22:28 Do not remove the ancient landmark that your ancestors set up.

text.NRSV: Do you see those who are skillful in their work? They will serve kings; they will not serve common people.
text.Rotherham: Seest thou a man prompt in his business? before kings, shall he stand, he shall not stand before men who are obscure.
text.KJV: 22:29 Seest thou a man diligent in his businesse? hee shall stand before kings, he shall not stand before meane men.
text.NABRE: 22:29 Do you see those skilled at their work? They will stand in the presence of kings, but not in the presence of the obscure.

text.NRSV: When you sit down to eat with a ruler, observe carefully what is before you,
text.Rotherham: When thou sittest to eat with a ruler, thou shalt consider well, what is before thee;
text.KJV: 23:1 When thou sittest to eate with a ruler, consider diligently what is before thee.
text.NABRE: 23:1 Chapter 23 - When you sit down to dine with a ruler, mark well the one who is before you;

text.NRSV: and put a knife to your throat if you have a big appetite.
text.Rotherham: And shalt put a knife to thy throat, if, of great appetite, thou art:
text.KJV: 23:2 And put a knife to thy throate, if thou be a man giuen to appetite.
text.NABRE: 23:2 Stick the knife in your gullet if you have a ravenous appetite.

text.NRSV: Do not desire the ruler’s delicacies, for they are deceptive food.
text.Rotherham: Do not crave his dainties, for, the same, are deceitful food.
text.KJV: 23:3 Be not desirous of his dainties: for they are deceitfull meate.
text.NABRE: 23:3 Do not desire his delicacies; it is food that deceives.

text.NRSV: Do not wear yourself out to get rich; be wise enough to desist.
text.Rotherham: Do not toil to get wealth, of thine own understanding, forbear:
text.KJV: 23:4 Labour not to bee rich: cease from thine owne wisedome.
text.NABRE: 23:4 Do not wear yourself out to gain wealth, cease to be worried about it;

text.NRSV: When your eyes light upon it, it is gone; for suddenly it takes wings to itself, flying like an eagle toward heaven.
text.Rotherham: Wilt thou let thine eye fly thereupon, when it is nothing? for it will, surely make, itself wings, Like an eagle, will it wing its way across the heavens.
text.KJV: 23:5 Wilt thou set thine eyes vpon that which is not? for riches certainly make themselues wings, they fly away as an Eagle toward heauen.
text.NABRE: 23:5 When your glance flits to it, it is gone! For assuredly it grows wings, like the eagle that flies toward heaven.

text.NRSV: Do not eat the food of the begrudging, or desire their delicacies;
text.Rotherham: Do not eat the food of him that hath a begrudging eye, neither crave thou his dainties;
text.KJV: 23:6 Eate thou not the bread of him that hath an euill eye, neither desire thou his dainty meates.
text.NABRE: 23:6 Do not take food with unwilling hosts, and do not desire their delicacies;

text.NRSV: for they are like a hair in the throat. “Eat and drink!” they say to you; but they do not mean it.
text.Rotherham: For, just as he hath thought in his own mind, so, he is: Eat and drink! he may say to thee, but, his heart, is not with thee.
text.KJV: 23:7 For as he thinketh in his heart, so is he: Eate, and drinke, sayth he to thee, but his heart is not with thee.
text.NABRE: 23:7 For like something stuck in the throat is that food. “Eat and drink,” they say to you, but their hearts are not with you;

text.NRSV: You will vomit up the morsels you have eaten, and waste your pleasant words.
text.Rotherham: As for thy morsel thou hast eaten, thou shalt vomit it, so shalt thou waste thy things so sweet.
text.KJV: 23:8 The morsell which thou hast eaten, shalt thou vomite vp, and loose thy sweete words.
text.NABRE: 23:8 The little you have eaten you will vomit up, and you will have wasted your agreeable words.

text.NRSV: Do not speak in the hearing of a fool, who will only despise the wisdom of your words.
text.Rotherham: In the ears of a dullard, do not speak, for he will despise the good sense of thy words.
text.KJV: 23:9 Speake not in the eares of a foole: for hee will despise the wisedome of thy words.
text.NABRE: 23:9 Do not speak in the hearing of fools; they will despise the wisdom of your words.

text.NRSV: Do not remove an ancient landmark or encroach on the fields of orphans,
text.Rotherham: Do not move back the ancient boundary, and, into the fields of the fatherless, do not enter;
text.KJV: 23:10 Remoue not the old landmarke; and enter not into the fields of the fatherlesse.
text.NABRE: 23:10 Do not remove the ancient landmark, nor invade the fields of the fatherless;

text.NRSV: for their Redeemer is strong; he will plead their cause against you.
text.Rotherham: For, their near of kin, is strong, he, will plead their cause with thee.
text.KJV: 23:11 For their redeemer is mighty; he shall plead their cause with thee.
text.NABRE: 23:11 For their redeemer is strong; he will defend their cause against you.

text.NRSV: Apply your mind to instruction and your ear to words of knowledge.
text.Rotherham: Bring, to correction, thy heart, and thine ears, to the sayings of knowledge.
text.KJV: 23:12 Apply thine heart vnto instruction, and thine eares to the words of knowledge.
text.NABRE: 23:12 Apply your heart to instruction, and your ear to words of knowledge.

text.NRSV: Do not withhold discipline from your children; if you beat them with a rod, they will not die.
text.Rotherham: Do not withhold, from a child, correction, When thou smitest him with the rod, he shall not die:
text.KJV: 23:13 Withhold not correction from the child: for if thou beatest him with the rod, he shall not die.
text.NABRE: 23:13 Do not withhold discipline from youths; if you beat them with the rod, they will not die.

text.NRSV: If you beat them with the rod, you will save their lives from Sheol.
text.Rotherham: Thou, with the rod, shalt smite him, and, his soul from hades, shalt thou deliver.
text.KJV: 23:14 Thou shalt beate him with the rod, and shalt deliuer his soule from hell.
text.NABRE: 23:14 Beat them with the rod, and you will save them from Sheol.

text.NRSV: My child, if your heart is wise, my heart also will be glad.
text.Rotherham: My son! if thy heart be wise, my heart shall rejoice, even mine.
text.KJV: 23:15 My sonne, if thine heart be wise, my heart shall reioyce, euen mine.
text.NABRE: 23:15 My son, if your heart is wise, my heart also will rejoice;

text.NRSV: My soul will rejoice when your lips speak what is right.
text.Rotherham: So shall my reins exult, when thy lips speak the things that are right.
text.KJV: 23:16 Yea my reines shall reioyce, when thy lippes speake right things.
text.NABRE: 23:16 And my inmost being will exult, when your lips speak what is right.

text.NRSV: Do not let your heart envy sinners, but always continue in the fear of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Let not thy heart be envious of sinners, only of the reverence of Yahweh, all day long;
text.KJV: 23:17 Let not thine heart enuy sinners, but be thou in the feare of the Lord all the day long.
text.NABRE: 23:17 Do not let your heart envy sinners, but only those who always fear the LORD;

text.NRSV: Surely there is a future, and your hope will not be cut off.
text.Rotherham: For surely there is a future, and, thine expectation, shall not be cut off.
text.KJV: 23:18 For surely there is an end, and thine expectation shall not be cut off.
text.NABRE: 23:18 For you will surely have a future, and your hope will not be cut off.

text.NRSV: Hear, my child, and be wise, and direct your mind in the way.
text.Rotherham: Hear, thou, my son, and be wise, and lead forward, in duty, thy heart.
text.KJV: 23:19 Heare thou, my sonne, and be wise, and guide thine heart in the way.
text.NABRE: 23:19 Hear, my son, and be wise, and guide your heart in the right way.

text.NRSV: Do not be among winebibbers, or among gluttonous eaters of meat;
text.Rotherham: Do not be among them who tipple with wine,—among them who are gluttons;
text.KJV: 23:20 Be not amongst wine-bibbers; amongst riotous eaters of flesh.
text.NABRE: 23:20 Do not join with wine bibbers, nor with those who glut themselves on meat.

text.NRSV: for the drunkard and the glutton will come to poverty, and drowsiness will clothe them with rags.
text.Rotherham: For, the tippler and the glutton, shall come to poverty, and, rags, shall Slumber put on!
text.KJV: 23:21 For the drunkard and the glutton shall come to pouerty; and drousinesse shall cloath a man with ragges.
text.NABRE: 23:21 For drunkards and gluttons come to poverty, and lazing about clothes one in rags.

text.NRSV: Listen to your father who begot you, and do not despise your mother when she is old.
text.Rotherham: Hearken to thy father here, who begat thee, and despise not, when she is old, thy mother.
text.KJV: 23:22 Hearken vnto thy father that begate thee, and despise not thy mother when she is old.
text.NABRE: 23:22 Listen to your father who begot you, do not despise your mother when she is old.

text.NRSV: Buy truth, and do not sell it; buy wisdom, instruction, and understanding.
text.Rotherham: Truth, buy thou, but do not sell, wisdom, and correction, and understanding.
text.KJV: 23:23 Buy the trueth, and sell it not; also wisedome and instruction and vnderstanding.
text.NABRE: 23:23 Buy truth and do not sell: wisdom, instruction, understanding!

text.NRSV: The father of the righteous will greatly rejoice; he who begets a wise son will be glad in him.
text.Rotherham: Greatly shall exult, the father of a righteous man, and, he that begetteth a wise son, shall rejoice in him:
text.KJV: 23:24 The father of the righteous shall greatly reioyce: and he that begetteth a wise child, shall haue ioy of him.
text.NABRE: 23:24 The father of a just person will exult greatly; whoever begets a wise son will rejoice in him.

text.NRSV: Let your father and mother be glad; let her who bore you rejoice.
text.Rotherham: Rejoice shall thy father and thy mother, yea she, shall exult, who bare thee.
text.KJV: 23:25 Thy father and thy mother shall be glad, and she that bare thee shall reioyce.
text.NABRE: 23:25 Let your father and mother rejoice; let her who bore you exult.

text.NRSV: My child, give me your heart, and let your eyes observe my ways.
text.Rotherham: Oh give, my son, thy mind unto me, and let, thine eyes, observe, my ways;
text.KJV: 23:26 My sonne, giue me thine heart, and let thine eyes obserue my wayes.
text.NABRE: 23:26 My son, give me your heart, and let your eyes keep to my ways,

text.NRSV: For a prostitute is a deep pit; an adulteress is a narrow well.
text.Rotherham: For, a deep chasm, is the unchaste woman, and, a narrow pit, the female unknown;
text.KJV: 23:27 For an whore is a deepe ditch; and a strange woman is a narrow pit.
text.NABRE: 23:27 For the harlot is a deep pit, and the foreign woman a narrow well;

text.NRSV: She lies in wait like a robber and increases the number of the faithless.
text.Rotherham: Yea, she, as for prey, lieth in wait, and, the treacherous among mankind, she causeth to abound.
text.KJV: 23:28 She also lyeth in wait as for a pray, and increaseth the transgressours among men.
text.NABRE: 23:28 Yes, she lies in wait like a robber, and increases the number of the faithless.

text.NRSV: Who has woe? Who has sorrow? Who has strife? Who has complaining? Who has wounds without cause? Who has redness of eyes?
text.Rotherham: Who hath woe? Who hath outcry of pain? Who hath contentions? Who hath complaining? Who hath needless wounds? Who hath dullness of eyes?
text.KJV: 23:29 Who hath woe? who hath sorrow? who hath contentions? who hath babbling? who hath wounds without cause? who hath rednesse of eyes?
text.NABRE: 23:29 Who scream? Who shout? Who have strife? Who have anxiety? Who have wounds for nothing? Who have bleary eyes?

text.NRSV: Those who linger late over wine, those who keep trying mixed wine.
text.Rotherham: They who tarry over wine, they who go in to search for mixed wine.
text.KJV: 23:30 They that tarry long at the wine, they that goe to seeke mixt wine.
text.NABRE: 23:30 Whoever linger long over wine, whoever go around quaffing wine.

text.NRSV: Do not look at wine when it is red, when it sparkles in the cup and goes down smoothly.
text.Rotherham: Do not look on wine when it becometh red, when it giveth in the cup its sparkle, glideth down smoothly.
text.KJV: 23:31 Looke not thou vpon the wine when it is red, when it giueth his colour in the cup, when it moueth it selfe aright.
text.NABRE: 23:31 Do not look on the wine when it is red, when it sparkles in the cup. It goes down smoothly,

text.NRSV: At the last it bites like a serpent, and stings like an adder.
text.Rotherham: Its after effect, is that, like a serpent, it biteth, and, like a viper, it doth sting.
text.KJV: 23:32 At the last it biteth like a serpent, and stingeth like an adder.
text.NABRE: 23:32 but in the end it bites like a serpent, and stings like an adder.

text.NRSV: Your eyes will see strange things, and your mind utter perverse things.
text.Rotherham: Thine eyes, will see strange women, and, thy heart, will speak perverse things:
text.KJV: 23:33 Thine eyes shall behold strange women, and thine heart shall vtter peruerse things.
text.NABRE: 23:33 Your eyes behold strange sights, and your heart utters incoherent things;

text.NRSV: You will be like one who lies down in the midst of the sea, like one who lies on the top of a mast.
text.Rotherham: So shalt thou become, as one lying down in the heart of the sea,—or as one lying down on the top of the mastgear:
text.KJV: 23:34 Yea thou shalt be as he that lyeth downe in the midst of the sea, or as he that lyeth vpon the top of a mast.
text.NABRE: 23:34 You are like one sleeping on the high seas, sprawled at the top of the mast.

text.NRSV: “They struck me,” you will say, “but I was not hurt; they beat me, but I did not feel it. When shall I awake? I will seek another drink.”
text.Rotherham: They smote me—I felt no pain, They struck me down—I noticed it not,—When shall I wake up? I will go on, I will seek it, again!
text.KJV: 23:35 They haue striken me, shalt thou say, and I was not sicke: they haue beaten me, and I felt it not: when shall I awake? I will seeke it yet againe.
text.NABRE: 23:35 “They struck me, but it did not pain me; they beat me, but I did not feel it. When can I get up, when can I go out and get more?”

text.NRSV: Do not envy the wicked, nor desire to be with them;
text.Rotherham: Be not thou envious of wicked men, neither crave to be with them;
text.KJV: 24:1 Be not thou enuious against euill men, neither desire to be with them.
text.NABRE: 24:1 Chapter 24 - Do not envy the wicked, nor desire to be with them;

text.NRSV: for their minds devise violence, and their lips talk of mischief.
text.Rotherham: For, violence, their heart muttereth, and, mischief, their lips do speak.
text.KJV: 24:2 For their heart studieth destruction, and their lippes talke of mischiefe.
text.NABRE: 24:2 For their hearts plot violence, and their lips speak of foul play.

text.NRSV: By wisdom a house is built, and by understanding it is established;
text.Rotherham: In wisdom, is a house builded, and, in understanding, is it established;
text.KJV: 24:3 Through wisedome is an house builded, and by vnderstanding it is established.
text.NABRE: 24:3 By wisdom a house is built, by understanding it is established;

text.NRSV: by knowledge the rooms are filled with all precious and pleasant riches.
text.Rotherham: And, in knowledge, chambers are filled, with all acquisitions, costly and fair.
text.KJV: 24:4 And by knowledge shall the chambers bee filled with all precious and pleasant riches.
text.NABRE: 24:4 And by knowledge its rooms are filled with every precious and pleasing possession.

text.NRSV: Wise warriors are better than strong ones, and those who have knowledge than those who have might;
text.Rotherham: A wise man, is mighty, and, a man of knowledge, becometh alert in vigour.
text.KJV: 24:5 A wise man is strong, yea a man of knowledge encreaseth strength.
text.NABRE: 24:5 The wise are more powerful than the strong, and the learned, than the mighty,

text.NRSV: for by wise guidance you can wage your war, and in abundance of counselors there is victory.
text.Rotherham: Surely, with concerted measures, shalt thou make for thyself war, and, success, lieth in the greatness of the counsellor.
text.KJV: 24:6 For by wise counsell thou shalt make thy warre: and in multitude of counsellers there is safetie.
text.NABRE: 24:6 For by strategy war is waged, and victory depends on many counselors.

text.NRSV: Wisdom is too high for fools; in the gate they do not open their mouths.
text.Rotherham: Unattainable to a foolish man, are the dictates of wisdom, in the gate, he openeth not his mouth.
text.KJV: 24:7 Wisedome is too high for a foole: he openeth not his mouth in the gate.
text.NABRE: 24:7 Wise words are beyond fools’ reach, in the assembly they do not open their mouth;

text.NRSV: Whoever plans to do evil will be called a mischief-maker.
text.Rotherham: He that deviseth to do mischief, him, shall men call, a master of plots.
text.KJV: 24:8 He that deuiseth to doe euill, shall be called a mischieuous person.
text.NABRE: 24:8 As they calculate how to do evil, people brand them troublemakers.

text.NRSV: The devising of folly is sin, and the scoffer is an abomination to all.
text.Rotherham: The purpose of folly, is sin, and, an abomination to mankind, is a buffoon.
text.KJV: 24:9 The thought of foolishnesse is sinne: and the scorner is an abomination to men.
text.NABRE: 24:9 The scheme of a fool gains no acceptance, the scoffer is an abomination to the community.

text.NRSV: If you falter in the day of adversity, your strength is small.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast been slothful in the day of straitness, Strait, is thy strength.
text.KJV: 24:10 If thou faint in the day of aduersitie, thy strength is small:
text.NABRE: 24:10 Did you fail in a day of adversity, did your strength fall short?

text.NRSV: Rescue those who are being taken away to death; hold back those who are stumbling to the slaughter.
text.Rotherham: Deliver thou such as are being led forth to death, and, them who are tottering to slaughter, oh that thou wouldst hold back!
text.KJV: 24:11 If thou forbeare to deliuer them that are drawen vnto death, and those that are ready to be slaine:
text.NABRE: 24:11 Did you fail to rescue those who were being dragged off to death, those tottering, those near death,

text.NRSV: If you say, “But we did not know this”— does not he who weighs the heart perceive it? Does not he who keeps watch over your soul know it? And will he not repay all according to their deeds?
text.Rotherham: Though thou say, Lo! we knew not this, Shall not, he that proveth hearts, himself, discern? And, he that formeth thy soul, himself, know? and bring back to a son of earth according to his deed?
text.KJV: 24:12 If thou sayest, Behold, we knew it not: doth not he that pondereth the heart, consider it? and he that keepeth thy soule, doth not he know it? and shall not hee render to euery man according to his workes?
text.NABRE: 24:12 because you said, “We didn’t know about it”? Surely, the Searcher of hearts knows and will repay all according to their deeds.

text.NRSV: My child, eat honey, for it is good, and the drippings of the honeycomb are sweet to your taste.
text.Rotherham: My son, eat thou honey, because it is good,—and droppings from the comb [because they are] sweet to thy palate:
text.KJV: 24:13 My sonne, eate thou honie, because it is good, and the honie combe, which is sweete to thy taste.
text.NABRE: 24:13 If you eat honey, my son, because it is good, if pure honey is sweet to your taste,

text.NRSV: Know that wisdom is such to your soul; if you find it, you will have a future, and your hope will not be cut off.
text.Rotherham: Thus, take knowledge of wisdom, for thine own soul; If thou find it, then there is a future, and, thine expectation, shall not be cut off.
text.KJV: 24:14 So shall the knowledge of wisedome be vnto thy soule: when thou hast found it, then there shall be a reward, and thy expectation shall not be cut off.
text.NABRE: 24:14 Such, you must know, is wisdom to your soul. If you find it, you will have a future, and your hope will not be cut off.

text.NRSV: Do not lie in wait like a wicked person against the home of the righteous; do no violence to their place of rest;
text.Rotherham: Do not lie in wait, thou lawless man, against the home of the righteous,—neither destroy thou his place of rest;
text.KJV: 24:15 Lay not waite, (O wicked man) against the dwelling of the righteous: spoile not his resting place.
text.NABRE: 24:15 Do not lie in wait at the abode of the just, do not ravage their dwelling places;

text.NRSV: for though they fall seven times, they will rise again; but the wicked are overthrown by calamity.
text.Rotherham: For, seven times, may the righteous fall and yet arise, but, lawless men, shall stumble into calamity.
text.KJV: 24:16 For a iust man falleth seuen times, and riseth vp againe: but the wicked shall fall into mischiefe.
text.NABRE: 24:16 Though the just fall seven times, they rise again, but the wicked stumble from only one mishap.

text.NRSV: Do not rejoice when your enemies fall, and do not let your heart be glad when they stumble,
text.Rotherham: When thine enemy falleth, do not thou rejoice, and, when he stumbleth, let not thy heart exult:
text.KJV: 24:17 Reioyce not when thine enemie falleth: and let not thine heart be glad when he stumbleth:
text.NABRE: 24:17 Do not rejoice when your enemies fall, and when they stumble, do not let your heart exult,

text.NRSV: or the Lord will see it and be displeased, and turn through his anger from them.
text.Rotherham: Lest Yahweh see it, and it be wicked in his eyes, and he turn away from him his anger.
text.KJV: 24:18 Lest the Lord see it, and it displease him, and hee turne away his wrath from him.
text.NABRE: 24:18 Lest the LORD see it, be displeased with you, and withdraw his wrath from your enemies.

text.NRSV: Do not fret because of evildoers. Do not envy the wicked;
text.Rotherham: Burn not with vexation against evil doers, be not envious of lawless men;
text.KJV: 24:19 Fret not thy selfe because of euill men; neither be thou enuious at the wicked.
text.NABRE: 24:19 Do not be provoked at evildoers, do not envy the wicked;

text.NRSV: for the evil have no future; the lamp of the wicked will go out.
text.Rotherham: For there shall be no future for the wicked, The lamp of the lawless, shall go out.
text.KJV: 24:20 For there shall be no reward to the euill man: the candle of the wicked shall be put out.
text.NABRE: 24:20 For the evil have no future, the lamp of the wicked will be put out.

text.NRSV: My child, fear the Lord and the king, and do not confide in rebels;
text.Rotherham: Revere thou Yahweh, my son, and the king, and, with the fickle, have thou no fellowship;
text.KJV: 24:21 My sonne, feare thou the Lord, and the king: and medle not with them that are giuen to change.
text.NABRE: 24:21 My son, fear the LORD and the king; have nothing to do with those who hate them;

text.NRSV: for disaster from them will rise suddenly, and who knows the ruin that will come from them both?
text.Rotherham: For, suddenly, shall arise their calamity; and, the misfortune of their years, who knoweth?
text.KJV: 24:22 For their calamity shall rise suddenly, and who knoweth the ruine of them both?
text.NABRE: 24:22 For disaster will issue suddenly, and calamity from them both, who knows when?

text.NRSV: These also are sayings of the wise: Partiality in judging is not good.
text.Rotherham: These things also, concern the wise, To take note of faces in judgment, is not good.
text.KJV: 24:23 These things also belong to the wise: It is not good to haue respect of persons in iudgement.
text.NABRE: 24:23 V. Further Sayings of the Wise - These also are Words of the Wise: To show partiality in judgment is not good.

text.NRSV: Whoever says to the wicked, “You are innocent,” will be cursed by peoples, abhorred by nations;
text.Rotherham: He that saith to the lawless man, Righteous, thou art, peoples shall denounce him, populations shall curse him;
text.KJV: 24:24 He that sayth vnto the wicked, Thou art righteous, him shall the people curse; nations shall abhorre him:
text.NABRE: 24:24 Whoever says to the guilty party, “You are innocent,” will be cursed by nations, scorned by peoples;

text.NRSV: but those who rebuke the wicked will have delight, and a good blessing will come upon them.
text.Rotherham: But, to reprovers, one should be pleasant, and, upon them, should come an excellent blessing:
text.KJV: 24:25 But to them that rebuke him shall be delight, and a good blessing shall come vpon them.
text.NABRE: 24:25 But those who render just verdicts will fare well, and on them will come the blessing of prosperity.

text.NRSV: One who gives an honest answer gives a kiss on the lips.
text.Rotherham: Lips, should one kiss with one who answereth in right words.
text.KJV: 24:26 Euery man shall kisse his lippes that giueth a right answere.
text.NABRE: 24:26 An honest reply— a kiss on the lips.

text.NRSV: Prepare your work outside, get everything ready for you in the field; and after that build your house.
text.Rotherham: Prepare, in the open, thy work, and make ready, in the field, for thyself, Afterwards, shalt thou build thy house.
text.KJV: 24:27 Prepare thy worke without, and make it fit for thy selfe in the field; and afterwards build thine house.
text.NABRE: 24:27 Complete your outdoor tasks, and arrange your work in the field; afterward you can build your house.

text.NRSV: Do not be a witness against your neighbor without cause, and do not deceive with your lips.
text.Rotherham: Do not become a needless witness against thy neighbour, so mightest thou open too wide thy lips:
text.KJV: 24:28 Be not a witnesse against thy neighbour without cause: and deceiue not with thy lippes.
text.NABRE: 24:28 Do not testify falsely against your neighbor and so deceive with your lips.

text.NRSV: Do not say, “I will do to others as they have done to me; I will pay them back for what they have done.”
text.Rotherham: Do not say—According to what he hath done to me, so, will I do to him, I will repay every one according to his work.
text.KJV: 24:29 Say not, I will doe so to him as he hath done to mee: I will render to the man according to his worke.
text.NABRE: 24:29 Do not say, “As they did to me, so will I do to them; I will repay them according to their deeds.”

text.NRSV: I passed by the field of one who was lazy, by the vineyard of a person without sense;
text.Rotherham: By the field of the sluggard, I passed, and by the vineyard of a man lacking sense;
text.KJV: 24:30 I went by the field of the slouthfull, and by the vineyard of the man voyd of vnderstanding:
text.NABRE: 24:30 I passed by the field of a sluggard, by the vineyard of one with no sense;

text.NRSV: and see, it was all overgrown with thorns; the ground was covered with nettles, and its stone wall was broken down.
text.Rotherham: And lo! there had come up all over it—thorns, there had covered the face thereof—thistles, and, the stone fence thereof, had been thrown down.
text.KJV: 24:31 And loe, it was all growen ouer with thornes, and nettles had couered the face thereof, and the stone wall therof was broken downe:
text.NABRE: 24:31 It was all overgrown with thistles; its surface was covered with nettles, and its stone wall broken down.

text.NRSV: Then I saw and considered it; I looked and received instruction.
text.Rotherham: So I observed it, for myself, I applied my heart, I looked—I accepted correction:
text.KJV: 24:32 Then I saw, and considered it well, I looked vpon it, and receiued instruction.
text.NABRE: 24:32 As I gazed at it, I reflected; I saw and learned a lesson:

text.NRSV: A little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to rest,
text.Rotherham: A little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to rest:
text.KJV: 24:33 Yet a little sleepe, a little slumber, a little folding of the handes to sleepe:
text.NABRE: 24:33 A little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the arms to rest—

text.NRSV: and poverty will come upon you like a robber, and want like an armed warrior.
text.Rotherham: So shall come in, as a highwayman, thy poverty, and, thy want, as one armed with a shield.
text.KJV: 24:34 So shall thy pouertie come, as one that traueileth, and thy want, as an armed man.
text.NABRE: 24:34 Then poverty will come upon you like a robber, and want like a brigand.

text.NRSV: These are other proverbs of Solomon that the officials of King Hezekiah of Judah copied.
text.Rotherham: These also, are proverbs of Solomon,—which the men of Hezekiah king of Judah transcribed.
text.KJV: 25:1 These are also Prouerbes of Solomon, which the men of Hezekiah king of Iudah copied out.
text.NABRE: 25:1 VI. Second Solomonic Collection, Collected Under King Hezekiah Chapter 25 - These also are proverbs of Solomon. The servants of Hezekiah, king of Judah, transmitted them.

text.NRSV: It is the glory of God to conceal things, but the glory of kings is to search things out.
text.Rotherham: The glory of God, is to conceal a thing, but, the glory of kings, is to search out a thing.
text.KJV: 25:2 It is the glory of God to conceale a thing: but the honour of Kings is to search out a matter.
text.NABRE: 25:2 It is the glory of God to conceal a matter, and the glory of kings to fathom a matter.

text.NRSV: Like the heavens for height, like the earth for depth, so the mind of kings is unsearchable.
text.Rotherham: The heavens for height, and the earth for depth, but, the heart of kings, cannot be searched.
text.KJV: 25:3 The heauen for height, and the earth for depth, and the heart of Kings is vnsearchable.
text.NABRE: 25:3 Like the heavens in height, and the earth in depth, the heart of kings is unfathomable.

text.NRSV: Take away the dross from the silver, and the smith has a vessel for it;
text.Rotherham: Remove the dross from the silver, and there cometh forth, to the refiner, a vessel:
text.KJV: 25:4 Take away the drosse from the siluer, and there shall come foorth a vessell for the finer.
text.NABRE: 25:4 Remove the dross from silver, and it comes forth perfectly purified;

text.NRSV: take away the wicked from the presence of the king, and his throne will be established in righteousness.
text.Rotherham: Remove a lawless man from before the king, that his throne, may be established in righteousness.
text.KJV: 25:5 Take away the wicked from before the king, and his throne shalbe established in righteousnes.
text.NABRE: 25:5 Remove the wicked from the presence of the king, and his throne is made firm through justice.

text.NRSV: Do not put yourself forward in the king’s presence or stand in the place of the great;
text.Rotherham: Do not honour thyself before a king, nor, in the place of great men, do thou stand;
text.KJV: 25:6 Put not forth thy selfe in the presence of the king, and stand not in the place of great men.
text.NABRE: 25:6 Claim no honor in the king’s presence, nor occupy the place of superiors;

text.NRSV: for it is better to be told, “Come up here,” than to be put lower in the presence of a noble. What your eyes have seen
text.Rotherham: For better it be said to thee, Come up hither,—than that thou be put lower down before a noble, whom thine own eyes, have beheld.
text.KJV: 25:7 For better it is that it be said vnto thee, Come vp hither; then that thou shouldest be put lower in the presence of the Prince whom thine eies haue seene.
text.NABRE: 25:7 For it is better to be told, “Come up closer!” than to be humbled before the prince.

text.NRSV: do not hastily bring into court; for what will you do in the end, when your neighbor puts you to shame?
text.Rotherham: Do not go forth to strive in haste,—lest [thou know not] what to do in the latter end thereof, when thy neighbour, hath put thee to shame.
text.KJV: 25:8 Goe not forth hastily to striue, lest thou know not what to doe in the ende thereof, when thy neighbour hath put thee to shame.
text.NABRE: 25:8 What your eyes have seen do not bring forth too quickly against an opponent; For what will you do later on when your neighbor puts you to shame?

text.NRSV: Argue your case with your neighbor directly, and do not disclose another’s secret;
text.Rotherham: Thy contention, urge thou with thy neighbour, and, the secret of another, do not reveal:
text.KJV: 25:9 Debate thy cause with thy neighbour himselfe; and discouer not a secret to another:
text.NABRE: 25:9 Argue your own case with your neighbor, but the secrets of others do not disclose;

text.NRSV: or he who hears you will bring shame upon you, and your ill repute will have no end.
text.Rotherham: Lest he that heareth expose thee, and, the report concerning thee, turn not away.
text.KJV: 25:10 Lest he that heareth it, put thee to shame, and thine infamie turne not away.
text.NABRE: 25:10 Lest, hearing it, they reproach you, and your ill repute never ceases.

text.NRSV: A word fitly spoken is like apples of gold in a setting of silver.
text.Rotherham: Golden fruit in figured silver baskets, is a word spoken on fitting occasion.
text.KJV: 25:11 A word fitly spoken is like apples of gold in pictures of siluer.
text.NABRE: 25:11 Golden apples in silver settings are words spoken at the proper time.

text.NRSV: Like a gold ring or an ornament of gold is a wise rebuke to a listening ear.
text.Rotherham: A ring of gold, and a vessel of precious metal, is a wise reprover, on a hearing ear.
text.KJV: 25:12 As an eare-ring of gold, and an ornament of fine gold, so is a wise reprouer vpon an obedient eare.
text.NABRE: 25:12 A golden earring or a necklace of fine gold— one who gives wise reproof to a listening ear.

text.NRSV: Like the cold of snow in the time of harvest are faithful messengers to those who send them; they refresh the spirit of their masters.
text.Rotherham: As the cold of snow in the day of harvest, is a faithful messenger to them who send him,—when, the life of his masters, he restoreth.
text.KJV: 25:13 As the cold of snow in the time of haruest, so is a faithfull messenger to them that send him: for hee refresheth the soule of his masters.
text.NABRE: 25:13 Like the coolness of snow in the heat of the harvest are faithful messengers for those who send them, lifting the spirits of their masters.

text.NRSV: Like clouds and wind without rain is one who boasts of a gift never given.
text.Rotherham: Clouds and wind, when rain there is none, is the man who boasteth himself of a pretended gift.
text.KJV: 25:14 Who so boasteth himselfe of a false gift, is like cloudes and winde without raine.
text.NABRE: 25:14 Clouds and wind but no rain— the one who boasts of a gift not given.

text.NRSV: With patience a ruler may be persuaded, and a soft tongue can break a bone.
text.Rotherham: By long patience, is a judge persuaded, and, a soft tongue, breaketh the bone.
text.KJV: 25:15 By long forbearing is a Prince perswaded, and a soft tongue breaketh the bone.
text.NABRE: 25:15 By patience is a ruler persuaded, and a soft tongue can break a bone.

text.NRSV: If you have found honey, eat only enough for you, or else, having too much, you will vomit it.
text.Rotherham: Honey having found, eat to suffice thee, lest thou loathe it, and vomit it forth.
text.KJV: 25:16 Hast thou found hony? eate so much as is sufficient for thee: lest thou be filled therewith, and vomit it.
text.NABRE: 25:16 If you find honey, eat only what you need, lest you have your fill and vomit it up.

text.NRSV: Let your foot be seldom in your neighbor’s house, otherwise the neighbor will become weary of you and hate you.
text.Rotherham: Withhold thy foot from the house of thy neighbour,—lest he be weary of thee, and hate thee.
text.KJV: 25:17 Withdraw thy foote from thy neighbours house: lest he be weary of thee, and so hate thee.
text.NABRE: 25:17 Let your foot be seldom in your neighbors’ house, lest they have their fill of you—and hate you.

text.NRSV: Like a war club, a sword, or a sharp arrow is one who bears false witness against a neighbor.
text.Rotherham: A hammer and a sword, and a sharpened arrow, is a man becoming a false witness against his neighbour.
text.KJV: 25:18 A man that beareth false witnes against his neighbour, is a maule, and a sword, and a sharpe arrow.
text.NABRE: 25:18 A club, sword, or sharp arrow— the one who bears false witness against a neighbor.

text.NRSV: Like a bad tooth or a lame foot is trust in a faithless person in time of trouble.
text.Rotherham: A broken tooth and a faltering foot, is confidence in the treacherous, in the day of danger.
text.KJV: 25:19 Confidence in an vnfaithfull man in time of trouble, is like a broken tooth, and a foot out of ioynt.
text.NABRE: 25:19 A bad tooth or an unsteady foot— a trust betrayed in time of trouble.

text.NRSV: Like one who takes off a garment on a cold day, and like vinegar on a wound, is one who sings songs to a heavy heart. Like a moth in clothing or a worm in wood, sorrow gnaws at the human heart.
text.Rotherham: As splendour of dress on a cold day—vinegar upon nitre, so is a singer with songs, unto a sad heart.
text.KJV: 25:20 As hee that taketh away a garment in cold weather; and as vineger vpon nitre; so is he that singeth songs to an heauy heart.
text.NABRE: 25:20 Like the removal of clothes on a cold day, or vinegar on soda, is the one who sings to a troubled heart.

text.NRSV: If your enemies are hungry, give them food to eat; and if they are thirsty, give them water to drink;
text.Rotherham: If he that hateth thee hunger, give him bread to eat, and, if he be thirsty, give him water to drink;
text.KJV: 25:21 If thine enemie be hungry, giue him bread to eate: and if hee be thirstie, giue him water to drinke.
text.NABRE: 25:21 If your enemies are hungry, give them food to eat, if thirsty, give something to drink;

text.NRSV: for you will heap burning coals on their heads, and the Lord will reward you.
text.Rotherham: For, burning coals, shalt thou be heaping upon his head,—and, Yahweh, will repay thee.
text.KJV: 25:22 For thou shalt heape coales of fire vpon his head, and the Lord shall reward thee.
text.NABRE: 25:22 For live coals you will heap on their heads, and the LORD will vindicate you.

text.NRSV: The north wind produces rain, and a backbiting tongue, angry looks.
text.Rotherham: A north wind, bringeth forth rain, and, a face stirred with indignation, a secretive tongue.
text.KJV: 25:23 The North winde driueth away raine: so doeth an angrie countenance a backbiting tongue.
text.NABRE: 25:23 The north wind brings rain, and a backbiting tongue, angry looks.

text.NRSV: It is better to live in a corner of the housetop than in a share of a house with a contentious woman.
text.Rotherham: Better to dwell on the corner of the roof, than a quarrelsome wife, and a house in common.
text.KJV: 25:24 It is better to dwell in a corner of the house top, then with a brawling woman, and in a wide house.
text.NABRE: 25:24 It is better to dwell in a corner of the housetop than in a mansion with a quarrelsome wife.

text.NRSV: Like cold water to a thirsty soul, so is good news from a far country.
text.Rotherham: As cold water to a thirsty soul, so is a good report from a far country.
text.KJV: 25:25 As cold waters to a thirstie soule: so is good newes from a farre countrey.
text.NABRE: 25:25 Cool water to one faint from thirst is good news from a far country.

text.NRSV: Like a muddied spring or a polluted fountain are the righteous who give way before the wicked.
text.Rotherham: A fountain fouled, a spring spoiled, is a righteous man tottering before one who is lawless.
text.KJV: 25:26 A righteous man falling downe before the wicked, is as a troubled fountaine, aud a corrupt spring.
text.NABRE: 25:26 A trampled fountain or a polluted spring— a just person fallen before the wicked.

text.NRSV: It is not good to eat much honey, or to seek honor on top of honor.
text.Rotherham: To eat honey in abundance, is not good, nor is, searching out their own honour, an honourable thing.
text.KJV: 25:27 It is not good to eat much hony: so for men to search their owne glory, is not glory.
text.NABRE: 25:27 To eat too much honey is not good; nor to seek honor after honor.

text.NRSV: Like a city breached, without walls, is one who lacks self-control.
text.Rotherham: A city broken down without a wall, is a man who hath no control over his own spirit.
text.KJV: 25:28 Hee that hath no rule ouer his owne spirit, is like a citie that is broken downe, and without walles.
text.NABRE: 25:28 A city breached and left defenseless are those who do not control their temper.

text.NRSV: Like snow in summer or rain in harvest, so honor is not fitting for a fool.
text.Rotherham: As snow in summer, and as rain in harvest, so, unbecoming to a dullard is honour.
text.KJV: 26:1 As snow in summer, and as raine in haruest: so honour is not seemely for a foole.
text.NABRE: 26:1 Chapter 26 - Like snow in summer, like rain in harvest, honor for a fool is out of place.

text.NRSV: Like a sparrow in its flitting, like a swallow in its flying, an undeserved curse goes nowhere.
text.Rotherham: As a sparrow in wandering, as a swallow in flying, so, a causeless curse, shall not alight.
text.KJV: 26:2 As the bird by wandring, as the swallow by flying: so the curse causelesse shall not come.
text.NABRE: 26:2 Like the sparrow in its flitting, like the swallow in its flight, a curse uncalled-for never lands.

text.NRSV: A whip for the horse, a bridle for the donkey, and a rod for the back of fools.
text.Rotherham: A whip for the horse, a bridle for the ass, and a rod for the back of dullards.
text.KJV: 26:3 A whip for the horse, a bridle for the asse; and a rod for the fooles backe.
text.NABRE: 26:3 The whip for the horse, the bridle for the ass, and the rod for the back of fools.

text.NRSV: Do not answer fools according to their folly, or you will be a fool yourself.
text.Rotherham: Do not answer a dullard, according to his folly, lest, even thou thyself, become like him;
text.KJV: 26:4 Answere not a foole according to his folly, lest thou also be like vnto him.
text.NABRE: 26:4 Do not answer fools according to their folly, lest you too become like them.

text.NRSV: Answer fools according to their folly, or they will be wise in their own eyes.
text.Rotherham: Answer a dullard according to his folly, lest he become wise in his own eyes.
text.KJV: 26:5 Answere a foole according to his folly, lest hee be wise in his owne conceit.
text.NABRE: 26:5 Answer fools according to their folly, lest they become wise in their own eyes.

text.NRSV: It is like cutting off one’s foot and drinking down violence, to send a message by a fool.
text.Rotherham: One who cutteth off feet, one who drinketh down wrong, is he who sendeth a message by the hand of a dullard.
text.KJV: 26:6 He that sendeth a message by the hand of a foole, cutteth off the feete, and drinketh dammage.
text.NABRE: 26:6 Those who send messages by a fool cut off their feet; they drink down violence.

text.NRSV: The legs of a disabled person hang limp; so does a proverb in the mouth of a fool.
text.Rotherham: Useless are the legs of the lame, and a proverb, in the mouth of a dullard.
text.KJV: 26:7 The legges of the lame are not equall: so is a parable in the mouth of fooles.
text.NABRE: 26:7 A proverb in the mouth of a fool hangs limp, like crippled legs.

text.NRSV: It is like binding a stone in a sling to give honor to a fool.
text.Rotherham: Like tying a stone to a sling, so, is he that giveth honour, to a dullard.
text.KJV: 26:8 As hee that bindeth a stone in a sling; so is hee that giueth honour to a foole.
text.NABRE: 26:8 Giving honor to a fool is like entangling a stone in the sling.

text.NRSV: Like a thornbush brandished by the hand of a drunkard is a proverb in the mouth of a fool.
text.Rotherham: A brier cometh into the hand of a drunken-man, a proverb into the mouth of dullards.
text.KJV: 26:9 As a thorne goeth vp into the hand of a drunkard; so is a parable in the mouth of fooles.
text.NABRE: 26:9 A thorn stuck in the hand of a drunkard is a proverb in the mouth of fools.

text.NRSV: Like an archer who wounds everybody is one who hires a passing fool or drunkard.
text.Rotherham: [As] an archer who woundeth every thing, [so] one who hireth a dullard, and a drunkard crossing the sea.
text.KJV: 26:10 The great God that formed all things, both rewardeth the foole, and rewardeth transgressours.
text.NABRE: 26:10 An archer wounding all who pass by is anyone who hires a drunken fool.

text.NRSV: Like a dog that returns to its vomit is a fool who reverts to his folly.
text.Rotherham: As, a dog, returneth onto his own vomit, a dullard, repeateth his folly.
text.KJV: 26:11 As a dogge returneth to his vomite: so a foole returneth to his folly.
text.NABRE: 26:11 As dogs return to their vomit, so fools repeat their folly.

text.NRSV: Do you see persons wise in their own eyes? There is more hope for fools than for them.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast seen a man wise in his own eye,—more hope of a dullard, than of him!
text.KJV: 26:12 Seest thou a man wise in his owne conceit? there is more hope of a foole then of him.
text.NABRE: 26:12 You see those who are wise in their own eyes? There is more hope for fools than for them.

text.NRSV: The lazy person says, “There is a lion in the road! There is a lion in the streets!”
text.Rotherham: Saith the sluggard, A roaring lion in the road! A tearing lion in the midst of the broadways.
text.KJV: 26:13 The slothfull man sayth, There is a lion in the way, a lion is in the streets.
text.NABRE: 26:13 The sluggard says, “There is a lion in the street, a lion in the middle of the square!”

text.NRSV: As a door turns on its hinges, so does a lazy person on a bed.
text.Rotherham: The door, turneth on its hinges, and, the sluggard, upon his bed.
text.KJV: 26:14 As the doore turneth vpon his hinges: so doeth the slothfull vpon his bedde.
text.NABRE: 26:14 The door turns on its hinges and sluggards, on their beds.

text.NRSV: The lazy person buries a hand in the dish, and is too tired to bring it back to the mouth.
text.Rotherham: The sluggard burieth his hand in the dish, he is too lazy to bring it back to his mouth.
text.KJV: 26:15 The slothfull hideth his hand in his bosome, it grieueth him to bring it againe to his mouth.
text.NABRE: 26:15 The sluggard buries a hand in the dish, too weary to lift it to the mouth.

text.NRSV: The lazy person is wiser in self-esteem than seven who can answer discreetly.
text.Rotherham: Wiser is the sluggard in his own eyes, than, seven persons, who can answer with judgment.
text.KJV: 26:16 The sluggard is wiser in his owne conceit, then seuen men that can render a reason.
text.NABRE: 26:16 In their own eyes sluggards are wiser than seven who answer with good judgment.

text.NRSV: Like one who seizes a passing dog by the ears is one who meddles in the quarrel of another.
text.Rotherham: As he who layeth hold of the ears of a dog, is a passer-by, who giveth vent to his wrath over a quarrel, not his!
text.KJV: 26:17 He that passeth by, and medleth with strife belonging not to him, is like one that taketh a dog by the eares.
text.NABRE: 26:17 Whoever meddles in the quarrel of another is one who grabs a passing dog by the ears.

text.NRSV: Like a maniac who shoots deadly firebrands and arrows,
text.Rotherham: As a madman throwing firebrands, arrows and death,
text.KJV: 26:18 As a mad man, who casteth firebrands, arrowes, and death:
text.NABRE: 26:18 Like a crazed archer scattering firebrands and deadly arrows,

text.NRSV: is one who deceives a neighbor and says, “I am only joking!”
text.Rotherham: So, is a man who deceiveth his neighbour, and saith, Was not, I, in sport?
text.KJV: 26:19 So is the man that deceiueth his neighbour, & sayth, Am not I in sport?
text.NABRE: 26:19 Such are those who deceive their neighbor, and then say, “I was only joking.”

text.NRSV: For lack of wood the fire goes out, and where there is no whisperer, quarreling ceases.
text.Rotherham: Without wood a fire is quenched, and, where there is no tattler, strife is hushed.
text.KJV: 26:20 Where no wood is, there the fire goeth out: so where there is no tale-bearer, the strife ceaseth.
text.NABRE: 26:20 Without wood the fire dies out; without a talebearer strife subsides.

text.NRSV: As charcoal is to hot embers and wood to fire, so is a quarrelsome person for kindling strife.
text.Rotherham: Black coal to burning blocks, and wood to fire, so is a contentious man, for kindling strife.
text.KJV: 26:21 As coales are to burning coales, and wood to fire; so is a contentious man to kindle strife.
text.NABRE: 26:21 Charcoal for coals, wood for fire— such are the quarrelsome, enkindling strife.

text.NRSV: The words of a whisperer are like delicious morsels; they go down into the inner parts of the body.
text.Rotherham: the words of a tattler, are dainties, they, therefore go down into the chambers of the inner man.
text.KJV: 26:22 The words of a tale-bearer are as woundes, and they goe downe into the innermost parts of the belly.
text.NABRE: 26:22 The words of a talebearer are like dainty morsels: they sink into one’s inmost being.

text.NRSV: Like the glaze covering an earthen vessel are smooth lips with an evil heart.
text.Rotherham: Dross silver overlaid upon earthenware, so are burning lips, with a mischievous heart:
text.KJV: 26:23 Burning lips, and a wicked heart, are like a potsheard couered with siluer drosse.
text.NABRE: 26:23 Like a glazed finish on earthenware are smooth lips and a wicked heart.

text.NRSV: An enemy dissembles in speaking while harboring deceit within;
text.Rotherham: With his lips, the hater dissembleth, but, within himself, he layeth up deceit:
text.KJV: 26:24 Hee that hateth, dissembleth with his lips, and layeth vp deceit within him.
text.NABRE: 26:24 With their lips enemies pretend, but inwardly they maintain deceit;

text.NRSV: when an enemy speaks graciously, do not believe it, for there are seven abominations in the mind;
text.Rotherham: Though he make gracious his voice, do not trust him, for, seven abominations, are in his heart:
text.KJV: 26:25 When he speaketh faire, beleeue him not: for there are seuen abominations in his heart.
text.NABRE: 26:25 When they speak graciously, do not trust them, for seven abominations are in their hearts.

text.NRSV: though hatred be covered with guile, its wickedness will be exposed in the assembly.
text.Rotherham: Hatred may clothe itself with guile, his wickedness shall be disclosed in the convocation.
text.KJV: 26:26 Whose hatred is couered by deceit, his wickednesse shall be shewed before the whole congregation.
text.NABRE: 26:26 Hatred can be concealed by pretense, but malice will be revealed in the assembly.

text.NRSV: Whoever digs a pit will fall into it, and a stone will come back on the one who starts it rolling.
text.Rotherham: He that diggeth a pit, thereinto, shall fall, and, he that rolleth a stone, upon himself, shall it return.
text.KJV: 26:27 Whoso diggeth a pit, shall fall therein: and he that rolleth a stone, it will returne vpon him.
text.NABRE: 26:27 Whoever digs a pit falls into it; and a stone comes back upon the one who rolls it.

text.NRSV: A lying tongue hates its victims, and a flattering mouth works ruin.
text.Rotherham: A false tongue, hateth them who are crushed by it, and, a flattering mouth, worketh occasion of stumbling.
text.KJV: 26:28 A lying tongue hateth those that are afflicted by it, and a flattering mouth worketh ruine.
text.NABRE: 26:28 The lying tongue is its owner’s enemy, and the flattering mouth works ruin.

text.NRSV: Do not boast about tomorrow, for you do not know what a day may bring.
text.Rotherham: Do not boast thyself of to-morrow, for thou knowest not what a day may bring forth.
text.KJV: 27:1 Boast not thy selfe of to morrow: for thou knowest not what a day may bring foorth.
text.NABRE: 27:1 Chapter 27 - Do not boast about tomorrow, for you do not know what any day may bring forth.

text.NRSV: Let another praise you, and not your own mouth—a stranger, and not your own lips.
text.Rotherham: Let another praise thee, and not thine own mouth, a stranger, and not thine own lips.
text.KJV: 27:2 Let another man praise thee, and not thine owne mouth; a stranger, and not thine owne lips.
text.NABRE: 27:2 Let another praise you, not your own mouth; a stranger, not your own lips.

text.NRSV: A stone is heavy, and sand is weighty, but a fool’s provocation is heavier than both.
text.Rotherham: Heavy is a stone, and weighty is sand,—but, the vexation of a fool, is heavier than both.
text.KJV: 27:3 A stone is heauie, and the sand weightie: but a fooles wrath is heauier then them both.
text.NABRE: 27:3 Stone is heavy, and sand a burden, but a fool’s provocation is heavier than both.

text.NRSV: Wrath is cruel, anger is overwhelming, but who can stand before jealousy?
text.Rotherham: The cruelty of rage, and the overflow of anger! But who can stand before, jealousy?
text.KJV: 27:4 Wrath is cruell, and anger is outragious: but who is able to stand before enuie?
text.NABRE: 27:4 Anger is cruel, and wrath overwhelming, but before jealousy who can stand?

text.NRSV: Better is open rebuke than hidden love.
text.Rotherham: Better is a rebuke that is open, than love carefully concealed.
text.KJV: 27:5 Open rebuke is better then secret loue.
text.NABRE: 27:5 Better is an open rebuke than a love that remains hidden.

text.NRSV: Well meant are the wounds a friend inflicts, but profuse are the kisses of an enemy.
text.Rotherham: Faithful are the wounds of a friend, but lavished are the kisses of an enemy.
text.KJV: 27:6 Faithfull are the woundes of a friend: but the kisses of an enemy are deceitfull.
text.NABRE: 27:6 Trustworthy are the blows of a friend, dangerous, the kisses of an enemy.

text.NRSV: The sated appetite spurns honey, but to a ravenous appetite even the bitter is sweet.
text.Rotherham: The surfeited soul, trampleth upon droppings from the comb, but, to the hungry soul, every bitter thing, is sweet.
text.KJV: 27:7 The full soule loatheth an honie combe: but to the hungry soule euery bitter thing is sweete.
text.NABRE: 27:7 One who is full spurns honey; but to the hungry, any bitter thing is sweet.

text.NRSV: Like a bird that strays from its nest is a person who strays from home.
text.Rotherham: As a bird wandering from her nest, so, is a man wandering from his place.
text.KJV: 27:8 As a bird that wandreth from her nest: so is a man that wandreth from his place.
text.NABRE: 27:8 Like a bird far from the nest so is anyone far from home.

text.NRSV: Perfume and incense make the heart glad, but the soul is torn by trouble.
text.Rotherham: Oil and perfume, rejoice the heart, the sweetness of one’s friend, more than fragrant wood.
text.KJV: 27:9 Oyntment and perfume reioyce the heart: so doeth the sweetnesse of a mans friend by heartie counsell.
text.NABRE: 27:9 Perfume and incense bring joy to the heart, but by grief the soul is torn asunder.

text.NRSV: Do not forsake your friend or the friend of your parent; do not go to the house of your kindred in the day of your calamity. Better is a neighbor who is near than kindred who are far away.
text.Rotherham: Thine own friend and thy father’s friend, do not thou forsake; but, the house of thy brother, do not enter, in thy day of calamity, Better a neighbour near, than a brother far off.
text.KJV: 27:10 Thine owne friend and thy fathers friend forsake not; neither goe in to thy brothers house in the day of thy calamitie: for better is a neighbour that is neere, then a brother farre off.
text.NABRE: 27:10 Do not give up your own friend and your father’s friend; do not resort to the house of your kindred when trouble strikes. Better a neighbor near than kin far away.

text.NRSV: Be wise, my child, and make my heart glad, so that I may answer my critics.
text.Rotherham: Be wise, my son, and rejoice my heart, that I may answer him that reproacheth me in a matter.
text.KJV: 27:11 My sonne, be wise, and make my heart glad, that I may answere him that reprocheth me.
text.NABRE: 27:11 Be wise, my son, and bring joy to my heart, so that I can answer whoever taunts me.

text.NRSV: The clever see danger and hide; but the simple go on, and suffer for it.
text.Rotherham: A prudent man, seeth calamity—he hideth himself, the simple, pass on—they suffer.
text.KJV: 27:12 A prudent man foreseeth the euil, and hideth himselfe: but the simple passe on, and are punished.
text.NABRE: 27:12 The astute see an evil and hide; the naive continue on and pay the penalty.

text.NRSV: Take the garment of one who has given surety for a stranger; seize the pledge from one who has given surety for foreigners.
text.Rotherham: Take a man’s garment when he hath become pledge for a stranger, then, for a female unknown, accept him as surety.
text.KJV: 27:13 Take his garment that is surety for a stranger, and take a pledge of him for a strange woman.
text.NABRE: 27:13 Take the garment of the one who became surety for a stranger; if for a foreign woman, exact the pledge!

text.NRSV: Whoever blesses a neighbor with a loud voice, rising early in the morning, will be counted as cursing.
text.Rotherham: He that blesseth his friend, with a loud voice, in the morning early, a reproach, shall it be reckoned to him.
text.KJV: 27:14 He that blesseth his friend with a loud voice, rising earely in the morning, it shall be counted a curse to him.
text.NABRE: 27:14 Those who greet their neighbor with a loud voice in the early morning, a curse can be laid to their charge.

text.NRSV: A continual dripping on a rainy day and a contentious woman are alike;
text.Rotherham: A continuous dripping on a day of downpour, and a contentious wife, are alike:
text.KJV: 27:15 A continuall dropping in a very rainie day, and a contentious woman, are alike.
text.NABRE: 27:15 For a persistent leak on a rainy day the match is a quarrelsome wife;

text.NRSV: to restrain her is to restrain the wind or to grasp oil in the right hand.
text.Rotherham: He that hideth her, hideth the wind, and, perfume, his right hand may proclaim.
text.KJV: 27:16 Whosoeuer hideth her, hideth the wind, and the ointment of his right hand which be wrayeth it selfe.
text.NABRE: 27:16 Whoever would hide her hides a stormwind and cannot tell north from south.

text.NRSV: Iron sharpens iron, and one person sharpens the wits of another.
text.Rotherham: Let, iron, by iron, become sharp, and let, a man, sharpen the face of his friend.
text.KJV: 27:17 Iron sharpeneth iron: so a man sharpeneth the countenance of his friend.
text.NABRE: 27:17 Iron is sharpened by iron; one person sharpens another.

text.NRSV: Anyone who tends a fig tree will eat its fruit, and anyone who renders service to a master will be honored.
text.Rotherham: He that guardeth the fig-tree, shall eat the fruit thereof, and, he that watcheth over his master, shall be honoured.
text.KJV: 27:18 Whoso keepeth the figtree, shall eate the fruit therof: so he that waiteth on his master, shall be honoured.
text.NABRE: 27:18 Those who tend a fig tree eat its fruit; so those attentive to their master will be honored.

text.NRSV: As sun reflects the face, so the mind of one person reflects that of another.
text.Rotherham: As in water, face [answereth] to face, so, the heart of man to man.
text.KJV: 27:19 As in water face answereth to face: so the heart of man to man.
text.NABRE: 27:19 As face mirrors face in water, so the heart reflects the person.

text.NRSV: Sheol and Abaddon are never satisfied, and human eyes are never satisfied.
text.Rotherham: Hades and destruction, are not satisfied, and, the eyes of a man, are not satisfied.
text.KJV: 27:20 Hell and destruction are neuer full: so the eyes of man are neuer satisfied.
text.NABRE: 27:20 Sheol and Abaddon can never be satisfied; so the eyes of mortals can never be satisfied.

text.NRSV: The crucible is for silver, and the furnace is for gold, so a person is tested by being praised.
text.Rotherham: Fining pot for silver, and crucible for gold, and, a man, [is to be tried] by what he praiseth.
text.KJV: 27:21 As the fining pot for siluer, and the furnace for gold: so is a man to his praise.
text.NABRE: 27:21 The crucible for silver, the furnace for gold, so you must assay the praise you receive.

text.NRSV: Crush a fool in a mortar with a pestle along with crushed grain, but the folly will not be driven out.
text.Rotherham: Though thou pound a fool in a mortar, amidst grain, with a pestle, his folly, will not depart from him.
text.KJV: 27:22 Though thou shouldest bray a foole in a morter among wheate with a pestell, yet will not his foolishnesse depart from him.
text.NABRE: 27:22 Though you pound fools with a pestle, their folly never leaves them.

text.NRSV: Know well the condition of your flocks, and give attention to your herds;
