text.Rotherham: Note well, the appearance of thy flock, apply thy mind to thy herds;
text.KJV: 27:23 Be thou diligent to knowe the state of thy flocks, and looke well to thy herds.
text.NABRE: 27:23 Take good care of your flocks, give careful attention to your herds;

text.NRSV: for riches do not last forever, nor a crown for all generations.
text.Rotherham: For, not age-abiding, are riches, nor is the diadem, from generation to generation:—
text.KJV: 27:24 For riches are not for euer: and doth the crowne endure to euery generation?
text.NABRE: 27:24 For wealth does not last forever, nor even a crown from age to age.

text.NRSV: When the grass is gone, and new growth appears, and the herbage of the mountains is gathered,
text.Rotherham: The grass, is taken away, and the young shoot, showeth itself, and the herbage of the mountains, is gathered;
text.KJV: 27:25 The hay appeareth, and the tender grasse sheweth it selfe, and herbes of the mountaines are gathered.
text.NABRE: 27:25 When the grass comes up and the new growth appears, and the mountain greens are gathered in,

text.NRSV: the lambs will provide your clothing, and the goats the price of a field;
text.Rotherham: There are, lambs, for thy clothing, and, for the price of thy field, there are he-goats;
text.KJV: 27:26 The lambes are for thy clothing, and the goates are the price of thy field.
text.NABRE: 27:26 The lambs will provide you with clothing, and the goats, the price of a field,

text.NRSV: there will be enough goats’ milk for your food, for the food of your household and maintenance for your servant-girls.
text.Rotherham: With, enough goats-milk, for thy food—for the food of thy household, and, a maintenance, for thy maidens.
text.KJV: 27:27 And thou shalt haue goats milke enough for thy food, for the food of thy houshold, and for the maintenance for thy maidens.
text.NABRE: 27:27 And there will be ample goat’s milk for your food, food for your house, sustenance for your maidens.

text.NRSV: The wicked flee when no one pursues, but the righteous are as bold as a lion.
text.Rotherham: The lawless, fleeth when no man pursueth, but, the righteous, like a lion, are confident.
text.KJV: 28:1 The wicked flee when no man pursueth: but the righteous are bolde as a lyon.
text.NABRE: 28:1 Chapter 28 - The wicked flee though none pursue; but the just, like a lion, are confident.

text.NRSV: When a land rebels, it has many rulers; but with an intelligent ruler there is lasting order.
text.Rotherham: For the transgressions of a land, many are the rulers thereof, but, under an intelligent and discerning man, stability is prolonged.
text.KJV: 28:2 For the transgression of a land, many are the princes thereof: but by a man of vnderstanding and knowledge the state thereof shall bee prolonged.
text.NABRE: 28:2 If a land is rebellious, its princes will be many; but with an intelligent and wise ruler there is stability.

text.NRSV: A ruler who oppresses the poor is a beating rain that leaves no food.
text.Rotherham: A poor man, who oppresseth the helpless, [is like] a rain beating down, leaving no food.
text.KJV: 28:3 A poore man that oppresseth the poore, is like a sweeping raine which leaueth no food.
text.NABRE: 28:3 One who is poor and extorts from the lowly is a devastating rain that leaves no food.

text.NRSV: Those who forsake the law praise the wicked, but those who keep the law struggle against them.
text.Rotherham: They who forsake instruction, praise one who is lawless, while, they who keep instruction, are at strife with them.
text.KJV: 28:4 They that forsake the law, praise the wicked: but such as keepe the Law, contend with them.
text.NABRE: 28:4 Those who abandon instruction praise the wicked, but those who keep instruction oppose them.

text.NRSV: The evil do not understand justice, but those who seek the Lord understand it completely.
text.Rotherham: Wicked men, consider not justice, but, they who seek Yahweh, consider everything.
text.KJV: 28:5 Euill men vnderstand not iudgement: but they that seeke the Lord, vnderstand all things.
text.NABRE: 28:5 The evil understand nothing of justice, but those who seek the LORD understand everything.

text.NRSV: Better to be poor and walk in integrity than to be crooked in one’s ways even though rich.
text.Rotherham: Better a poor man walking in his integrity, than one who is crooked—turning two ways, though, he, be rich.
text.KJV: 28:6 Better is the poore that walketh in his vprightnesse, then he that is peruerse in his wayes, though he be rich.
text.NABRE: 28:6 Better to be poor and walk in integrity than rich and crooked in one’s ways.

text.NRSV: Those who keep the law are wise children, but companions of gluttons shame their parents.
text.Rotherham: He that keepeth instruction, is a son with discernment, but, a companion of squanderers, bringeth shame to his father.
text.KJV: 28:7 Whoso keepeth the law, is a wise sonne: but he that is a companion of riotous men, shameth his father.
text.NABRE: 28:7 Whoever heeds instruction is a wise son, but whoever joins with wastrels disgraces his father.

text.NRSV: One who augments wealth by exorbitant interest gathers it for another who is kind to the poor.
text.Rotherham: He that increaseth his substance by interest and profit, for one ready to favour the poor, doth gather it.
text.KJV: 28:8 He that by vsurie and vniust gaine increaseth his substance, he shall gather it for him that wil pity the poore.
text.NABRE: 28:8 Whoever amasses wealth by interest and overcharge gathers it for the one who is kind to the poor.

text.NRSV: When one will not listen to the law, even one’s prayers are an abomination.
text.Rotherham: He that turneth away his ear from hearing instruction, even his prayer, is an abomination.
text.KJV: 28:9 He that turneth away his eare from hearing the law, euen his prayer shalbe abomination.
text.NABRE: 28:9 Those who turn their ears from hearing instruction, even their prayer is an abomination.

text.NRSV: Those who mislead the upright into evil ways will fall into pits of their own making, but the blameless will have a goodly inheritance.
text.Rotherham: He that misguideth the upright into a hurtful way! into his own pit, he himself, shall fall, but, men of integrity, shall inherit good.
text.KJV: 28:10 Who so causeth the righteous to goe astray in an euill way, he shall fall himselfe into his owne pit: but the vpright shall haue good things in possession.
text.NABRE: 28:10 Those who mislead the upright into an evil way will themselves fall into their own pit, but the blameless will attain prosperity.

text.NRSV: The rich is wise in self-esteem, but an intelligent poor person sees through the poses.
text.Rotherham: Wise in his own eyes, is the man that is rich, but, a poor man of discernment, searcheth him out.
text.KJV: 28:11 The rich man is wise in his owne conceit: but the poore that hath vnderstanding searcheth him out.
text.NABRE: 28:11 The rich are wise in their own eyes, but the poor who are intelligent see through them.

text.NRSV: When the righteous triumph, there is great glory, but when the wicked rise, people hide themselves.
text.Rotherham: In the exulting of the righteous, there is great glorying, but, when the lawless arise, a man must be sought for.
text.KJV: 28:12 When righteous men do reioyce, there is great glory: but when the wicked rise, a man is hidden.
text.NABRE: 28:12 When the just triumph, there is great glory; but when the wicked prevail, people hide.

text.NRSV: No one who conceals transgressions will prosper, but one who confesses and forsakes them will obtain mercy.
text.Rotherham: He that covereth his transgressions, shall not prosper, but, he that confesseth and forsaketh, shall find compassion.
text.KJV: 28:13 He that couereth his sinnes, shall not prosper: but who so confesseth and forsaketh them, shall haue mercie.
text.NABRE: 28:13 Those who conceal their sins do not prosper, but those who confess and forsake them obtain mercy.

text.NRSV: Happy is the one who fears the Lord always, but the stubborn of heart will fall into calamity.
text.Rotherham: How happy the man who is ever circumspect, whereas, he that hardeneth his heart, shall fall into calamity.
text.KJV: 28:14 Happy is the man that feareth always: but he that hardeneth his heart, shall fall into mischiefe.
text.NABRE: 28:14 Happy those who always fear; but those who harden their hearts fall into evil.

text.NRSV: Like a roaring lion or a charging bear is a wicked ruler over a poor people.
text.Rotherham: A growling lion, and a ranging bear, is a lawless ruler, over a poor people.
text.KJV: 28:15 As a roaring lyon and a ranging beare: so is a wicked ruler ouer the poore people.
text.NABRE: 28:15 A roaring lion or a ravenous bear is a wicked ruler over a poor people.

text.NRSV: A ruler who lacks understanding is a cruel oppressor; but one who hates unjust gain will enjoy a long life.
text.Rotherham: A leader, may lack intelligence, yet abound in oppressions, The hater of greed, shall lengthen out days.
text.KJV: 28:16 The prince that wanteth vnderstanding, is also a great oppressour: but he that hateth couetousnesse, shall prolong his dayes.
text.NABRE: 28:16 The less prudent the rulers, the more oppressive their deeds. Those who hate ill-gotten gain prolong their days.

text.NRSV: If someone is burdened with the blood of another, let that person be a fugitive until death; let no one help.
text.Rotherham: A man oppressed with a person’s blood, unto a pit, shall flee, let them not hold him back.
text.KJV: 28:17 A man that doth violence to the blood of any person, shall flie to the pit, let no man stay him.
text.NABRE: 28:17 Though a person burdened with blood guilt is in flight even to the grave, let no one offer support.

text.NRSV: One who walks in integrity will be safe, but whoever follows crooked ways will fall into the Pit.
text.Rotherham: He that walketh with integrity, shall be saved, but, he that is crooked, turning two ways, shall fall in one.
text.KJV: 28:18 Whoso walketh vprightly, shall be saued: but he that is peruerse in his wayes, shall fall at once.
text.NABRE: 28:18 Whoever walks blamelessly is safe, but one whose ways are crooked falls into a pit.

text.NRSV: Anyone who tills the land will have plenty of bread, but one who follows worthless pursuits will have plenty of poverty.
text.Rotherham: He that tilleth his ground, shall have plenty of bread, but, he that pursueth empty-heads, shall have plenty of poverty.
text.KJV: 28:19 He that tilleth his land, shal haue plentie of bread: but he that followeth after vaine persons, shall haue pouerty enough.
text.NABRE: 28:19 Those who cultivate their land will have plenty of food, but those who engage in idle pursuits will have plenty of want.

text.NRSV: The faithful will abound with blessings, but one who is in a hurry to be rich will not go unpunished.
text.Rotherham: A man of fidelity, aboundeth in blessings, but, one hasting to be rich, shall not be held innocent.
text.KJV: 28:20 A faithfull man shall abound with blessings: but hee that maketh haste to be rich, shall not be innocent.
text.NABRE: 28:20 The trustworthy will be richly blessed; but whoever hastens to be rich will not go unpunished.

text.NRSV: To show partiality is not good— yet for a piece of bread a person may do wrong.
text.Rotherham: To take note of faces [in judgment], is not good, and, for a bit of bread, a man will transgress.
text.KJV: 28:21 To haue respect of persons, is not good: for, for a piece of bread that man will transgresse.
text.NABRE: 28:21 To show partiality is never good: for even a morsel of bread one may do wrong.

text.NRSV: The miser is in a hurry to get rich and does not know that loss is sure to come.
text.Rotherham: A man, hasting to be rich, hath an evil eye, and knoweth not when want may overtake him.
text.KJV: 28:22 He that hasteth to bee rich, hath an euill eye, and considereth not that pouerty shall come vpon him.
text.NABRE: 28:22 Misers hurry toward wealth, not knowing that want is coming toward them.

text.NRSV: Whoever rebukes another will afterward find more favor than one who flatters with the tongue.
text.Rotherham: He that reproveth a man, shall, afterwards, find more, favour, than he that useth a flattering tongue.
text.KJV: 28:23 He that rebuketh a man, afterwards shall find more fauour, then he that flattereth with the tongue.
text.NABRE: 28:23 Whoever rebukes another wins more favor than one who flatters with the tongue.

text.NRSV: Anyone who robs father or mother and says, “That is no transgression,” is a partner to one who destroys.
text.Rotherham: He that robbeth his father or his mother, and saith, It is no transgression, companion, is he to one who wasteth.
text.KJV: 28:24 Who so robbeth his father or his mother, and saith, it is no transgression, the same is the companion of a destroyer.
text.NABRE: 28:24 Whoever defrauds father or mother and says, “It is no sin,” is a partner to a brigand.

text.NRSV: The greedy person stirs up strife, but whoever trusts in the Lord will be enriched.
text.Rotherham: The ambitious in soul, stirreth up strife, but, he that trusteth in Yahweh, shall be enriched.
text.KJV: 28:25 He that is of a proud heart, stirreth vp strife: but he that putteth his trust in the Lord, shalbe made fat.
text.NABRE: 28:25 The greedy person stirs up strife, but the one who trusts in the LORD will prosper.

text.NRSV: Those who trust in their own wits are fools, but those who walk in wisdom come through safely.
text.Rotherham: He that trusteth his own heart, the same, is a dullard, but, he that walketh wisely, the same, shall be delivered.
text.KJV: 28:26 Hee that trusteth in his owne heart, is a foole: but who so walketh wisely, he shall be deliuered.
text.NABRE: 28:26 Those who trust in themselves are fools, but those who walk in wisdom are safe.

text.NRSV: Whoever gives to the poor will lack nothing, but one who turns a blind eye will get many a curse.
text.Rotherham: One who giveth to the poor, shall have no want, but, he that hideth his eyes, shall receive many a curse.
text.KJV: 28:27 He that giueth vnto the poore, shall not lacke: but he that hideth his eyes, shall haue many a curse.
text.NABRE: 28:27 Those who give to the poor have no lack, but those who avert their eyes, many curses.

text.NRSV: When the wicked rise, people hide themselves, but when they perish, the righteous increase.
text.Rotherham: When lawless men rise, a common man will hide himself, but, when they perish, righteous men multiply.
text.KJV: 28:28 When the wicked rise, men hide themselues: but when they perish, the righteous increase.
text.NABRE: 28:28 When the wicked prevail, people hide; but at their fall the just abound.

text.NRSV: One who is often reproved, yet remains stiff-necked, will suddenly be broken beyond repair.
text.Rotherham: He that being often reproved stiffeneth his neck, suddenly shall be hurt, and there be no healing.
text.KJV: 29:1 He that being often reproued, hardeneth his necke, shal suddenly be destroied, and that without remedy.
text.NABRE: 29:1 Chapter 29 - Those stiff-necked in the face of reproof in an instant will be shattered beyond cure.

text.NRSV: When the righteous are in authority, the people rejoice; but when the wicked rule, the people groan.
text.Rotherham: When the righteous become great, the people rejoice, but, when a lawless man beareth rule, a people sigh.
text.KJV: 29:2 When the righteous are in authoritie, the people reioyce: but when the wicked beareth rule, the people mourne.
text.NABRE: 29:2 When the just flourish, the people rejoice; but when the wicked rule, the people groan.

text.NRSV: A child who loves wisdom makes a father glad, but to keep company with prostitutes is to squander one’s substance.
text.Rotherham: A man who loveth wisdom, gladdeneth his father, but, a companion of harlots, destroyeth wealth.
text.KJV: 29:3 Whoso loueth wisedome, reioyceth his father: but hee that keepeth company with harlots, spendeth his substance.
text.NABRE: 29:3 Whoever loves wisdom gives joy to his father, but whoever consorts with harlots squanders his wealth.

text.NRSV: By justice a king gives stability to the land, but one who makes heavy exactions ruins it.
text.Rotherham: A king, by justice, shall establish a land,—but, a man open to bribes, bringeth it to ruin.
text.KJV: 29:4 The king by iudgement stablisheth the land: but he that receiueth gifts, ouerthroweth it.
text.NABRE: 29:4 By justice a king builds up the land; but one who raises taxes tears it down.

text.NRSV: Whoever flatters a neighbor is spreading a net for the neighbor’s feet.
text.Rotherham: A man who flattereth his neighbour, spreadeth, a net, over his steps.
text.KJV: 29:5 A man that flattereth his neighbour, spreadeth a net for his feet.
text.NABRE: 29:5 Those who speak flattery to their neighbor cast a net at their feet.

text.NRSV: In the transgression of the evil there is a snare, but the righteous sing and rejoice.
text.Rotherham: In the transgression of a wicked man, is a snare, but, the righteous, doth shout in triumph and rejoice.
text.KJV: 29:6 In the transgression of an euill man there is a snare: but the righteous doth sing and reioyce.
text.NABRE: 29:6 The sin of the wicked is a trap, but the just run along joyfully.

text.NRSV: The righteous know the rights of the poor; the wicked have no such understanding.
text.Rotherham: The righteous doth acknowledge the plea of the poor, but, the lawless, regardeth not knowledge.
text.KJV: 29:7 The righteous considereth the cause of the poore: but the wicked regardeth not to know it.
text.NABRE: 29:7 The just care for the cause of the poor; the wicked do not understand such care.

text.NRSV: Scoffers set a city aflame, but the wise turn away wrath.
text.Rotherham: Men given to mockery, inflame a city,—but, wise men, turn away anger.
text.KJV: 29:8 Scornefull men bring a citie into a snare: but wise men turne away wrath.
text.NABRE: 29:8 Scoffers enflame the city, but the wise calm the fury.

text.NRSV: If the wise go to court with a fool, there is only fretting and laughing, and no rest.
text.Rotherham: A wise man pleading with a foolish man, whether he rage or laugh, there is no settlement.
text.KJV: 29:9 If a wise man contendeth with a foolish man, whether hee rage or laugh, there is no rest.
text.NABRE: 29:9 If a wise person disputes with a fool, there is railing and ridicule but no resolution.

text.NRSV: The bloodthirsty hate the blameless, and they seek the life of the upright.
text.Rotherham: Blood-thirsty men, hate the blameless man, and, as for the upright, they seek his life.
text.KJV: 29:10 The bloodthirstie hate the vpright: but the iust seeke his soule.
text.NABRE: 29:10 The bloodthirsty hate the blameless, but the upright seek his life.

text.NRSV: A fool gives full vent to anger, but the wise quietly holds it back.
text.Rotherham: All his anger, doth a dullard let go, but, a wise man, by keeping it back, stilleth it.
text.KJV: 29:11 A foole vttereth all his mind: but a wise man keepeth it in till afterwards.
text.NABRE: 29:11 Fools give vent to all their anger; but the wise, biding their time, control it.

text.NRSV: If a ruler listens to falsehood, all his officials will be wicked.
text.Rotherham: When a ruler giveth heed to the word of falsehood, all his attendants, become lawless.
text.KJV: 29:12 If a ruler hearken to lies, all his seruants are wicked.
text.NABRE: 29:12 If rulers listen to lying words, their servants all become wicked.

text.NRSV: The poor and the oppressor have this in common: the Lord gives light to the eyes of both.
text.Rotherham: The poor man and the man of usury, meet together, he that enlighteneth the eyes of them both, is Yahweh.
text.KJV: 29:13 The poore and the deceitful man meet together: the Lord lightneth both their eyes.
text.NABRE: 29:13 The poor and the oppressor meet: the LORD gives light to the eyes of both.

text.NRSV: If a king judges the poor with equity, his throne will be established forever.
text.Rotherham: When a king judgeth faithfully the poor, his throne, to futurity, shall be established.
text.KJV: 29:14 The King that faithfully iudgeth the poore, his throne shall be established for euer.
text.NABRE: 29:14 If a king is honestly for the rights of the poor, his throne stands firm forever.

text.NRSV: The rod and reproof give wisdom, but a mother is disgraced by a neglected child.
text.Rotherham: A rod with rebuke, giveth wisdom, but, a youth unrestrained, bringeth shame to his mother.
text.KJV: 29:15 The rod and reproofe giue wisedome: but a child left to himselfe bringeth his mother to shame.
text.NABRE: 29:15 The rod of correction gives wisdom, but uncontrolled youths disgrace their mothers.

text.NRSV: When the wicked are in authority, transgression increases, but the righteous will look upon their downfall.
text.Rotherham: When the lawless become great, transgression increaseth, but, the righteous, shall behold, their ruin.
text.KJV: 29:16 When the wicked are multiplied, transgression increaseth: but the righteous shall see their fall.
text.NABRE: 29:16 When the wicked increase, crime increases; but the just will behold their downfall.

text.NRSV: Discipline your children, and they will give you rest; they will give delight to your heart.
text.Rotherham: Correct thy son, that he may give thee rest, that he may give delight to thy soul.
text.KJV: 29:17 Correct thy sonne, and hee shall giue thee rest: yea he shall giue delight vnto thy soule.
text.NABRE: 29:17 Discipline your children, and they will bring you comfort, and give delight to your soul.

text.NRSV: Where there is no prophecy, the people cast off restraint, but happy are those who keep the law.
text.Rotherham: Where there is no vision, a people is let loose, but, he that keepeth instruction, how happy is he!
text.KJV: 29:18 Where there is no vision, the people perish: but he that keepeth the Law, happy is he.
text.NABRE: 29:18 Without a vision the people lose restraint; but happy is the one who follows instruction.

text.NRSV: By mere words servants are not disciplined, for though they understand, they will not give heed.
text.Rotherham: By words, a servant will not be corrected, though he perceiveth, yet is there no answer.
text.KJV: 29:19 A seruant will not be corrected by words: for though hee vnderstand, hee will not answere.
text.NABRE: 29:19 Not by words alone can servants be trained; for they understand but do not respond.

text.NRSV: Do you see someone who is hasty in speaking? There is more hope for a fool than for such a person.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast seen a man hasty in his words,—there is, more hope of a dullard, than of him.
text.KJV: 29:20 Seest thou a man that is hasty in his words? there is more hope of a foole then of him.
text.NABRE: 29:20 Do you see someone hasty in speech? There is more hope for a fool!

text.NRSV: A slave pampered from childhood will become a rebel in the end.
text.Rotherham: He that dealeth tenderly with his servant from childhood, in his after life, shall have him for a son.
text.KJV: 29:21 He that delicately bringeth vp his seruant from a child, shall haue him become his sonne at the length.
text.NABRE: 29:21 If servants are pampered from childhood they will turn out to be stubborn.

text.NRSV: One given to anger stirs up strife, and the hot-tempered person causes many a transgression.
text.Rotherham: A man given to anger, stirreth up strife, and, he that exceedeth in wrath, aboundeth in transgression.
text.KJV: 29:22 An angry man stirreth vp strife, and a furious man aboundeth in transgression.
text.NABRE: 29:22 The ill-tempered stir up strife, and the hotheaded cause many sins.

text.NRSV: A person’s pride will bring humiliation, but one who is lowly in spirit will obtain honor.
text.Rotherham: The loftiness of a man, layeth him low,—but, one of a lowly spirit, shall attain unto honour.
text.KJV: 29:23 A mans pride shall bring him lowe: but honour shall vpholde the humble in spirit.
text.NABRE: 29:23 Haughtiness brings humiliation, but the humble of spirit acquire honor.

text.NRSV: To be a partner of a thief is to hate one’s own life; one hears the victim’s curse, but discloses nothing.
text.Rotherham: He that shareth with a thief, hateth himself, an oath, he heareth, yet may not tell.
text.KJV: 29:24 Who so is partner with a thiefe, hateth his owne soule: hee heareth cursing, and bewrayeth it not.
text.NABRE: 29:24 Partners of a thief hate themselves; they hear the imprecation but do not testify.

text.NRSV: The fear of others lays a snare, but one who trusts in the Lord is secure.
text.Rotherham: The fear of man, setteth a snare, but, he that trusteth in Yahweh, shall be placed on high.
text.KJV: 29:25 The feare of man bringeth a snare: but who so putteth his trust in the Lord, shall be safe.
text.NABRE: 29:25 Fear of others becomes a snare, but the one who trusts in the LORD is safe.

text.NRSV: Many seek the favor of a ruler, but it is from the Lord that one gets justice.
text.Rotherham: Many, seek the face of a ruler, but, from Yahweh, is the sentence of each one.
text.KJV: 29:26 Many seeke the rulers fauour, but euery mans iudgement commeth from the Lord.
text.NABRE: 29:26 Many curry favor with a ruler, but it is from the LORD that one receives justice.

text.NRSV: The unjust are an abomination to the righteous, and those whose way is straight are an abomination to the wicked.
text.Rotherham: An abomination to the righteous, is the man of perversity, and, an abomination to the lawless, is a man of straightforward way.
text.KJV: 29:27 An vniust man is an abomination to the iust: and he that is vpright in the way, is abomination to the wicked.
text.NABRE: 29:27 An abomination to the just, the evildoer; an abomination to the wicked, one whose way is straight.

text.NRSV: The words of Agur son of Jakeh. An oracle. Thus says the man: I am weary, O God, I am weary, O God. How can I prevail?
text.Rotherham: The words of Agur, son of Jakeh, [even] the oracle,—The utterance of the man, for Ithiel, for Ithiel and Ucal.
text.KJV: 30:1 The words of Agur the sonne of Iakeh, euen the prophecy: The man spake vnto Ithiel, euen vnto Ithiel and Ucal.
text.NABRE: 30:1 VII. Sayings of Agur and Others Chapter 30 - The words of Agur, son of Jakeh the Massaite: The pronouncement of mortal man: “I am weary, O God; I am weary, O God, and I am exhausted.

text.NRSV: Surely I am too stupid to be human; I do not have human understanding.
text.Rotherham: Surely, more brutish, am, I, than any man, nor doth, the understanding of a son of earth, pertain to me;
text.KJV: 30:2 Surely I am more brutish then any man, and haue not the vnderstanding of a man.
text.NABRE: 30:2 I am more brute than human being, without even human intelligence;

text.NRSV: I have not learned wisdom, nor have I knowledge of the holy ones.
text.Rotherham: Neither have I learned wisdom, nor, the knowledge of the Holy Ones, can I acquire.
text.KJV: 30:3 I neither learned wisedome, nor haue the knowledge of the holy.
text.NABRE: 30:3 Neither have I learned wisdom, nor have I the knowledge of the Holy One.

text.NRSV: Who has ascended to heaven and come down? Who has gathered the wind in the hollow of the hand? Who has wrapped up the waters in a garment? Who has established all the ends of the earth? What is the name, and what is the name of the child? Surely you know!
text.Rotherham: Who hath ascended the heavens and then descended? Who hath gathered the wind into his two hands? Who hath wrapped up the waters in a mantle? Who hath set up all the ends of the earth? What is his name and what the name of his son, when thou knowest?
text.KJV: 30:4 Who hath ascended vp into heauen, or descended? who hath gathered the wind in his fists? who hath bound the waters in a garment? who hath established all the ends of the earth? what is his name, and what is his sonnes name, if thou canst tell?
text.NABRE: 30:4 Who has gone up to heaven and come down again— who has cupped the wind in the hollow of the hand? Who has bound up the waters in a cloak— who has established all the ends of the earth? What is that person’s name, or the name of his son?”

text.NRSV: Every word of God proves true; he is a shield to those who take refuge in him.
text.Rotherham: Every saying of God is refined, A shield, is, he, to them who seek refuge in him.
text.KJV: 30:5 Euery word of God is pure: he is a shield vnto them that put their trust in him.
text.NABRE: 30:5 Every word of God is tested; he is a shield to those who take refuge in him.

text.NRSV: Do not add to his words, or he will rebuke you, and you will be found a liar.
text.Rotherham: Do not add unto his words, lest he convict thee, and thou be found false.
text.KJV: 30:6 Adde thou not vnto his words, lest he reproue thee, and thou be found a lyar.
text.NABRE: 30:6 Add nothing to his words, lest he reprimand you, and you be proved a liar.

text.NRSV: Two things I ask of you; do not deny them to me before I die:
text.Rotherham: Two things, have I asked of thee, withhold them not from me, ere yet I die:
text.KJV: 30:7 Two things haue I required of thee, deny me them not before I die.
text.NABRE: 30:7 Two things I ask of you, do not deny them to me before I die:

text.NRSV: Remove far from me falsehood and lying; give me neither poverty nor riches; feed me with the food that I need,
text.Rotherham: Vanity and falsehood, remove far from me, Neither poverty nor riches, give me, Feed me with the food appointed me:
text.KJV: 30:8 Remoue farre from mee vanity, and lyes; giue me neither pouerty, nor riches, feede me with food conuenient for me.
text.NABRE: 30:8 Put falsehood and lying far from me, give me neither poverty nor riches; provide me only with the food I need;

text.NRSV: or I shall be full, and deny you, and say, “Who is the Lord?” or I shall be poor, and steal, and profane the name of my God.
text.Rotherham: Lest I be full, and deny, and say—Who is Yahweh? or lest I be impoverished and steal, and do violence to the Name of my God.
text.KJV: 30:9 Lest I be full, and deny thee, and say, Who is the Lord? or lest I be poore, and steale, and take the name of my God in vaine.
text.NABRE: 30:9 Lest, being full, I deny you, saying, “Who is the LORD?” Or, being in want, I steal, and profane the name of my God.

text.NRSV: Do not slander a servant to a master, or the servant will curse you, and you will be held guilty.
text.Rotherham: Do not slander a servant to his master, lest he revile thee, and thou be found guilty.
text.KJV: 30:10 Accuse not a seruant vnto his master; lest he curse thee, and thou be found guilty.
text.NABRE: 30:10 Do not criticize servants to their master, lest they curse you, and you have to pay the penalty.

text.NRSV: There are those who curse their fathers and do not bless their mothers.
text.Rotherham: A generation! Its father, it revileth, and, its mother, it doth not bless.
text.KJV: 30:11 There is a generation that curseth their father, and doth not blesse their mother.
text.NABRE: 30:11 There are some who curse their fathers, and do not bless their mothers.

text.NRSV: There are those who are pure in their own eyes yet are not cleansed of their filthiness.
text.Rotherham: A generation! Pure in its own eyes, yet, from its filth, hath it not been bathed,
text.KJV: 30:12 There is a generation that are pure in their owne eyes, and yet is not washed from their filthinesse.
text.NABRE: 30:12 There are some pure in their own eyes, yet not cleansed of their filth.

text.NRSV: There are those—how lofty are their eyes, how high their eyelids lift!
text.Rotherham: A generation! How lofty are its eyes, and its eyelashes uplifted.
text.KJV: 30:13 There is a generation, O howe lofty are their eyes! and their eye-lids are lifted vp.
text.NABRE: 30:13 There are some—how haughty their eyes! how overbearing their glance!

text.NRSV: There are those whose teeth are swords, whose teeth are knives, to devour the poor from off the earth, the needy from among mortals.
text.Rotherham: A generation! Swords, are its teeth, and, knives, its incisors,—to devour the humbled out of the earth, and the needy, from among men.
text.KJV: 30:14 There is a generation, whose teeth are as swords, and their iaw-teeth as kniues, to deuoure the poore from off the earth, and the needy from among men.
text.NABRE: 30:14 There are some—their teeth are swords, their teeth are knives, Devouring the needy from the earth, and the poor from the human race.

text.NRSV: The leech has two daughters; “Give, give,” they cry. Three things are never satisfied; four never say, “Enough”:
text.Rotherham: The vampire, hath two daughters, Give! Give! Three, there are will not be satisfied, four, have not said, Enough!
text.KJV: 30:15 The horse-leach hath two daughters, crying, Giue, giue. There are three things that are neuer satisfied, yea foure things say not, It is enough:
text.NABRE: 30:15 The leech has two daughters: “Give,” and “Give.” Three things never get their fill, four never say, “Enough!”

text.NRSV: Sheol, the barren womb, the earth ever thirsty for water, and the fire that never says, “Enough.”
text.Rotherham: Hades, and barrenness,—A land not satisfied with water, and fire, that saith not, Enough!
text.KJV: 30:16 The graue; and the barren wombe; the earth that is not filled with water; and the fire that saith not, It is enough.
text.NABRE: 30:16 Sheol, a barren womb, land that never gets its fill of water, and fire, which never says, “Enough!”

text.NRSV: The eye that mocks a father and scorns to obey a mother will be pecked out by the ravens of the valley and eaten by the vultures.
text.Rotherham: The eye that mocketh a father, and despiseth to obey a mother, the ravens of the valley shall pick it out, and the young of the eagle shall eat it.
text.KJV: 30:17 The eye that mocketh at his father, and despiseth to obey his mother; the rauens of the valley shall picke it out, and the young Eagles shall eate it.
text.NABRE: 30:17 The eye that mocks a father, or scorns the homage due a mother, Will be plucked out by brook ravens; devoured by a brood of vultures.

text.NRSV: Three things are too wonderful for me; four I do not understand:
text.Rotherham: Three, things there are, too difficult for me, yea, four, which I do not understand:
text.KJV: 30:18 There be three things which are too wonderfull for me; yea foure, which I know not:
text.NABRE: 30:18 Three things are too wonderful for me, yes, four I cannot understand:

text.NRSV: the way of an eagle in the sky, the way of a serpent on a rock, the way of a ship on the high seas, and the way of a man with a girl.
text.Rotherham: The way of an eagle in the heavens, the way of a serpent on the rock,—the way of a ship in the heart of the sea, and the way of a man with a maid.
text.KJV: 30:19 The way of an Eagle in the ayre; the way of a serpent vpon a rocke; the the way of a ship in the midst of the sea; and the way of a man with a maid.
text.NABRE: 30:19 The way of an eagle in the sky, the way of a serpent upon a rock, The way of a ship on the high seas, and the way of a man with a woman.

text.NRSV: This is the way of an adulteress: she eats, and wipes her mouth, and says, “I have done no wrong.”
text.Rotherham: So, is the way of a woman committing adultery,—she eateth, and wipeth her mouth, and saith, I have done no iniquity!
text.KJV: 30:20 Such is the way of an adulterous woman: she eateth, and wipeth her mouth, and saith, I haue done no wickednesse.
text.NABRE: 30:20 This is the way of an adulterous woman: she eats, wipes her mouth, and says, “I have done no wrong.”

text.NRSV: Under three things the earth trembles; under four it cannot bear up:
text.Rotherham: Under three things, a land is stirred, yea, under four, she cannot bear up:
text.KJV: 30:21 For three things the earth is disquieted, and for foure which it cannot beare:
text.NABRE: 30:21 Under three things the earth trembles, yes, under four it cannot bear up:

text.NRSV: a slave when he becomes king, and a fool when glutted with food;
text.Rotherham: Under a servant, when he reigneth, and a base man, when he is surfeited with food;
text.KJV: 30:22 For a seruant when he reigneth, and a foole when hee is filled with meate:
text.NABRE: 30:22 Under a slave who becomes king, and a fool who is glutted with food;

text.NRSV: an unloved woman when she gets a husband, and a maid when she succeeds her mistress.
text.Rotherham: Under a hateful woman, when she is married, and a handmaid when she driveth out her mistress.
text.KJV: 30:23 For an odious woman when shee is married, and an handmayd that is heire to her mistresse.
text.NABRE: 30:23 Under an unloved woman who is wed, and a maidservant who displaces her mistress.

text.NRSV: Four things on earth are small, yet they are exceedingly wise:
text.Rotherham: Four, things there are, the small of the earth,—yet, they, are wiser than the wise:
text.KJV: 30:24 There be foure things which are little vpon the earth; but they are exceeding wise:
text.NABRE: 30:24 Four things are among the smallest on the earth, and yet are exceedingly wise:

text.NRSV: the ants are a people without strength, yet they provide their food in the summer;
text.Rotherham: The ants, a people, not strong,—yet prepare they, in summer, their food;
text.KJV: 30:25 The Ants are a people not strong, yet they prepare their meate in the summer.
text.NABRE: 30:25 Ants—a species not strong, yet they store up their food in the summer;

text.NRSV: the badgers are a people without power, yet they make their homes in the rocks;
text.Rotherham: The conies, a people of, no power, yet set they, among the crags, their house;
text.KJV: 30:26 The conies are but a feeble folke, yet make they their houses in the rocks
text.NABRE: 30:26 Badgers—a species not mighty, yet they make their home in the crags;

text.NRSV: the locusts have no king, yet all of them march in rank;
text.Rotherham: King, is there none, to, the locusts,—yet go forth in swarms, do they all;
text.KJV: 30:27 The locustes haue no king, yet goe they forth all of them by bands.
text.NABRE: 30:27 Locusts—they have no king, yet they march forth in formation;

text.NRSV: the lizard can be grasped in the hand, yet it is found in kings’ palaces.
text.Rotherham: The lizard, with hands, reneweth its hold,—yet, the same, is in the palaces of the king.
text.KJV: 30:28 The spider taketh hold with her hands, and is in kings palaces.
text.NABRE: 30:28 Lizards—you can catch them with your hands, yet they find their way into kings’ palaces.

text.NRSV: Three things are stately in their stride; four are stately in their gait:
text.Rotherham: Three, things there are which step along well, ye, four, which excel in going:
text.KJV: 30:29 There be three things which goe well, yea foure are comely in going:
text.NABRE: 30:29 Three things are stately in their stride, yes, four are stately in their carriage:

text.NRSV: the lion, which is mightiest among wild animals and does not turn back before any;
text.Rotherham: The lion, hero among beasts, which turneth aside from the face of no one;
text.KJV: 30:30 A lyon which is strongest among beastes, and turneth not away for any:
text.NABRE: 30:30 The lion, mightiest of beasts, retreats before nothing;

text.NRSV: the strutting cock, the he-goat, and a king striding before his people.
text.Rotherham: The greyhound, or the he-goat,—and a king, having a band of soldiers with him.
text.KJV: 30:31 A gray-hound; an hee-goate also; and a king, against whom there is no rising vp.
text.NABRE: 30:31 The strutting cock, and the he-goat, and the king at the head of his people.

text.NRSV: If you have been foolish, exalting yourself, or if you have been devising evil, put your hand on your mouth.
text.Rotherham: If thou hast acted basely by lifting thyself up,—if thou hast plotted evil, [put thy] hand to [thy] mouth!
text.KJV: 30:32 If thou hast done foolishly in lifting vp thy selfe, or if thou hast thought euill, lay thine hand vpon thy mouth.
text.NABRE: 30:32 If you have foolishly been proud or presumptuous—put your hand on your mouth;

text.NRSV: For as churning milk produces butter, and twisting the nose produces blood, so forcing anger produces strife.
text.Rotherham: Surely, the pressing of milk, bringeth forth curd, and, the pressing of the nose, bringeth forth blood, and, the pressing of wrath, bringeth forth strife.
text.KJV: 30:33 Surely the churning of milke bringeth forth butter; and the wringing of the nose bringeth forth blood: so the forcing of wrath bringeth forth strife.
text.NABRE: 30:33 For as the churning of milk produces curds, and the pressing of the nose produces blood, the churning of anger produces strife.

text.NRSV: The words of King Lemuel. An oracle that his mother taught him:
text.Rotherham: The words of Lemuel the king,—the strain which was taught him by his mother:—
text.KJV: 31:1 The wordes of King Lemuel, the prophecie that his mother taught him.
text.NABRE: 31:1 VIII. Sayings of King Lemuel Chapter 31 - The words of Lemuel, king of Massa, the instruction his mother taught him:

text.NRSV: No, my son! No, son of my womb! No, son of my vows!
text.Rotherham: What, my son? and what, the son of my womb? aye what, the son of my vows?
text.KJV: 31:2 What, my sonne! and what, the sonne of my wombe! and what, the sonne of my vowes!
text.NABRE: 31:2 What are you doing, my son! what are you doing, son of my womb; what are you doing, son of my vows!

text.NRSV: Do not give your strength to women, your ways to those who destroy kings.
text.Rotherham: Do not give, to women, thy strength, nor thy ways, to them who ruin kings.
text.KJV: 31:3 Giue not thy strength vnto women, nor thy wayes to that which destroyeth kings.
text.NABRE: 31:3 Do not give your vigor to women, or your strength to those who ruin kings.

text.NRSV: It is not for kings, O Lemuel, it is not for kings to drink wine, or for rulers to desire strong drink;
text.Rotherham: It is not for kings, O Lemuel, it is not for kings to drink wine, nor, for dignitaries, to desire strong drink,
text.KJV: 31:4 It is not for kings, O Lemuel, it is not for kings to drinke wine, nor for Princes, strong drinke:
text.NABRE: 31:4 It is not for kings, Lemuel, not for kings to drink wine; strong drink is not for princes,

text.NRSV: or else they will drink and forget what has been decreed, and will pervert the rights of all the afflicted.
text.Rotherham: Lest he drink, and forget that which is decreed, and alter the plea of any who are sorely oppressed.
text.KJV: 31:5 Lest they drinke, and forget the Law, and peruert the iudgement of any of the afflicted.
text.NABRE: 31:5 Lest in drinking they forget what has been decreed, and violate the rights of any who are in need.

text.NRSV: Give strong drink to one who is perishing, and wine to those in bitter distress;
text.Rotherham: Give strong drink, to him that is perishing, and wine, to such as are embittered in soul:
text.KJV: 31:6 Giue strong drinke vnto him that is ready to perish, and wine vnto those that be of heauie hearts.
text.NABRE: 31:6 Give strong drink to anyone who is perishing, and wine to the embittered;

text.NRSV: let them drink and forget their poverty, and remember their misery no more.
text.Rotherham: Let him drink, and forget his poverty, and, his wearying toil, let him remember no more.
text.KJV: 31:7 Let him drinke, and forget his pouertie, and remember his misery no more.
text.NABRE: 31:7 When they drink, they will forget their misery, and think no more of their troubles.

text.NRSV: Speak out for those who cannot speak, for the rights of all the destitute.
text.Rotherham: Open thy mouth for the dumb, for the cause of all the children of the departed.
text.KJV: 31:8 Open thy mouth for the dumbe in the cause of all such as are appointed to destruction.
text.NABRE: 31:8 Open your mouth in behalf of the mute, and for the rights of the destitute;

text.NRSV: Speak out, judge righteously, defend the rights of the poor and needy.
text.Rotherham: Open thy mouth—judge righteously,—and administer justice for the poor and the needy.
text.KJV: 31:9 Open thy mouth, iudge righteously, and plead the cause of the poore and needy.
text.NABRE: 31:9 Open your mouth, judge justly, defend the needy and the poor!

text.NRSV: A capable wife who can find? She is far more precious than jewels.
text.Rotherham: A virtuous woman, who can find? for, far beyond corals, is her worth.
text.KJV: 31:10 ¶ Who can finde a vertuous woman? for her price is farre aboue Rubies.
text.NABRE: 31:10 IX. Poem on the Woman of Worth - Who can find a woman of worth? Far beyond jewels is her value.

text.NRSV: The heart of her husband trusts in her, and he will have no lack of gain.
text.Rotherham: The heart of her husband, trusteth her, and, gain, he shall not lack:
text.KJV: 31:11 The heart of her husband doeth safely trust in her, so that he shall haue no need of spoile.
text.NABRE: 31:11 Her husband trusts her judgment; he does not lack income.

text.NRSV: She does him good, and not harm, all the days of her life.
text.Rotherham: She doeth him good and not evil, all the days of her life:
text.KJV: 31:12 She will doe him good, and not euill, all the dayes of her life.
text.NABRE: 31:12 She brings him profit, not loss, all the days of her life.

text.NRSV: She seeks wool and flax, and works with willing hands.
text.Rotherham: She seeketh wool and flax, and worketh willingly with her hands:
text.KJV: 31:13 She seeketh wooll and flaxe, and worketh willingly with her hands.
text.NABRE: 31:13 She seeks out wool and flax and weaves with skillful hands.

text.NRSV: She is like the ships of the merchant, she brings her food from far away.
text.Rotherham: She is like the ships of the merchant, from afar, she bringeth in her food;
text.KJV: 31:14 She is like the merchants ships, she bringeth her food from afarre.
text.NABRE: 31:14 Like a merchant fleet, she secures her provisions from afar.

text.NRSV: She rises while it is still night and provides food for her household and tasks for her servant-girls.
text.Rotherham: And she riseth, while yet it is night, and giveth food to her household, and a task to her maidens:
text.KJV: 31:15 Shee riseth also while it is yet night, and giueth meate to her household, and a portion to her maydens.
text.NABRE: 31:15 She rises while it is still night, and distributes food to her household, a portion to her maidservants.

text.NRSV: She considers a field and buys it; with the fruit of her hands she plants a vineyard.
text.Rotherham: She considereth a field, and procureth it, Out of the fruit of her hands, she planteth a vineyard:
text.KJV: 31:16 She considereth a field, and buyeth it: with the fruit of her handes she planteth a Uineyard.
text.NABRE: 31:16 She picks out a field and acquires it; from her earnings she plants a vineyard.

text.NRSV: She girds herself with strength, and makes her arms strong.
text.Rotherham: She girdeth, with strength her loins, and putteth vigour into her arms:
text.KJV: 31:17 She girdeth her loynes with strength, and strengtheneth her armes.
text.NABRE: 31:17 She girds herself with strength; she exerts her arms with vigor.

text.NRSV: She perceives that her merchandise is profitable. Her lamp does not go out at night.
text.Rotherham: She tasteth, whether, good, be her merchandise, and her lamp, goeth not out by night:
text.KJV: 31:18 She perceiueth that her merchandise is good; her candle goeth not out by night.
text.NABRE: 31:18 She enjoys the profit from her dealings; her lamp is never extinguished at night.

text.NRSV: She puts her hands to the distaff, and her hands hold the spindle.
text.Rotherham: Her hands, she putteth forth to the distaff, and, her palms, lay hold of the spindle:
text.KJV: 31:19 She layeth her handes to the spindle, and her handes hold the distaffe.
text.NABRE: 31:19 She puts her hands to the distaff, and her fingers ply the spindle.

text.NRSV: She opens her hand to the poor, and reaches out her hands to the needy.
text.Rotherham: Her palm, she spreadeth out to the oppressed, and, her hands, she extendeth to the needy:
text.KJV: 31:20 She stretcheth out her hand to the poore, yea she reacheth foorth her handes to the needy.
text.NABRE: 31:20 She reaches out her hands to the poor, and extends her arms to the needy.

text.NRSV: She is not afraid for her household when it snows, for all her household are clothed in crimson.
text.Rotherham: She feareth not, for her household, because of the snow, for, all her household, are clothed with crimson:
text.KJV: 31:21 She is not afraid of the snow for her houshold: for all her houshold are cloathed with scarlet.
text.NABRE: 31:21 She is not concerned for her household when it snows— all her charges are doubly clothed.

text.NRSV: She makes herself coverings; her clothing is fine linen and purple.
text.Rotherham: Coverlets, she maketh for herself, Of white linen and of purple, is her clothing:
text.KJV: 31:22 She maketh herselfe couerings of tapestrie; her cloathing is silke and purple.
text.NABRE: 31:22 She makes her own coverlets; fine linen and purple are her clothing.

text.NRSV: Her husband is known in the city gates, taking his seat among the elders of the land.
text.Rotherham: Known in the gates, is her husband, when he sitteth, with the elders of the land:
text.KJV: 31:23 Her husband is knowen in the gates, when he sitteth among the Elders of the land.
text.NABRE: 31:23 Her husband is prominent at the city gates as he sits with the elders of the land.

text.NRSV: She makes linen garments and sells them; she supplies the merchant with sashes.
text.Rotherham: Fine linen wraps, she maketh and selleth, and, girdles, doth she deliver to the trader:
text.KJV: 31:24 She maketh fine linnen, and selleth it, and deliuereth girdles vnto the merchant.
text.NABRE: 31:24 She makes garments and sells them, and stocks the merchants with belts.

text.NRSV: Strength and dignity are her clothing, and she laughs at the time to come.
text.Rotherham: Strength and dignity, are her clothing, and she laugheth at the time to come:
text.KJV: 31:25 Strength and honour are her cloathing; and she shall reioyce in time to come.
text.NABRE: 31:25 She is clothed with strength and dignity, and laughs at the days to come.

text.NRSV: She opens her mouth with wisdom, and the teaching of kindness is on her tongue.
text.Rotherham: Her mouth, she openeth with wisdom, and, the instruction of kindness, is on her tongue:
text.KJV: 31:26 She openeth her mouth with wisedome; and in her tongue is the law of kindnesse.
text.NABRE: 31:26 She opens her mouth in wisdom; kindly instruction is on her tongue.

text.NRSV: She looks well to the ways of her household, and does not eat the bread of idleness.
text.Rotherham: She looketh well to the goings of her household, and, the bread of idleness, will she not eat.
text.KJV: 31:27 She looketh well to the wayes of her housholde, and eateth not the bread of idlenesse.
text.NABRE: 31:27 She watches over the affairs of her household, and does not eat the bread of idleness.

text.NRSV: Her children rise up and call her happy; her husband also, and he praises her:
text.Rotherham: Her children rise up, and call her happy! her husband, and he praiseth her:—
text.KJV: 31:28 Her children arise vp, and call her blessed; her husband also, and he praiseth her.
text.NABRE: 31:28 Her children rise up and call her blessed; her husband, too, praises her:

text.NRSV: “Many women have done excellently, but you surpass them all.”
text.Rotherham: Many daughters, have done virtuously, but, thou, excellest them all!
text.KJV: 31:29 Many daughters haue done vertuously, but thou excellest them all.
text.NABRE: 31:29 “Many are the women of proven worth, but you have excelled them all.”

text.NRSV: Charm is deceitful, and beauty is vain, but a woman who fears the Lord is to be praised.
text.Rotherham: Deceitful is loveliness and vain is beauty, The woman that revereth Yahweh, she, shall be praised:
text.KJV: 31:30 Fauour is deceitfull, and beautie is vaine: but a woman that feareth the Lord, she shalbe praised.
text.NABRE: 31:30 Charm is deceptive and beauty fleeting; the woman who fears the LORD is to be praised.

text.NRSV: Give her a share in the fruit of her hands, and let her works praise her in the city gates.
text.Rotherham: Give her of the fruit of her own hands, and let her own works, praise her in the gates.
text.KJV: 31:31 Giue her of the fruit of her hands, and let her owne workes praise her in the gates.
text.NABRE: 31:31 Acclaim her for the work of her hands, and let her deeds praise her at the city gates.
